This variable takes three main values: (1) tax farming (a market form), in which independent entrepreneurs purchase the right to collect a particular tax in a given area by paying the ruler a fixed sum determined by auction, retaining any surplus collected above that price as profit (p. 284–285); (2) state administration (a hierarchical form), in which rulers employ permanent, salaried state officials to collect taxes under direct monitoring and sanctioning (p. 285); and (3) intermediate or proportional payment forms, in which agents receive a proportion of the revenue they collect, thereby splitting residual claimancy between principal and agent (p. 290). Kiser examines variation in this dependent variable along three dimensions: across different types of taxes (direct versus indirect), across different countries (England /ˈɪŋɡlənd/, France /fræns/, Spain /speɪn/, Prussia /ˈprʌʃə/, Sweden /ˈswiːdən/, China /ˈtʃaɪnə/, Russia /ˈrʌʃə/, the Ottoman Empire /ˈɒtəmən ˈɛmpaɪər/, and the Netherlands /ˈnɛðərləndz/), and over time within the early modern period (roughly the 13th through 18th centuries) (p. 285, 295).
zh: 主要因变量是早期现代国家中的税收征集制度结构——具体来说，是统治者与税收征集者之间代理关系的形式。该变量有三种主要取值：（1）包税制（市场形式），即独立企业家通过拍卖方式向统治者支付一笔固定金额，购买在特定区域征收特定税种的权利，征收金额超出购买价格的部分作为利润归包税人所有（第284–285页）；（2）国家行政征收（科层形式），即统治者雇佣固定薪资的永久官员来征收税款，并对其进行直接监督和惩罚（第285页）；（3）中间形式或比例支付形式，即代理人获得其征收收入的一定比例作为报酬，从而在委托人和代理人之间分割剩余索取权（第290页）。凯泽沿三个维度考察该因变量的变异：不同税种之间（直接税与间接税）、不同国家之间（英格兰 /ˈɪŋɡlənd/、法兰西 /fræns/、西班牙 /speɪn/、普鲁士 /ˈprʌʃə/、瑞典 /ˈswiːdən/、中国 /ˈtʃaɪnə/、俄罗斯 /ˈrʌʃə/、奥斯曼帝国 /ˈɒtəmən ˈɛmpaɪər/ 和尼德兰 /ˈnɛðərləndz/），以及早期现代时期（大约13至18世纪）的时间变化（第285、295页）。

en: The main independent variable is the control capacity of rulers — that is, the structural ability of rulers (as principals) to monitor and sanction their tax-collecting agents. This control capacity is itself shaped by several structural factors: (1) the level of development of technologies of control, including communications, transportation, and record-keeping systems, which determine how effectively rulers can oversee agents operating at a distance (p. 290, 293–294); (2) the size of the country, since larger territories and geographical obstacles reduce rulers’ ability to monitor agents (p. 290); (3) the nature of the tax being collected — whether the taxable asset is mobile, variable over time, or difficult to measure — which determines how hard it is for rulers to detect corruption and evasion by agents and taxpayers (p. 291–292); and (4) the autonomy of the ruler, meaning the extent to which rulers are constrained by political institutions such as parliaments, which affects both the transaction costs of creating tax farming contracts and the type of corruption that is most damaging (p. 291). When control capacities are low, rulers will prefer market forms (tax farming); when they are high, rulers will prefer hierarchical forms (state administration) (p. 290).
zh: 主要自变量是统治者的控制能力——即统治者作为委托人监督和惩罚其税收征集代理人的结构性能力。该控制能力本身受到若干结构性因素的塑造：（1）控制技术的发展水平，包括通讯、交通运输和档案记录体系，它们决定了统治者能多有效地监控在远方运作的代理人（第290、293–294页）；（2）国家的规模，因为更大的领土和地理障碍降低了统治者监控代理人的能力（第290页）；（3）所征税种的性质——应税资产是否具有流动性、是否随时间波动、是否难以度量——这决定了统治者发现代理人和纳税人腐败与逃税行为的难度（第291–292页）；（4）统治者的自主性，即统治者在多大程度上受到议会等政治制度的约束，这既影响创建包税合同的交易成本，也影响何种类型的腐败最具危害性（第291页）。当控制能力低时，统治者倾向于选择市场形式（包税制）；当控制能力高时，统治者倾向于选择科层形式（国家行政征收）（第290页）。

en: Kiser conceptualizes the state primarily as a revenue-extracting organization headed by a ruler who seeks to maximize net tax revenue (defined as total tax collected minus the costs of collection, including corruption) in order to maintain power and maximize personal wealth (p. 285, 290). State building in this framework is understood as the gradual transition from market-based (tax farming) to hierarchical (bureaucratic state administration) forms of tax collection — a transition driven by the development of control technologies that make direct bureaucratic management increasingly efficient relative to contracting with independent entrepreneurs (p. 293–294). The state is not measured through a single index but rather through the observable organizational form of its fiscal apparatus: the proportion of total revenue collected via tax farms versus state officials, the degree of consolidation of tax farms, and the level of bureaucratic development in tax collection (salaries, monitoring procedures, merit-based recruitment) (p. 295, 298, 303). Kiser does not adopt a Weberian ideal-type of the state as a starting point, but rather treats the actual, imperfect hierarchical structures available to rulers as the relevant comparison to market alternatives. The key insight is that state building in the fiscal domain was not a linear march toward bureaucratization; instead, rulers frequently switched back and forth between tax farming and state administration as structural conditions changed (p. 298).
zh: 凯泽主要将国家概念化为一个以统治者为首的资源汲取组织，统治者寻求最大化净税收收入（定义为税收总额减去征收成本，包括腐败损失），以维持权力和最大化个人财富（第285、290页）。在这一框架中，国家建设被理解为税收征集形式从市场化（包税制）向科层化（官僚国家行政征收）的逐步转型——这一转型是由控制技术的发展所驱动的，这些技术使直接的官僚管理相对于与独立企业家签约变得越来越高效（第293–294页）。国家不是通过单一指标来测量的，而是通过其财政机构的可观察组织形式来衡量的：包税收入占总收入的比例、包税场的整合程度，以及税收征集中的官僚化发展水平（薪资、监督程序、基于绩效的招聘等）（第295、298、303页）。凯泽没有采用韦伯式的国家理想类型作为起点，而是将统治者实际可用的、不完美的科层结构作为与市场替代方案进行比较的参照。其关键洞见是，财政领域的国家建设并非线性地走向官僚化；相反，随着结构条件的变化，统治者频繁地在包税制和国家行政征收之间来回切换（第298页）。

en: Kiser’s core argument is that the structure of early modern tax systems was determined by efficiency considerations rooted in the control capacities of rulers. Rulers were rational wealth-maximizers who attempted to choose the form of tax collection — tax farming, state administration, or an intermediate proportional-payment form — that would produce the highest net revenue (p. 285). The choice between these forms was fundamentally a principal-agent problem: since tax collectors (agents) had interests divergent from those of rulers (principals), rulers had to find ways to control agent behavior (p. 290). When rulers could effectively monitor and sanction their agents — that is, when control capacities were high — they chose state administration (hierarchy), because paying agents fixed salaries and directly overseeing their work minimized the share of revenue lost to agents’ profits, corruption, or shirking. When rulers could not effectively control their agents — that is, when control capacities were low — they chose tax farming (market relations), because making the agent the residual claimant gave the agent a strong self-interest in maximizing tax collection, thereby reducing the need for direct monitoring (p. 290). This argument challenges the prevailing scholarly consensus that tax farming was inherently inefficient and was used only because rulers needed loans or wanted predictable revenue. Kiser contends instead that tax farming was often the most efficient system available given the structural constraints rulers faced, and that tax farming organizations actually employed better monitoring and control techniques than the state bureaucracies of the same period (p. 294, 302–303).
zh: 凯泽的核心论点是，早期现代税收体系的结构是由根植于统治者控制能力的效率考量所决定的。统治者是理性的财富最大化者，他们试图选择能产生最高净收入的税收征集形式——包税制、国家行政征收或中间性的比例支付形式（第285页）。这些形式之间的选择本质上是一个委托-代理问题：由于税收征集者（代理人）的利益与统治者（委托人）的利益存在分歧，统治者必须找到控制代理人行为的方式（第290页）。当统治者能有效监督和惩罚其代理人——即控制能力高时——他们选择国家行政征收（科层制），因为向代理人支付固定薪资并直接监督其工作，可以最小化因代理人利润、腐败或偷懒而造成的收入损失。当统治者不能有效控制其代理人——即控制能力低时——他们选择包税制（市场关系），因为让代理人成为剩余索取者，使其产生了最大化税收征集的强烈自利动机，从而减少了对直接监督的需求（第290页）。这一论点挑战了学界流行的共识，即包税制天然低效、仅因统治者需要贷款或希望获得可预测收入才被采用。凯泽则认为，鉴于统治者面临的结构性约束，包税制往往是可用的最高效体系，而且包税组织实际上采用了比同时期国家官僚机构更好的监控和控制技术（第294、302–303页）。

en: Kiser proposes several interconnected mechanisms through which control capacity shapes the choice of tax collection system:

(1) The incentive-alignment mechanism. In state administration, agents on fixed salaries have weak incentives to maximize revenue collection; their income does not depend on how much they collect. This creates classic shirking and corruption problems: officials may accept bribes from taxpayers in exchange for underassessing taxable assets, or they may embezzle collected funds. Since the principal (ruler) bears the residual risk, the ruler must invest heavily in monitoring. In tax farming, by contrast, the agent is the residual claimant — everything collected above the fixed purchase price is the farmer’s profit. This creates a powerful self-interest in maximizing collection and, critically, in controlling corruption among the farmer’s own subordinate agents, because corruption directly reduces the farmer’s income (p. 290, 294).

(2) The monitoring-difficulty mechanism. The nature of the taxable asset determines how difficult it is for rulers to monitor both their agents and taxpayers. When taxable assets are mobile (e.g., traded commodities subject to customs duties), variable over time (e.g., fluctuating trade volumes), and difficult to measure (e.g., the value of goods in transit), it is extremely hard for rulers to determine whether low revenue reflects honest collection of a genuinely low tax base, agent shirking, agent corruption (taking bribes to underassess), or taxpayer evasion. Under these conditions of high information asymmetry, making the agent the residual claimant through tax farming is the most efficient solution, because it shifts the measurement problem to the agent who has the strongest incentive to solve it. Conversely, when assets are immobile, stable, and easily measured (e.g., land), state administration becomes feasible because rulers can verify agent performance through relatively simple cross-checks such as repeating land surveys (p. 291–293).

(3) The corruption-type mechanism. The form of the agency relation determines not just the total level of corruption but the type of corruption that predominates. Tax farmers with short-term contracts tend toward overtaxation (using surcharges to extract maximum revenue before the contract expires), which harms the tax base in the long run. Salaried state officials tend toward embezzlement and bribery (taking bribes for underassessment), which reduces revenue flowing to the ruler but may be less damaging to the tax base. Since the type of corruption associated with tax farming (overtaxation) hurts taxpayers more directly and visibly than the type associated with state administration, tax farming should be more common when rulers have high autonomy and are less constrained by taxpayer resistance (p. 291).

(4) The technology-of-control mechanism. Improvements in communications, transportation, and record-keeping directly increase rulers’ ability to monitor distant agents, thereby increasing the relative efficiency of state administration. In the early modern period, basic transportation and communication technologies remained quite primitive until the late 18th century (for example, in the 16th century, it took three weeks to travel from Paris /pəˈriː/ to Lyon /liˈɒ̃/, and even in 1765 it took fourteen days from Paris to Toulouse /tuːˈluːz/), which meant that rulers’ control capacities remained structurally low for most of the period (p. 298). The gradual improvement of these technologies over time explains the long-run shift from tax farming to state administration.

(5) The consolidation-and-learning mechanism. Over time, tax farms themselves consolidated into larger organizations, and in the process developed sophisticated administrative techniques — improved accounting procedures, rigorous inspections and audits, merit-based recruitment, higher salaries for agents — that were eventually adopted by state administrations when they took over tax collection. Thus, ironically, tax farming paved the way for its own replacement by creating the bureaucratic innovations that made state administration efficient enough to collect indirect taxes (p. 299–300, 303).
zh: 凯泽提出了若干相互关联的机制，解释控制能力如何塑造税收征集制度的选择：

（1）激励对齐机制。 在国家行政征收中，领取固定薪资的代理人缺乏最大化税收征收的激励；其收入不取决于征收多少。这产生了经典的偷懒和腐败问题：官员可能接受纳税人贿赂以换取低估应税资产，或挪用已征收的资金。由于委托人（统治者）承担剩余风险，统治者必须在监督上投入大量资源。相比之下，在包税制中，代理人是剩余索取者——超出固定购买价格的所有征收额都是包税人的利润。这创造了最大化征收的强大自利动机，而且关键的是，也激励包税人控制其下属代理人的腐败，因为腐败直接减少包税人的收入（第290、294页）。

（2）监督困难机制。 应税资产的性质决定了统治者监督其代理人和纳税人的难度。当应税资产具有流动性（如受关税约束的贸易商品）、随时间变动（如波动的贸易量）、且难以度量（如运输中货物的价值）时，统治者极难判断低收入究竟反映的是诚实征收下真正低的税基、代理人偷懒、代理人腐败（受贿低估）还是纳税人逃税。在这种高度信息不对称的条件下，通过包税制使代理人成为剩余索取者是最有效的解决方案，因为它将度量问题转移给了最有激励去解决它的代理人。反之，当资产不可移动、稳定且容易度量（如土地）时，国家行政征收变得可行，因为统治者可以通过相对简单的交叉核查（如重复土地调查）来验证代理人的表现（第291–293页）。

（3）腐败类型机制。 代理关系的形式不仅决定腐败的总体水平，还决定占主导地位的腐败类型。持有短期合同的包税人倾向于过度征税（使用附加费在合同到期前榨取最大收入），这从长远来看损害税基。领取固定薪资的国家官员则倾向于挪用公款和受贿（为低估资产而收受贿赂），这减少了流向统治者的收入，但对税基的损害可能较小。由于与包税制相关的腐败类型（过度征税）比与国家行政征收相关的腐败类型更直接、更明显地伤害纳税人，因此包税制应在统治者自主性高、较少受纳税人抵抗约束时更为普遍（第291页）。

（4）控制技术机制。 通讯、交通和档案记录的改进直接提高了统治者监控远方代理人的能力，从而提高了国家行政征收的相对效率。在早期现代时期，基本的交通和通讯技术直到18世纪晚期仍然相当原始（例如，16世纪从巴黎 /pəˈriː/ 到里昂 /liˈɒ̃/ 需要三周，甚至到1765年从巴黎到图卢兹 /tuːˈluːz/ 仍需十四天），这意味着统治者的控制能力在该时期的大部分时间内结构性地保持在低水平（第298页）。这些技术的逐渐改进解释了从包税制到国家行政征收的长期转变。
（5）整合与学习机制。 随着时间推移，包税场本身整合为更大的组织，并在此过程中发展出精密的行政技术——改进的会计程序、严格的检查和审计、基于绩效的招聘、更高的代理人薪资——这些技术最终在国家行政部门接管税收征集时被采用。因此，颇具讽刺意味的是，包税制通过创造使国家行政征收足以高效征收间接税的官僚创新，为自身的被取代铺平了道路（第299–300、303页）。

en: Kiser uses qualitative, comparative-historical data drawn from secondary historical sources covering the fiscal systems of multiple early modern states. The geographic coverage includes England /ˈɪŋɡlənd/, France /fræns/, Spain /speɪn/, Prussia /ˈprʌʃə/, Sweden /ˈswiːdən/, China /ˈtʃaɪnə/, Russia /ˈrʌʃə/, the Ottoman Empire /ˈɒtəmən ˈɛmpaɪər/, and the Netherlands /ˈnɛðərləndz/ (p. 295). The time period spans roughly from the 13th century to the late 18th century, with the most detailed coverage of England (1275–1688) and France (13th century to the Revolution of 1789) (p. 295–299). The unit of analysis varies: it is sometimes a country-period (e.g., Elizabethan /ɪˌlɪzəˈbiːθən/ England), sometimes a specific tax (e.g., the English customs, the French gabelle /ɡəˈbɛl/), and sometimes a specific tax farming organization (e.g., the French General Farms). The dependent variable — the form of tax collection — is measured qualitatively as whether a given tax in a given country-period was collected by tax farmers, state officials, lump-sum negotiated payments, or some combination thereof (p. 295–296). Key independent variables are measured as follows: the nature of the tax (direct versus indirect) is coded based on whether the tax falls on immobile, easily measured assets like land or on mobile, variable assets like traded commodities (p. 291–292); the development of control technologies is assessed through historical evidence on transportation times, communications infrastructure, and record-keeping practices (p. 298); country size is assessed in broad comparative terms; ruler autonomy is assessed through the presence or absence of absolutist versus parliamentary regimes (p. 291, 298–299). The data are drawn from a wide range of secondary historical works, including Matthews /ˈmæθjuːz/ on the French Royal General Farms, Ashton /ˈæʃtən/ on English revenue farming, Dietz /diːts/ on English public finance, Wolfe /wʊlf/ on Renaissance France, and many others cited in the extensive endnotes (p. 306–315). Kiser explicitly acknowledges that the data are preliminary and not sufficient for systematic hypothesis testing, but argues they can provide useful preliminary assessments of the theoretical approaches (p. 295).
zh: 凯泽使用的是从二手历史文献中提取的定性比较历史数据，涵盖多个早期现代国家的财政体系。地理覆盖范围包括英格兰 /ˈɪŋɡlənd/、法兰西 /fræns/、西班牙 /speɪn/、普鲁士 /ˈprʌʃə/、瑞典 /ˈswiːdən/、中国 /ˈtʃaɪnə/、俄罗斯 /ˈrʌʃə/、奥斯曼帝国 /ˈɒtəmən ˈɛmpaɪər/ 和尼德兰 /ˈnɛðərləndz/（第295页）。时间跨度大约从13世纪到18世纪晚期，其中对英格兰（1275–1688年）和法兰西（13世纪至1789年大革命）的覆盖最为详细（第295–299页）。分析单位各有不同：有时是国家-时段（如伊丽莎白时代 /ɪˌlɪzəˈbiːθən/ 的英格兰），有时是特定税种（如英国关税、法国盐税 /ɡəˈbɛl/），有时是特定的包税组织（如法国总包税场）。因变量——税收征集形式——通过定性方式测量，即特定国家-时段的特定税种是由包税人、国家官员、一次性协商支付还是某种组合方式征收（第295–296页）。主要自变量的测量方式如下：税种性质（直接税与间接税）根据税收是否落在土地等不动产、易于度量的资产上还是贸易商品等流动性、可变资产上来编码（第291–292页）；控制技术的发展通过关于交通运输时间、通讯基础设施和档案记录实践的历史证据来评估（第298页）；国家规模以宽泛的比较方式评估；统治者自主性通过绝对主义政体与议会制政体的存在与否来评估（第291、298–299页）。数据来源于大量二手历史著作，包括马修斯 /ˈmæθjuːz/ 关于法国皇家总包税场的研究、阿什顿 /ˈæʃtən/ 关于英国收入包税的研究、迪茨 /diːts/ 关于英国公共财政的研究、沃尔夫 /wʊlf/ 关于文艺复兴时期法兰西的研究，以及大量尾注中引用的其他文献（第306–315页）。凯泽明确承认这些数据是初步性的，不足以进行系统性的假设检验，但认为它们可以为理论方法提供有用的初步评估（第295页）。

en: Kiser employs comparative-historical analysis guided by rational choice theory, specifically principal-agent (agency) theory. The method involves deriving a set of testable propositions from the theoretical model and then assessing their plausibility against the historical record of multiple early modern states. Seven formal propositions are stated (p. 294–295): (1) the greater the difficulty of controlling agents, the greater the use of tax farming; (2) tax farming will be more common for indirect than direct taxes; (3) larger countries will use more tax farming; (4) higher ruler autonomy leads to more tax farming; (5) better control technologies lead to less tax farming; (6) switching between systems will be common; and (7) tax farms will have higher salaries, better monitoring, and faster innovation than state administrations. Each proposition is then assessed against the available historical evidence from the cases examined. The assessment is conducted through structured qualitative comparison: Kiser compares patterns across types of taxes within countries, across countries at similar time periods, and within countries over time. He also uses process tracing to examine the causal mechanisms at work in specific historical episodes, such as the consolidation of English and French tax farms and the timing of the shift from tax farming to state administration relative to political revolutions (p. 295–300). For the key methodological assumptions to hold, several conditions must be met: rulers must be reasonably approximated as rational revenue-maximizers; the structural factors identified (control technologies, country size, tax type, ruler autonomy) must be the primary determinants of control capacity; and the secondary historical sources must provide reasonably accurate accounts of the fiscal institutions described. Kiser acknowledges that the data are not adequate for systematic testing and that the results should be treated as preliminary (p. 295, 303).
zh: 凯泽采用了以理性选择理论，特别是委托-代理（代理）理论为指导的比较历史分析方法。其方法是从理论模型中推导出一组可检验的命题，然后对照多个早期现代国家的历史记录评估其合理性。文章陈述了七个正式命题（第294–295页）：（1）控制代理人的难度越大，使用包税制的程度越高；（2）包税制用于间接税比直接税更为普遍；（3）较大的国家更多使用包税制；（4）统治者自主性越高，包税制越多；（5）控制技术越发达，包税制越少；（6）在不同体系之间切换将是常见的；（7）包税场的薪资将更高、监督将更好、控制技术的创新将更快。然后，每个命题都对照所考察案例的可用历史证据进行评估。评估通过结构化的定性比较进行：凯泽比较了同一国家内不同税种之间的模式、相似时期不同国家之间的模式，以及同一国家内随时间变化的模式。他还使用过程追踪来考察特定历史事件中运作的因果机制，如英法包税场的整合以及从包税制向国家行政征收转变的时机与政治革命之间的关系（第295–300页）。为使关键方法论假设成立，需要满足若干条件：统治者必须可以合理地近似为理性的收入最大化者；所识别的结构性因素（控制技术、国家规模、税种、统治者自主性）必须是控制能力的主要决定因素；二手历史文献必须提供对所描述财政制度的合理准确的记述。凯泽承认数据不足以进行系统性检验，结果应被视为初步性的（第295、303页）。

en: What would the academia see as the reading’s main contribution?

The article makes several important contributions that would be recognized by scholars in political science, historical sociology, and economic history. First, and most significantly, Kiser offers a new theoretical framework that applies principal-agent theory — originally developed in economics to analyze the firm — to the analysis of state fiscal institutions. This represents an innovative bridge between rational choice institutionalism and comparative-historical analysis, demonstrating that formal microeconomic models of organizational design can be productively applied to the study of premodern state building (p. 289–290). Second, Kiser provides a major reinterpretation of tax farming, challenging the longstanding consensus (from Adam Smith /ˈædəm smɪθ/ through Weber /ˈveɪbər/ to Webber /ˈwɛbər/ and Wildavsky /wɪlˈdævski/) that tax farming was inherently inefficient. By arguing that tax farming was often the most efficient system given prevailing structural constraints, Kiser reframes the history of fiscal state building as a story of rational adaptation to changing conditions rather than a teleological march toward bureaucratic rationality (p. 302–303). Third, the article systematically evaluates and compares multiple alternative explanations — the insurance/predictability argument, Williamson’s /ˈwɪljəmsənz/ transaction costs theory, North’s /nɔːrθs/ arguments about institutional change, Levi’s /ˈliːvaɪz/ power/dependence model, and Webber and Wildavsky’s political culture argument — finding that agency theory can generate additional predictions that the alternatives cannot, particularly regarding variations over time and across countries (p. 286–289, 304). Fourth, the article contributes a rich comparative-historical dataset synthesizing evidence from nine different early modern polities, providing a broader empirical base than most prior studies which tended to focus on a single country.
zh: / 学术贡献：学界如何看待该文献的主要贡献？

这篇文章做出了若干重要贡献，会得到政治学、历史社会学和经济史领域学者的认可。首先，也是最重要的，凯泽提供了一个新的理论框架，将最初在经济学中发展起来用于分析企业的委托-代理理论应用于国家财政制度的分析。这代表了理性选择制度主义与比较历史分析之间的创新性桥梁，表明组织设计的形式微观经济模型可以有效地应用于前现代国家建设的研究（第289–290页）。其次，凯泽对包税制进行了重大的重新解释，挑战了从亚当·斯密 /ˈædəm smɪθ/ 到韦伯 /ˈveɪbər/，再到韦伯尔 /ˈwɛbər/ 和怀尔达夫斯基 /wɪlˈdævski/ 以来关于包税制天然低效的长期共识。通过论证包税制在当时的结构性约束条件下往往是最有效的制度，凯泽将财政国家建设的历史重新框定为对变化条件的理性适应，而非向官僚理性的目的论式进军（第302–303页）。第三，文章系统地评估和比较了多种替代性解释——保险/可预测性论点、威廉姆森 /ˈwɪljəmsənz/ 的交易成本理论、诺斯 /nɔːrθs/ 关于制度变迁的论点、列维 /ˈliːvaɪz/ 的权力/依赖模型，以及韦伯尔和怀尔达夫斯基的政治文化论点——发现代理理论能够产生替代理论无法产生的额外预测，特别是关于时间变化和跨国变异（第286–289、304页）。第四，文章贡献了一个丰富的比较历史数据集，综合了九个不同早期现代政体的证据，提供了比大多数先前单一国家研究更广泛的经验基础。

en: What would professional scholars and researchers identify as limitations?
Professional scholars would likely identify several limitations:

(1) Data limitations and lack of systematic testing. Kiser himself acknowledges that the data are preliminary and not adequate for systematic hypothesis testing (p. 295). The evidence is drawn entirely from secondary sources, and the assessments of propositions are impressionistic rather than based on systematic coding or statistical analysis. Scholars would suggest that future work should develop more systematic datasets — perhaps coding each tax-country-period unit for the form of collection, control technology levels, ruler autonomy, and other variables — to permit more rigorous cross-case comparison or even quantitative analysis.

(2) Potential selection bias in case coverage. The cases examined are predominantly Western European, with only brief coverage of China, Russia, and the Ottoman Empire. The theory’s applicability to non-European fiscal systems remains largely untested. Scholars would recommend extending the analysis to a wider range of polities, including those in South and Southeast Asia, the Islamic world, and pre-Columbian Americas, to assess the generalizability of the framework.

(3) The autonomy variable performs poorly. Kiser finds that ruler autonomy does not work well as a predictor: Prussia /ˈprʌʃə/ had high ruler autonomy but did not use tax farming, while the Netherlands /ˈnɛðərləndz/ had no autonomous absolute monarch but did use tax farming (p. 300, 305). This significant anomaly is left largely unresolved. Scholars would suggest either reconceptualizing the autonomy variable (perhaps as a threshold rather than linear effect, as Kiser tentatively suggests in note 160, p. 315) or incorporating additional mediating variables.

(4) Endogeneity and reverse causality. The relationship between tax farming and control technologies may be endogenous: as Kiser himself shows, the consolidation of tax farms spurred the development of sophisticated administrative techniques that were later adopted by state bureaucracies (p. 299–300). This raises the question of whether control technology drives the choice of fiscal system or whether the fiscal system drives the development of control technology. Scholars would recommend more careful process tracing or the identification of exogenous shocks to control technology (such as specific innovations in record-keeping or transportation) to disentangle this relationship.

(5) Undertheorized role of political conflict and collective action. The framework focuses heavily on the ruler-as-principal and treats the choice of fiscal system as primarily a top-down decision. It gives relatively little attention to the role of taxpayer resistance, elite bargaining, parliamentary politics, and social movements in shaping fiscal institutions. While Kiser notes that taxpayers are formally outside the agency relation (p. 290), their collective action clearly shaped fiscal outcomes (as the attacks on tax farmers during the French Revolution illustrate, p. 299). Scholars would recommend integrating insights from fiscal sociology and the literature on taxation and representation to provide a more complete account of the political determinants of fiscal structure.
zh: / 建议：专业学者会指出哪些局限性？
专业学者可能会指出以下几个局限性：
（1）数据局限和缺乏系统性检验。 凯泽自己承认数据是初步性的，不足以进行系统性假设检验（第295页）。证据完全来自二手文献，对命题的评估是印象式的，而非基于系统性编码或统计分析。学者会建议未来研究应开发更系统的数据集——或许可以对每个税种-国家-时段单位编码其征集形式、控制技术水平、统治者自主性和其他变量——以允许更严格的跨案例比较甚至定量分析。
（2）案例覆盖的潜在选择偏差。 所考察的案例以西欧为主，对中国、俄罗斯和奥斯曼帝国的覆盖只是简略的。该理论对非欧洲财政体系的适用性在很大程度上未经检验。学者会建议将分析扩展到更广泛的政体范围，包括南亚和东南亚、伊斯兰世界和前哥伦布美洲的政体，以评估该框架的可推广性。
（3）自主性变量表现不佳。 凯泽发现统治者自主性作为预测变量效果不好：普鲁士 /ˈprʌʃə/ 拥有高度统治者自主性但不使用包税制，而尼德兰 /ˈnɛðərləndz/ 没有自主的绝对君主却使用包税制（第300、305页）。这一重大异常在很大程度上未得到解决。学者会建议重新概念化自主性变量（或许如凯泽在注释160第315页中试探性建议的那样，将其视为阈值效应而非线性效应），或纳入额外的中介变量。
（4）内生性和反向因果。 包税制与控制技术之间的关系可能是内生的：如凯泽自己所展示的，包税场的整合推动了精密行政技术的发展，这些技术后来被国家官僚机构采用（第299–300页）。这引发了一个问题：到底是控制技术驱动了财政制度的选择，还是财政制度驱动了控制技术的发展？学者会建议进行更细致的过程追踪，或识别对控制技术的外生冲击（如档案记录或交通运输中的具体创新），以厘清这一关系。

（5）政治冲突和集体行动的作用理论化不足。 该框架过于聚焦于作为委托人的统治者，将财政制度的选择主要视为自上而下的决策。它对纳税人抵抗、精英谈判、议会政治和社会运动在塑造财政制度中的作用关注相对不足。虽然凯泽指出纳税人在形式上处于代理关系之外（第290页），但他们的集体行动显然塑造了财政结果（如法国大革命期间对包税人的攻击所表明的那样，第299页）。学者会建议整合财政社会学以及关于税收与代表制文献的洞见，以提供对财政结构政治决定因素的更完整解释。

en: What potential extensions or new research questions are inspired by this paper?
Professional scholars would propose several promising extensions:

(1) Formal modeling of the ruler’s optimization problem. Kiser’s argument is stated verbally rather than in a formal mathematical model. A natural extension would be to construct a formal principal-agent model in which a ruler chooses between fixed-salary contracts, residual-claimant contracts, and proportional-payment contracts as a function of monitoring costs, the variability of the tax base, and the agent’s outside options. Such a model could generate sharper comparative static predictions and identify conditions under which intermediate forms (proportional payment) dominate the corner solutions.

(2) Quantitative testing with systematic data. The most obvious extension is to construct a systematic dataset covering the form of tax collection for all major taxes in all major European (and non-European) polities across the early modern period, and to use this dataset for quantitative analysis — panel regressions, event-history analysis of transitions between systems, or matching methods to compare otherwise-similar taxes collected under different institutional arrangements. The work of scholars such as Mark Dincecco /dɪnˈtʃɛkoʊ/ on European fiscal capacity provides a model for how such quantitative work might proceed.

(3) Extension to contemporary developing states. Kiser notes in passing that many features of early modern fiscal systems persist in contemporary developing countries (note 148, p. 314). A fruitful extension would be to apply the principal-agent framework to the study of tax collection in contemporary states where rulers face similar control problems — for example, the use of outsourced tax collection in sub-Saharan Africa, the contracting out of customs collection to firms like SGS or Cotecna, or the rise of digital tax administration technologies as a modern analogue to the control technologies Kiser emphasizes.

(4) The micro-politics of tax farming contracts. Kiser treats the auction process and the terms of tax farming contracts as largely given, but the design of these contracts is itself an important object of study. How were bidding processes structured? How did rulers set reserve prices? How did the terms of contracts (duration, penalties for default, provisions regarding loans) evolve over time, and how did these contract design features affect the efficiency and distributional consequences of tax farming? Archival research on surviving tax farming contracts could provide rich micro-level data to address these questions.

(5) The relationship between fiscal institutions and economic development. Kiser’s framework implies that the choice of fiscal system has consequences for economic development (since overtaxation by short-term tax farmers can damage the tax base), but this implication is not systematically explored. An important extension would be to examine whether regions or countries that relied more heavily on tax farming experienced different patterns of economic growth, investment, or commercial development than those that relied more on state administration — and whether the type of tax farming (short-term versus lifetime contracts, fragmented versus consolidated farms) mattered for these outcomes.

(6) Integrating the demand side: taxpayer compliance and resistance. The framework could be enriched by integrating a more systematic analysis of taxpayer behavior. Under what conditions do taxpayers prefer one system over another? When does taxpayer resistance constrain the ruler’s choice of fiscal system? How do different fiscal institutions affect the development of fiscal legitimacy and voluntary compliance? These questions connect Kiser’s framework to the broader literatures on taxation and state legitimacy, consent and coercion in state building, and the fiscal contract between rulers and ruled.
zh: / 延伸：这篇论文启发了哪些潜在的研究延伸或新研究问题？
专业学者会提出若干有前景的研究延伸：
（1）对统治者优化问题的形式化建模。 凯泽的论点是以文字而非正式数学模型的方式陈述的。一个自然的延伸是构建一个正式的委托-代理模型，其中统治者根据监督成本、税基的波动性和代理人的外部选择权，在固定薪资合同、剩余索取权合同和比例支付合同之间做出选择。这样的模型可以产生更精确的比较静态预测，并识别出中间形式（比例支付）主导角点解的条件。
（2）用系统数据进行定量检验。 最明显的延伸是构建一个系统性数据集，覆盖早期现代时期所有主要欧洲（和非欧洲）政体中所有主要税种的征集形式，并利用该数据集进行定量分析——面板回归、制度转型的事件史分析，或匹配方法以比较在不同制度安排下征收的其他方面相似的税种。如马克·丁切科 /dɪnˈtʃɛkoʊ/ 等学者关于欧洲财政能力的研究提供了此类定量工作的范例。
（3）向当代发展中国家的延伸。 凯泽顺带提到早期现代财政体系的许多特征在当代发展中国家持续存在（注释148，第314页）。一个富有成果的延伸是将委托-代理框架应用于当代国家税收征集的研究，在这些国家中统治者面临类似的控制问题——例如撒哈拉以南非洲的外包税收征集、将海关征收外包给SGS或Cotecna等公司，或数字化税收管理技术的兴起（作为凯泽所强调的控制技术的现代类比）。
（4）包税合同的微观政治。 凯泽将拍卖过程和包税合同条款基本视为既定的，但这些合同的设计本身就是一个重要的研究对象。竞标过程如何结构化？统治者如何设定底价？合同条款（期限、违约惩罚、贷款条款）如何随时间演变，这些合同设计特征如何影响包税制的效率和分配后果？对留存包税合同的档案研究可以提供丰富的微观层面数据来回答这些问题。
（5）财政制度与经济发展的关系。 凯泽的框架暗示财政制度的选择对经济发展有后果（因为短期包税人的过度征税可能损害税基），但这一含义没有被系统探索。一个重要的延伸是考察更多依赖包税制的地区或国家是否经历了不同于更多依赖国家行政征收的地区或国家的经济增长、投资或商业发展模式——以及包税制的类型（短期合同与终身合同、分散包税场与整合包税场）是否对这些结果有影响。
（6）整合需求侧：纳税人合规与抵抗。 该框架可以通过整合对纳税人行为的更系统分析来丰富。在什么条件下纳税人偏好一种制度而非另一种？纳税人抵抗何时约束统治者的财政制度选择？不同的财政制度如何影响财政合法性和自愿合规的发展？这些问题将凯泽的框架与关于税收与国家合法性、国家建设中的同意与强制、以及统治者与被统治者之间财政契约的更广泛文献联系起来。

en: “Size and Dynastic Decline: The Principal-Agent Problem in Late Imperial China, 1700–1850”
zh: 《规模与王朝衰落：晚期中华帝国的委托—代理问题，1700—1850》

en: Tuan-Hwee Sng /twɑːn hweɪ sŋ/, Department of Economics, National University of Singapore /ˌsɪŋəˈpɔːr/ (p. 107).
zh: 孙端辉（Tuan-Hwee Sng /twɑːn hweɪ sŋ/），新加坡国立大学（National University of Singapore /ˌsɪŋəˈpɔːr/）经济学系（第107页）。

en: 2014, published in Explorations in Economic History, Volume 54, pages 107–127 (p. 107).
zh: 2014年，发表于《经济史探索》（Explorations in Economic History），第54卷，第107至127页（第107页）。

en: The paper has multiple interconnected dependent variables, all revolving around state fiscal capacity and its decline. The most important is the Qing /tʃɪŋ/ state’s tax revenue over time, measured in both nominal silver taels, real terms (converted to rice equivalents), and per capita terms. The author shows that the Qing state’s real tax revenue peaked early in the eighteenth century and then contracted steadily even as the economy continued to grow (p. 109). Beyond aggregate revenue, the paper also examines the spatial distribution of land tax per capita across prefectures and the density of administrative units (number of counties per 10,000 km²), both measured circa 1820. Finally, the paper treats the decline in public goods provision—military strength and local administrative capacity—as downstream consequences of fiscal contraction (pp. 119–122). In sum, the dependent variable is fundamentally about why a large, stable, and outwardly powerful autocratic state was fiscally weak and grew progressively weaker over time, and what this fiscal weakness meant for China’s ability to respond to the crises of the nineteenth century.
zh: 本文有多个相互关联的因变量，全部围绕国家财政能力及其衰退展开。最核心的因变量是清朝（Qing /tʃɪŋ/）国家的税收收入随时间的变化，以名义白银两、实际值（折算为大米等价物）和人均值三种方式衡量。作者表明，清朝的实际税收收入在十八世纪初达到峰值，此后即使经济持续增长，税收却稳步萎缩（第109页）。除总收入外，本文还考察了1820年前后各府的人均土地税的空间分布以及行政单位密度（每万平方公里的县数量）。最后，本文将公共物品供给的下降——军事力量与地方行政能力——视为财政萎缩的下游后果（第119至122页）。总之，因变量从根本上关注的是：为何一个庞大、稳定、外表强大的专制国家在财政上是虚弱的，并且随时间日益衰弱，以及这种财政虚弱对中国应对十九世纪危机的能力意味着什么。

en: The main independent variable is the size of the Chinese empire, understood in two dimensions: geographic size and economic size. Geographic size refers to the physical extent of the territory that the emperor must govern from the capital Beijing /beɪˈdʒɪŋ/. It determines the severity of information asymmetry between the ruler (the principal) and his agents (local officials) scattered across the empire. The author operationalizes geographic size as the distance from each prefecture to Beijing, measured along imperial postal relay routes (p. 114). Economic size refers to the aggregate output of the economy (Y = AN, where A is agricultural output per capita and N is population). As the economy grows through population expansion—the Chinese population more than doubled from less than 150 million to over 400 million between 1650 and 1850 (p. 109, footnote 6)—the economic stakes at every administrative node increase, intensifying agents’ incentives for corruption. Hence, the independent variable is not merely “bigness” in the static sense, but a dynamic interaction between territorial extent and economic expansion that progressively worsened the principal-agent problem over time.
zh: 主要自变量是中华帝国的规模，从两个维度理解：地理规模和经济规模。地理规模指的是皇帝必须从首都北京（Beijing /beɪˈdʒɪŋ/）治理的疆域物理范围。它决定了统治者（委托人）与散布帝国各地的代理人（地方官员）之间信息不对称的严重程度。作者将地理规模操作化为各府到北京的距离，沿帝国驿道路线度量（第114页）。经济规模指的是经济的总产出（Y = AN，其中A为人均农业产出，N为人口数量）。随着经济通过人口扩张而增长——中国人口从1650年不足1.5亿增长到1850年超过4亿（第109页，注释6）——每个行政节点的经济利益增加，加剧了代理人的腐败动机。因此，自变量不仅仅是静态意义上的“庞大”，而是领土幅员与经济扩张之间的动态交互，随时间推移不断恶化委托—代理问题。

en: Sng conceptualizes the Chinese state as a large, stable dictatorship governed through a centralized, hierarchical bureaucracy—not as the kind of despotic Leviathan that older scholarship (e.g., Wittfogel /ˈvɪtfəʊɡəl/, 1957; Balazs /ˈbɑːlɑːʒ/, 1964) described. Building on Mancur Olson /ˈmæŋkər ˈoʊlsən/’s (1993) theory of the stationary bandit, Sng treats the emperor as a rational ruler with an encompassing interest in the long-term prosperity of his domain—he understands that excessive exaction reduces future revenues and provokes rebellion. The problem is not that the ruler is predatory, but that his agents are. The state is therefore conceptualized primarily through the lens of the principal-agent problem: the emperor is the principal who sets tax rates and attempts to monitor his bureaucracy; the magistrates, clerks, runners, and local gentry are the agents who collect taxes on his behalf but have private incentives to extort taxpayers for personal gain (pp. 107–108, 111).

Sng measures “stateness” or state capacity in several concrete ways. First, fiscal capacity is measured as total tax revenue, reconstructed from official Qing documents including the Collected Statutes of the Qing Dynasty, the Veritable Records, and archival records from the National Library of China (p. 117). Second, administrative capacity is measured as the density of county-level administrative units (counties per 10,000 km²) and the ratio of officials to population—around 20,000 ranked officials for a population exceeding 300 million in 1800, or roughly one official per 15,000 people (p. 113). Third, coercive capacity is proxied by troop deployment data, specifically the statutory number of Green Standard Army soldiers in the provinces (p. 122, Table 6). In all three dimensions, Sng shows that the Chinese state was remarkably thin. This picture stands in sharp contrast to the traditional “Oriental Despotism” image and aligns instead with a growing consensus among Chinese historians that the Qing state was a “night watchman state” that governed minimally and administered sparingly (p. 120).

This conceptualization is closely connected to the broader state formation literature. Unlike the European experience analyzed by Charles Tilly /ˈtʃɑːrlz ˈtɪli/, where interstate warfare drove state-building and fiscal extraction (Tilly, 1990), Sng’s China faced a peaceful external environment during the eighteenth century, which reduced the pressure to build fiscal capacity (p. 119). And unlike the European absolutist states analyzed by Dincecco /dɪnˈtʃɛkoʊ/ (2011) and Hoffman /ˈhɒfmən/ and Rosenthal /ˈroʊzənθɔːl/ (1997), where institutional fragmentation limited the extractive power of kings, China had overcome jurisdictional fragmentation quite early through a nonhereditary bureaucracy selected by civil service examinations (p. 108, footnote 4). The puzzle, then, is why a politically unified empire with no institutional fragmentation remained fiscally weak—and the answer, Sng argues, lies in the principal-agent problem that size imposed.
zh: 孙端辉将中国国家概念化为一个大型、稳定的独裁政权，通过中央集权的层级官僚体系进行治理——而非旧有学术文献（如魏特夫 Wittfogel /ˈvɪtfəʊɡəl/，1957；白乐日 Balazs /ˈbɑːlɑːʒ/，1964）所描述的那种专制利维坦。在曼瑟·奥尔森（Mancur Olson /ˈmæŋkər ˈoʊlsən/，1993）“驻足匪帮”（stationary bandit）理论基础上，孙端辉将皇帝视为一个理性统治者，对其领地的长期繁荣拥有涵盖性利益——他明白过度榨取会减少未来收入并激发叛乱。问题不在于统治者是掠夺性的，而在于他的代理人是。因此，国家主要通过委托—代理问题的视角被概念化：皇帝是设定税率并试图监督官僚的委托人；知县、胥吏、衙役和地方绅士则是代他征税但有私人动机向纳税人勒索敛财的代理人（第107至108页，第111页）。

孙端辉以几种具体方式度量“国家性”或国家能力。第一，财政能力以总税收收入衡量，根据清朝官方文献重建，包括《大清会典》、《清实录》以及中国国家图书馆的档案记录（第117页）。第二，行政能力以县级行政单位密度（每万平方公里的县数量）和官民比衡量——1800年约有20,000名有品级官员治理超过3亿人口，约每15,000人对应一名官员（第113页）。第三，强制能力以军队部署数据代理，具体为各省绿营兵的编制人数（第122页，表6）。在所有三个维度上，孙端辉都表明中国国家极为单薄。这幅图景与传统的“东方专制主义”形象形成鲜明对比，而与中国历史学家日益形成的共识一致：清朝是一个治理极简、行政俭省的“守夜人国家”（第120页）。

这一概念化与更广泛的国家形成文献密切相关。与查尔斯·蒂利（Charles Tilly /ˈtʃɑːrlz ˈtɪli/，1990）分析的欧洲经验不同——在欧洲，国际间战争推动了国家建设和财政汲取——孙端辉笔下的中国在十八世纪面临和平的外部环境，这降低了建设财政能力的压力（第119页）。与丁切科（Dincecco /dɪnˈtʃɛkoʊ/，2011）以及霍夫曼（Hoffman /ˈhɒfmən/）和罗森塔尔（Rosenthal /ˈroʊzənθɔːl/，1997）分析的欧洲绝对君主制国家也不同——在那些国家，制度碎片化限制了国王的榨取权力——中国很早就通过科举选拔的非世袭官僚制克服了管辖碎片化（第108页，注释4）。那么谜题就是：为何一个政治统一、没有制度碎片化的帝国仍然财政虚弱——答案，孙端辉论证道，在于规模所施加的委托—代理问题。

en: The core argument is that China’s vast territorial size created a severe principal-agent problem that constrained the state’s fiscal capacity, and economic expansion during the prosperous eighteenth century paradoxically worsened the problem, causing fiscal decline that sowed the seeds of the nineteenth-century crises.

The argument proceeds in several steps. First, Sng challenges the traditional view that China’s economic stagnation was caused by an all-powerful, interventionist autocratic state that suppressed private enterprise (Wittfogel, 1957; Balazs, 1964). He points out that this view is theoretically problematic because the Chinese emperor, as a stable dictator with long time horizons, had rational incentives to restrain his exaction to preserve future revenue and maintain political stability, as Olson (1993) demonstrated (p. 107). The view is also empirically inconsistent with recent scholarship showing that the eighteenth-century Chinese economy was far more commercialized, market-integrated, and developed than previously assumed—as argued by Pomeranz /ˈpɒmərænts/ (2000), Shiue /ʃjuː/ and Keller /ˈkɛlər/ (2007), and others (p. 108).

Second, if the eighteenth-century economy was prosperous, why did China’s fortunes reverse in the nineteenth century? Sng proposes that the answer lies not in the ruler’s behavior but in the behavior of his agents. Reprising the earlier work of Kiser /ˈkaɪzər/ and Tong /tɒŋ/ (1992), Sng argues that in a large dictatorship, excessive exploitation comes not from the ruler—who has encompassing interests—but from his agents, who have shorter decision horizons and less encompassing interests. The agents have private incentives to extort the taxpayers for personal enrichment (p. 108).

Third, size exacerbates this problem through two channels. Geographically, the vast territory makes monitoring costly. Information must travel enormous distances—in 1853, news of Wuchang /ˈwuːtʃɑːŋ/’s fall to the Taiping /ˈtaɪˌpɪŋ/ rebels took eight days to reach Beijing, 1200 km away (p. 108). Regional diversity further complicates monitoring because local agents require flexibility to adapt central government decrees to local conditions, but that same flexibility provides cover for self-interested deviations (p. 108). Economically, as the economy grows, the rent-seeking incentives of agents increase because there is more surplus to extract. This puts pressure on the ruler to lower the tax rate to prevent the total effective burden (official tax plus unofficial extortion) from exceeding the rebellion threshold (p. 108).

Fourth, and most critically, Sng argues that if the principal-agent problem is sufficiently severe, economic expansion can actually hurt the ruler’s fiscal position. While economic growth expands the tax base (positive effect), it also increases corruption which forces the ruler to lower the tax rate (negative effect). Under severe agency costs, the negative effect eventually dominates the positive effect, producing a hump-shaped pattern: tax revenue first rises and then falls as the economy continues to grow. This is the paper’s most striking theoretical prediction—the “paradox of development” in which a regime that successfully fosters growth may become a victim of its own success (pp. 108, 113, 119).
zh: 核心论点是：中国辽阔的疆域造成了严重的委托—代理问题，制约了国家的财政能力，而十八世纪繁荣时期的经济扩张反而使问题悖论性地恶化，导致了财政衰退，为十九世纪的危机埋下了种子。

这一论点分几步推进。首先，孙端辉挑战了传统观点，即中国的经济停滞是由一个全能的、干预主义的专制国家压制私人企业所导致（魏特夫，1957；白乐日，1964）。他指出这一观点在理论上有问题，因为中国皇帝作为一个稳定的独裁者，有着长远的时间视野，有理性动机约束自己的榨取以保全未来收入和维持政治稳定，正如奥尔森（1993）所论证的（第107页）。这一观点在经验上也与近年学术研究不一致——这些研究表明，十八世纪的中国经济远比此前认为的更加商业化、市场整合和发达——如彭慕兰（Pomeranz /ˈpɒmərænts/，2000）、史志宏（Shiue /ʃjuː/）和凯勒（Keller /ˈkɛlər/，2007）等人所论证（第108页）。
其次，如果十八世纪经济繁荣，为何中国的命运在十九世纪逆转？孙端辉提出答案不在于统治者的行为，而在于其代理人的行为。延续基瑟（Kiser /ˈkaɪzər/）和佟（Tong /tɒŋ/，1992）的早期研究，孙端辉论证在大型独裁政权中，过度榨取不是来自统治者——统治者有涵盖性利益——而是来自他的代理人，代理人有更短的决策视野和更不具涵盖性的利益。代理人有私人动机向纳税人勒索以自肥（第108页）。

第三，规模通过两条渠道加剧了这一问题。在地理上，辽阔疆域使监督成本高昂。信息必须跨越巨大距离传递——1853年太平军（Taiping /ˈtaɪˌpɪŋ/）攻占武昌（Wuchang /ˈwuːtʃɑːŋ/）的消息花了八天才传到1200公里外的北京（第108页）。地域多样性进一步增加了监督难度，因为地方代理人需要灵活变通中央政府法令以适应当地情况，但这种灵活性同时也为自利偏离提供了掩护（第108页）。在经济上，随着经济增长，代理人的寻租激励加强，因为有更多的剩余可供榨取。这迫使统治者降低税率，以防止总有效负担（官方税加非官方勒索）超过叛乱门槛（第108页）。

第四，也是最关键的，孙端辉论证如果委托—代理问题足够严重，经济扩张实际上可能损害统治者的财政状况。虽然经济增长扩大了税基（正效应），但它同时增加了腐败，迫使统治者降低税率（负效应）。在严重的代理成本下，负效应最终压倒正效应，产生驼峰形模式：税收收入先升后降，即使经济继续增长。这是本文最引人注目的理论预测——“发展悖论”，即一个成功促进增长的政权可能成为自身成功的牺牲品（第108、113、119页）。

en: The paper proposes a clear causal chain with several linked mechanisms:

Mechanism 1: Information asymmetry from geographic distance. The ruler resides in Beijing and must rely on agents stationed across a vast empire to collect taxes and administer justice. Distance increases the cost and time of transmitting information. Regional diversity compounds the problem because local agents must exercise discretion, and the ruler cannot easily distinguish between an agent who deviates for legitimate local reasons and one who deviates for personal gain (p. 108). The formal model captures this through a monitoring parameter m, where the probability of detecting extortion ε is mε². A larger territory implies a smaller m for distant regions, meaning the ruler’s audit is less effective (p. 112).

Mechanism 2: Agent extortion (“tyranny at the bottom”). Given weak monitoring, the agent’s optimal extortion rate is ε* = Y/(2mX), where Y is aggregate output and X is the punishment for getting caught (p. 112, Eq. 1). This means extortion increases with the size of the economy and decreases with monitoring capacity. The representative agent in the model embodies the magistrate, county clerks, runners, and local gentry, all of whom had historical incentives and opportunities to exploit taxpayers—for example, by manipulating tax documents, imposing unauthorized surcharges, or engaging in tax arbitrage (pp. 110–111).

Mechanism 3: The ruler’s constrained response—low taxes. The ruler understands that the total effective tax burden on the peasants is τ + ε (official tax rate plus extortion rate). If this exceeds a rebellion threshold r, the peasants revolt. Because the ruler wants to avoid revolt at all costs, he sets τ* = r − Y/(2mX) (p. 112, Eq. 2). When monitoring is weak (m is small), equilibrium extortion is high (ε* is large), so the ruler must set the official tax rate low. This is Proposition 1: “a severe principal-agent problem leads to a (fiscally) weak state” (p. 112). It explains the paradox that official taxes in Qing China were low while the effective burden on peasants was relatively heavy.

Mechanism 4: Small bureaucracy. When the ruler extends the model to choose the number of counties (β), the equilibrium condition τY = C'(β) (p. 113, Eq. 3) means that a low equilibrium tax rate implies a smaller bureaucracy, because the marginal benefit of adding another county is too small to justify the cost. This is Proposition 2: “a severe principal-agent problem leads to a small bureaucracy” (p. 113). Historically, there were only about 20,000 ranked officials governing over 300 million people (p. 113), and this extraordinary thinness of the administrative apparatus is predicted by the model.

Mechanism 5: The dynamic trap—economic growth worsening fiscal capacity. As the population N grows, extortion ε* rises (dε*/dN > 0) and the tax rate τ* falls (dτ*/dN < 0). When m is small enough, the negative effect (falling tax rate) eventually overwhelms the positive effect (growing tax base), and total tax revenue VR = τ*βY follows a hump-shaped pattern (p. 113). This is Proposition 3: “if the principal-agent problem is severe, economic expansion will initially increase the tax revenue but eventually depress it” (p. 113). This mechanism explains why the Qing state’s real tax revenue peaked early in the eighteenth century and then declined steadily through the prosperous High Qing period, long before the military and social upheavals of the nineteenth century.

Mechanism 6: Fiscal decline leading to reduced public goods and vulnerability. The sustained revenue contraction led to cuts in military spending (troop numbers declined in southern provinces before the Opium War) and administrative underinvestment (the number of counties barely increased despite a population that more than doubled). Understaffed and underfunded magistracies could not maintain social order, especially in fast-growing frontier areas where communal violence was common (p. 122). The White Lotus Rebellion (1796–1804), the Taiping Rebellion (1850–64), and China’s defeat in the Opium War (1839–42) were all consequences, not causes, of this long-term fiscal and administrative decline (pp. 119–123).
zh: 本文提出了一条由多个环节链接的清晰因果链：

机制1：地理距离导致的信息不对称。 统治者驻扎在北京，必须依靠部署在广袤帝国各地的代理人征税和司法。距离增加了信息传递的成本和时间。地域多样性使问题更加复杂，因为地方代理人必须行使裁量权，而统治者难以区分一个因合理的地方原因而偏离的代理人与一个为私利而偏离的代理人（第108页）。正式模型通过监督参数m捕捉这一点，发现勒索ε的概率为mε²。更大的领土意味着对偏远地区而言m更小，即统治者的审计效力更弱（第112页）。
机制2：代理人勒索（“底层暴政”）。 在监督薄弱的情况下，代理人的最优勒索率为ε* = Y/(2mX)，其中Y是总产出，X是被抓获的惩罚（第112页，方程1）。这意味着勒索随经济规模增大而增加，随监督能力增强而减少。模型中的代表性代理人体现了知县、胥吏、衙役和地方绅士，他们在历史上都有剥削纳税人的动机和机会——例如，操纵税务文件、征收未经授权的附加费、或从事税收套利（第110至111页）。

机制3：统治者的受限回应——低税率。 统治者明白农民承受的总有效税负是τ + ε（官方税率加勒索率）。如果超过叛乱门槛r，农民就会造反。因为统治者不惜一切代价避免叛乱，他设定τ* = r − Y/(2mX)（第112页，方程2）。当监督薄弱（m小）时，均衡勒索率高（ε*大），所以统治者必须设定低官方税率。这就是命题1：“严重的委托—代理问题导致（财政上）虚弱的国家”（第112页）。它解释了清代中国官方税收低而农民实际负担却相对沉重的悖论。
机制4：小官僚机构。 当统治者将模型扩展到选择县的数量（β）时，均衡条件τY = C'(β)（第113页，方程3）意味着低均衡税率对应更小的官僚机构，因为增设一个县的边际收益太小，不足以抵偿成本。这就是命题2：“严重的委托—代理问题导致小官僚机构”（第113页）。历史上，仅约20,000名有品级官员治理超过3亿人口（第113页），这种行政机器的非凡单薄正是模型所预测的。

机制5：动态陷阱——经济增长恶化财政能力。 随着人口N增长，勒索ε上升（dε/dN > 0），税率τ下降（dτ/dN < 0）。当m足够小时，负效应（税率下降）最终压倒正效应（税基增长），总税收VR = τ*βY呈驼峰形（第113页）。这就是命题3：“如果委托—代理问题严重，经济扩张最初将增加税收，但最终会压低税收”（第113页）。这一机制解释了为何清朝的实际税收在十八世纪初达到峰值，然后在繁荣的盛清时期稳步下降，远早于十九世纪的军事和社会动荡。

机制6：财政衰退导致公共物品减少和脆弱性。 持续的收入萎缩导致军费开支削减（南方各省兵力在鸦片战争前即已下降）和行政投入不足（尽管人口翻了一番多，县的数量几乎没有增加）。人手不足、经费匮乏的衙门无法维持社会秩序，尤其是在快速增长的边疆地区，那里社区暴力频发（第122页）。白莲教起义（1796—1804年）、太平天国运动（1850—1864年）以及中国在鸦片战争（1839—1842年）中的战败，都是这一长期财政和行政衰退的后果，而非原因（第119至123页）。

en: The paper uses several distinct data sources to test its three main propositions:

1. Cross-sectional data on prefectures in 1820 (for testing Propositions 1 and 2). The unit of analysis is the prefecture, of which there are 268 in China proper. The key dependent variables are (a) land tax per capita in grams of silver and (b) the number of counties per 10,000 km², both measured circa 1820. The key independent variable is the distance from each prefecture to Beijing, calculated as the sum of the GIS-estimated least-cost path from the prefectural seat to the provincial capital plus the imperial postal road distance from the provincial capital to Beijing. The control variables include population density (as a proxy for agricultural output per capita, following Acemoglu et al., 2002), average elevation, a dummy for whether the prefecture is a provincial capital seat, population size, and land area. Data sources include the Harvard University China Historical GIS Project (CHGIS, 2007) for the GIS demarcation of county seats and prefectures; the Grand Gazetteer of the Qing during the Reign of Jiaqing /dʒjɑːˈtʃɪŋ/ as reproduced in Liang /ljɑːŋ/ (2008) for population and land tax quotas; and the Collected Statutes of the Qing Dynasty (Yongzheng /jʊŋˈdʒʌŋ/ edition) for the imperial postal relay routes (pp. 114–115, Table 2).

2. Time-series data on Qing state tax revenue, 1650–1850 (for testing Proposition 3). The author reconstructed annual tax revenue from multiple published and archival Qing fiscal records. Published sources include the Collected Statutes of the Qing Dynasty, the Encyclopedia of the Historical Records of the Qing, and the Veritable Records of the Qing Dynasty. Archival sources include records from the National Library of China Department of Rare Books and Special Collections and the Library of Ancient Books in Beijing. Revenue includes the land tax (diding, stipulated in silver), the grain tax (land tax stipulated in grain), the salt tax, customs duties, and miscellaneous taxes—with the two land taxes together accounting for more than 80% of total revenue (p. 110, footnote 10). For conversion to real terms, the author uses the Wang /wɑːŋ/ (1992) rice price series. For per capita calculations, the author uses Perkins /ˈpɜːrkɪnz/ (1969) population estimates (pp. 117–118).

3. Historical narrative evidence on public goods provision. For military strength, the author uses troop deployment data from the Green Standard Army in four southern provinces—Guangdong /ɡwɑːŋˈdɒŋ/, Fujian /fuːˈdʒjɛn/, Zhejiang /dʒʌˈdʒjɑːŋ/, and Guangxi /ɡwɑːŋˈʃiː/—from Wang Qingyun /wɑːŋ tʃɪŋˈjuːn/’s (1890) collection (Table 6, p. 122). For local administration, the author draws on secondary scholarship documenting the growth of the magistrate’s workload, the outlawing of litigation masters, and the undergovernment of frontier societies (pp. 121–122). For social unrest, the author uses Chen Gaoyong /tʃɛn ɡaʊˈjʊŋ/’s (1939) chronicle of uprisings to construct a frequency plot of rebellions alongside the tax revenue series (p. 123, Fig. 4).
zh: 本文使用了几种不同的数据源来检验其三个主要命题：

1. 1820年的府级横截面数据（用于检验命题1和2）。 分析单位是府，中国本部共268个。关键因变量是（a）以克白银计的人均土地税和（b）每万平方公里的县数，均约在1820年测量。关键自变量是各府到北京的距离，计算方式为GIS估算的从府治到省会的最低成本路径加上省会到北京的帝国驿道距离之和。控制变量包括人口密度（作为人均农业产出的代理变量，遵循阿西莫格鲁等人2002年的方法）、平均海拔、该府是否为省会所在地的虚拟变量、人口总量和土地面积。数据来源包括哈佛大学中国历史地理信息系统项目（CHGIS，2007）提供的县治和府的GIS界定；嘉庆（Jiaqing /dʒjɑːˈtʃɪŋ/）朝《大清一统志》（收录于梁方仲 Liang /ljɑːŋ/，2008）提供的人口和土地税额；以及《大清会典》（雍正 Yongzheng /jʊŋˈdʒʌŋ/ 版）提供的帝国驿道路线（第114至115页，表2）。

2. 清朝国家税收的时间序列数据，1650—1850年（用于检验命题3）。 作者从多种已出版和档案的清朝财政记录中重建了年度税收。已出版来源包括《大清会典》、《清朝文献通考》和《清实录》。档案来源包括中国国家图书馆善本特藏部和北京古籍图书馆的记录。收入包括土地税（地丁，以白银计定）、漕粮（以粮食计定的土地税）、盐税、关税和杂税——两种土地税合计占总收入的80%以上（第110页，注释10）。为折算为实际值，作者使用王业键（Wang /wɑːŋ/，1992）的米价序列。为计算人均值，作者使用珀金斯（Perkins /ˈpɜːrkɪnz/，1969）的人口估计（第117至118页）。

3. 关于公共物品供给的历史叙事证据。 关于军事力量，作者使用四个南方省份——广东（Guangdong /ɡwɑːŋˈdɒŋ/）、福建（Fujian /fuːˈdʒjɛn/）、浙江（Zhejiang /dʒʌˈdʒjɑːŋ/）和广西（Guangxi /ɡwɑːŋˈʃiː/）——的绿营兵部署数据，来自王庆云（Wang Qingyun /wɑːŋ tʃɪŋˈjuːn/，1890）的汇编（表6，第122页）。关于地方行政，作者引用了记录知县工作量增长、禁止讼师、以及边疆社会治理不足的二手学术研究（第121至122页）。关于社会动乱，作者使用陈高佣（Chen Gaoyong /tʃɛn ɡaʊˈjʊŋ/，1939）的历代天灾人祸编年表构建了叛乱频次图，与税收序列并列展示（第123页，图4）。

en: The paper employs a combination of formal modeling, cross-sectional regression analysis, time-series construction, and historical narrative.

Formal model. The core theoretical contribution is a simple game-theoretic model involving three players: the ruler (principal), a representative tax agent, and a peasant population. The ruler sets the tax rate τ, the agent chooses an extortion level ε, and the peasants revolt if the combined burden τ + ε exceeds a threshold r. The ruler audits the agent after tax collection; the probability of catching the agent is mε². The model is solved by backward induction and yields closed-form solutions for the equilibrium tax rate, extortion rate, and bureaucratic size. The model generates three propositions: (1) a severe principal-agent problem leads to low taxes and a weak state; (2) it leads to a small bureaucracy; (3) under severe agency costs, economic expansion eventually depresses tax revenue (pp. 111–113).

Cross-sectional regressions. To test Propositions 1 and 2, the author estimates log-linear regressions (Eq. 4, p. 114) where the dependent variable is either log(tax per capita) or log(counties per 10,000 km²), and the key regressor is distance from Beijing (in 1000 km). Standard errors are clustered by province to address potential heteroskedasticity. The baseline OLS results show that every 1000 km increase in distance from Beijing is associated with a statistically significant decrease in both land tax per capita and administrative density (Table 3, p. 117). The author addresses endogeneity concerns (that postal routes might have been routed through heavily taxed regions) in two ways: first, by showing via GIS analysis that the hypothetical slope-dependent least-cost road network closely resembles the historical imperial road system, suggesting the road system was determined mainly by physical geography rather than human choices (p. 116); second, by using the straight-line distance between each prefecture and Beijing as an instrument for road distance in IV regressions. The IV results are stable: every 1000 km increase in distance is associated with a 25.9% decrease in tax per capita and a 25.7% decrease in county density (Table 4, p. 118). Multiple robustness checks are performed, including restricting the sample to the “developed area” in the east, dropping outlier prefectures with extremely low taxes (which had experienced recent rebellions), restricting the sample to prefectures within 1500 km of Beijing, adding dummy variables for Yellow River proximity and southern provinces, using linear instead of log-linear specifications, and using alternative measures of distance, tax, and administrative density (Tables 4 and A1, pp. 118, 124–125).

The key assumptions for these methods to be valid are: (a) that the instrument (straight-line distance) satisfies the exclusion restriction—that it affects tax per capita and county density only through its effect on road distance (and hence monitoring difficulty), not through other channels. This is plausible but not beyond challenge, as straight-line distance from the capital could correlate with other factors such as economic geography, proximity to foreign threats, or historical patterns of settlement. (b) That the distance-based measure adequately captures monitoring difficulty, which conflates geographic distance with other correlated factors. (c) That the cross-sectional data from 1820 reflects long-run equilibrium patterns rather than short-run shocks. (d) That the relationship is not driven by reverse causation (e.g., the state taxing more in areas where it already had more administrative presence, rather than the other way around).

Time-series reconstruction. For Proposition 3, the author reconstructed Qing tax revenue year by year from 1650 to 1850. The reconstruction draws on both national-level aggregates from the Veritable Records and more reliable provincial-level breakdowns from other official sources. The real revenue series is obtained by deflating with rice prices; per capita revenue is computed using population estimates. The resulting figure (Fig. 3, p. 120) shows a clear hump-shaped pattern: nominal revenue rose steadily but real revenue peaked around the early 1700s and then declined, while per capita revenue fell more precipitously—from enough to feed and clothe 9.6% of the population in 1685 to only 2.3% in 1848. This is descriptive rather than causal, but the pattern is striking and directly consistent with Proposition 3.

Historical narrative. The final empirical component uses qualitative historical evidence to trace the consequences of fiscal decline. This includes analysis of troop deployment records, imperial edicts revealing the emperors’ awareness of military weaknesses (Emperor Qianlong /tʃjɛnˈlʊŋ/’s 1793 directive after the Macartney /məˈkɑːrtni/ Mission, Emperor Daoguang /daʊˈɡwɑːŋ/’s 1834 lament), the stagnation of the county system, and the geographical origins of major rebellions in undergovernment frontier areas (pp. 119–123). The historical narrative is not formally tested but serves as process-tracing evidence linking fiscal decline to concrete policy outcomes and eventual state failure.
zh: 本文综合运用了形式化建模、横截面回归分析、时间序列构建和历史叙事。

形式化模型。 核心理论贡献是一个涉及三个参与者的简单博弈论模型：统治者（委托人）、一个代表性税务代理人和农民群体。统治者设定税率τ，代理人选择勒索水平ε，如果合计负担τ + ε超过门槛r，农民就造反。统治者在税收征收后审计代理人；抓获代理人的概率为mε²。模型通过逆向归纳法求解，得出均衡税率、勒索率和官僚规模的闭式解。模型产生三个命题：(1) 严重的委托—代理问题导致低税收和弱国家；(2) 导致小官僚机构；(3) 在严重的代理成本下，经济扩张最终压低税收（第111至113页）。

横截面回归。 为检验命题1和2，作者估计了对数线性回归（方程4，第114页），因变量为log(人均税收)或log(每万平方公里县数)，关键回归变量为到北京的距离（以千公里计）。标准误按省聚类以应对潜在异方差性。基线OLS结果表明，距北京每增加1000公里，人均土地税和行政密度均显著下降（表3，第117页）。作者通过两种方式解决内生性疑虑（驿道可能经过高税区）：首先，通过GIS分析表明假设的基于坡度的最低成本路网与历史帝国驿道系统高度相似，暗示驿道系统主要由自然地理而非人为选择决定（第116页）；其次，使用各府到北京的直线距离作为路线距离的工具变量进行IV回归。IV结果稳定：距离每增加1000公里，人均税收减少25.9%，县密度减少25.7%（表4，第118页）。进行了多项稳健性检验，包括将样本限制在东部“发达地区”、剔除税收极低的异常值府（这些府近期经历过叛乱）、将样本限制在距北京1500公里以内的府、为是否临近黄河和南方省份添加虚拟变量、使用线性而非对数线性模型、以及使用距离、税收和行政密度的替代度量（表4和表A1，第118、124至125页）。

这些方法有效性所依赖的关键假设是：（a）工具变量（直线距离）满足排除性限制——它仅通过影响道路距离（从而影响监督难度）来影响人均税收和县密度，不通过其他渠道。这是合理的但并非不可质疑，因为到首都的直线距离可能与其他因素相关，如经济地理、与外部威胁的距离、或历史上的定居模式。（b）基于距离的度量充分捕捉了监督难度，实际上这与其他相关因素混合在一起。（c）1820年的横截面数据反映的是长期均衡模式而非短期冲击。（d）关系不是由反向因果驱动的（例如，国家在已有更多行政存在的地方征收更多税收，而非反过来）。

时间序列重建。 对于命题3，作者逐年重建了1650至1850年的清朝税收。重建工作既利用了《清实录》的全国性汇总数据，也利用了其他官方来源中更可靠的省级分项数据。实际收入序列通过米价平减获得；人均收入使用人口估计值计算。得到的图表（图3，第120页）呈现明显的驼峰形模式：名义收入稳步上升，但实际收入约在1700年代初达到峰值后下降，而人均收入下降更为剧烈——从1685年足以养活9.6%人口降至1848年仅2.3%。这是描述性而非因果性的，但这一模式引人注目且与命题3直接一致。

历史叙事。 最后的经验部分使用定性历史证据追踪财政衰退的后果。包括分析军队部署记录、皇帝诏书中显示皇帝对军事弱点的认识（乾隆帝 Qianlong /tʃjɛnˈlʊŋ/ 1793年马戛尔尼 Macartney /məˈkɑːrtni/ 使团后的指令、道光帝 Daoguang /daʊˈɡwɑːŋ/ 1834年的感叹）、县制的停滞、以及主要叛乱发生在治理不足的边疆地区的地理起源（第119至123页）。历史叙事未经正式检验，但作为过程追踪证据，将财政衰退与具体政策后果和最终国家失败联系起来。

en: The paper makes several significant contributions that would be recognized by the scholarly community:

First, it offers a new theoretical framework that reconciles seemingly contradictory features of the late imperial Chinese state—absolutist yet weak, low-taxing yet exploitative—within a single, parsimonious model. By applying the principal-agent framework to the Chinese case, Sng provides a rational-choice explanation for the fiscal weakness of a unified empire that does not require assumptions about cultural stagnation, ideological rigidity, or irrational despotism (p. 107). This is a significant contribution to the comparative state formation literature, which has long been dominated by the European experience (Tilly, 1990; Dincecco, 2011).

Second, the paper’s most novel theoretical contribution is Proposition 3—the dynamic prediction that economic growth can worsen fiscal capacity when agency costs are severe. This “paradox of development” is a genuinely new insight with implications beyond the Chinese case: it suggests that any large, centralized polity with weak monitoring capacity may face an inherent tension between economic prosperity and state capacity. This connects to Ian Morris /ˈɪən ˈmɒrɪs/’s broader observation that “social development creates the very forces that undermine it” (p. 119).

Third, the paper contributes original data: the reconstruction of the Qing state’s annual tax revenue from 1650 to 1850, drawing on both published and previously underutilized archival sources. This time series is a valuable empirical contribution in its own right, documenting a striking hump-shaped fiscal pattern that had not been systematically demonstrated before (pp. 117–120, Fig. 3).

Fourth, the cross-sectional analysis provides new descriptive patterns: the finding that both tax per capita and administrative density decline significantly with distance from Beijing, even after controlling for population density, elevation, and other factors. This spatial gradient is a direct empirical imprint of the principal-agent problem and adds to the burgeoning literature on the spatial dimensions of state capacity (Stasavage /stəˈsɑːvɪdʒ/, 2010; Olsson /ˈɒlsən/ and Hansson /ˈhænsən/, 2011).

Fifth, the paper contributes to the “Great Divergence” debate by providing a structural, institutional explanation for China’s nineteenth-century decline that does not rely on cultural essentialism. Rather than blaming Confucian conservatism or Chinese cultural attitudes, Sng argues that it was structural, fiscal, and administrative weaknesses—consequences of governing a vast territory with imperfect monitoring—that left China unable to respond to the rise of the West. “It was structural, fiscal, and administrative weaknesses, not ignorance and hubris, that resulted in China’s inability to respond more resolutely to the rise of the West” (p. 121).
zh: 本文做出了几项将获得学术界认可的重要贡献：
首先，它提供了一个新的理论框架，在一个简约的模型中调和了晚期中华帝国国家看似矛盾的特征——专制却虚弱、低税却剥削性。通过将委托—代理框架应用于中国案例，孙端辉为统一帝国的财政虚弱提供了一个理性选择解释，无需假设文化停滞、意识形态僵化或非理性专制（第107页）。这是对比较国家形成文献的重要贡献，该文献长期以欧洲经验为主导（蒂利，1990；丁切科，2011）。
其次，本文最新颖的理论贡献是命题3——当代理成本严重时，经济增长可能恶化财政能力的动态预测。这一“发展悖论”是一个真正的新洞见，其含义超越了中国案例：它暗示任何大型、中央集权、监督能力弱的政体都可能面临经济繁荣与国家能力之间的内在张力。这与伊恩·莫里斯（Ian Morris /ˈɪən ˈmɒrɪs/）更广泛的观察相呼应：“社会发展创造了恰恰颠覆它的力量”（第119页）。
第三，本文贡献了原创数据：重建了1650至1850年清朝国家的年度税收，利用了已出版和此前未充分利用的档案来源。这一时间序列本身就是有价值的经验贡献，记录了一个此前未被系统展示的引人注目的驼峰形财政模式（第117至120页，图3）。
第四，横截面分析提供了新的描述性模式：即使在控制了人口密度、海拔和其他因素后，人均税收和行政密度都随到北京的距离增加而显著下降。这一空间梯度是委托—代理问题的直接经验印记，丰富了关于国家能力空间维度的新兴文献（斯塔萨维奇 Stasavage /stəˈsɑːvɪdʒ/，2010；奥尔森 Olsson /ˈɒlsən/ 和汉森 Hansson /ˈhænsən/，2011）。
第五，本文为**“大分流”辩论**做出了贡献，提供了一个关于中国十九世纪衰落的结构性、制度性解释，不依赖文化本质主义。孙端辉没有归咎于儒家保守主义或中国的文化态度，而是论证正是结构性的、财政的和行政的弱点——治理广袤疆域中监督不完善的后果——使中国无力应对西方的崛起。“正是结构性的、财政的和行政的弱点，而非无知和傲慢，导致了中国无法更果断地回应西方的崛起”（第121页）。

en: Professional scholars and researchers would likely identify the following limitations:

1. The model is highly stylized and abstracts away from important features. The model has only one agent per district, no interaction between agents, no heterogeneity among agents (some were honest, others corrupt), and no role for informal institutions, kinship networks, or cultural norms that shaped governance in practice. The representative agent conflates the magistrate, clerks, runners, and local gentry into a single decision-maker, which obscures the complex and sometimes conflicting incentive structures within each county (pp. 111, footnotes 15–16). A richer model might allow for heterogeneity among agents and incorporate the gentry’s dual role as both taxpayers protected from abuse and participants in the exploitation of small farmers (p. 111).

2. The cross-sectional analysis does not identify causality. The author explicitly acknowledges this limitation (p. 115), but it remains a significant one. The negative correlation between distance from Beijing and tax per capita / county density could be driven by many factors other than monitoring costs—for instance, the historical pattern of Chinese state formation, which expanded outward from the North China Plain; differences in agricultural productivity, land tenure systems, or commercial development between north and south; or the strategic importance of regions near the capital for defense. The IV strategy (using straight-line distance as an instrument for road distance) addresses endogeneity of the road network but does not solve the broader exclusion restriction concern: straight-line distance itself may affect outcomes through channels other than monitoring, such as proximity to trade networks, exposure to frontier conflicts, or cultural distance from the center.

3. The time-series evidence is descriptive, not causal. The hump-shaped pattern of real tax revenue is striking and consistent with Proposition 3, but the paper does not rule out alternative explanations for the fiscal decline. For example, the decline could be driven by the specific policy of freezing the head tax in 1712 and the decision in the 1730s to stop incentivizing the reporting of newly reclaimed lands—both of which the author discusses (p. 119) but treats as secondary factors rather than competing explanations. It could also be driven by the long-term silver-copper exchange rate fluctuation, the drying up of silver inflows from the Americas and Japan, or the increasing sophistication of tax avoidance by the gentry. The model predicts a hump shape, but many other models could also predict a hump shape for different reasons.

4. The absence of direct corruption data. The paper’s central mechanism is agent extortion, but there is no direct measurement of corruption at the prefectural level. The author openly acknowledges this gap: “Due to a lack of data on corruption, I am unable to empirically investigate the relationship between distance and agency costs directly” (p. 114). The spatial gradient in taxes and counties is an indirect test—consistent with the hypothesis but also consistent with alternative explanations. Future work could potentially use historical records of impeachments, criminal cases against officials, or reports from the Censorate to construct proxies for corruption.

5. The model takes political unification as given. The paper explicitly takes the unified, centralized Chinese empire as a point of departure (p. 109). But political unification is itself an outcome that requires explanation—why was China unified in the first place, and why did it remain so? If size causes fiscal weakness, wouldn’t the logical prediction be that the empire should fragment? This is the question addressed in the companion papers by Ko /koʊ/ and Sng (2013) and Ko, Koyama /kɔɪˈjɑːmə/, and Sng (2014), as well as Fernández-Villaverde et al. (2023)—the “Fractured-Land Hypothesis” paper we read previously—which argues that China’s flat, interconnected geography created incentives for unification despite the governance costs. But within the scope of this paper, the exogeneity of unification is an important limitation.

6. Limited consideration of the benefits of large size. The paper focuses exclusively on the costs of governing a large territory but acknowledges that political unification also brought significant benefits—above all the peace dividend, as Rosenthal /ˈroʊzənθɔːl/ and Wong /wɒŋ/ (2011) highlight (p. 109). A more complete analysis would weigh the costs (fiscal weakness from agency problems) against the benefits (reduced warfare, larger markets, economies of scale in defense) to assess whether the Chinese empire’s size was, on net, a help or a hindrance to development. Without this, the paper risks overstating the negative effects of size.
zh: 专业学者和研究者可能会指出以下局限：

1. 模型高度简化，抽象掉了重要特征。 模型每个辖区只有一个代理人，代理人之间没有互动，代理人之间没有异质性（有些是廉洁的，有些是腐败的），而且没有为非正式制度、亲属网络或文化规范在实践治理中的角色留出位置。代表性代理人将知县、胥吏、衙役和地方绅士混合为一个单一决策者，这模糊了每个县内部复杂且有时相互冲突的激励结构（第111页，注释15至16）。一个更丰富的模型可以允许代理人异质性，并纳入绅士的双重角色——既是受保护免遭剥削的纳税人，又是小农剥削的参与者（第111页）。

2. 横截面分析没有识别因果关系。 作者明确承认了这一局限（第115页），但它仍然是一个重要的问题。到北京的距离与人均税收/县密度之间的负相关可能由监督成本以外的许多因素驱动——例如，中国国家形成从华北平原向外扩展的历史模式；南北之间农业生产力、土地保有制度或商业发展的差异；或靠近首都地区在防御方面的战略重要性。IV策略（使用直线距离作为道路距离的工具变量）解决了道路网络的内生性，但没有解决更广泛的排除性限制问题：直线距离本身可能通过监督以外的渠道影响结果，如与贸易网络的距离、边境冲突的暴露程度、或与中心的文化距离。

3. 时间序列证据是描述性的，不是因果性的。 实际税收的驼峰形模式引人注目且与命题3一致，但本文没有排除财政衰退的替代解释。例如，衰退可能是由1712年冻结人头税的具体政策和1730年代停止激励报告新垦土地的决定驱动的——作者讨论了这两者（第119页），但将其视为次要因素而非竞争性解释。衰退也可能是由长期银铜汇率波动、来自美洲和日本的白银流入枯竭、或绅士阶层日益精巧的避税手段驱动的。模型预测了驼峰形，但许多其他模型也可能因不同原因预测驼峰形。
4. 缺乏直接的腐败数据。 本文的核心机制是代理人勒索，但没有府级层面的腐败直接度量。作者坦然承认这一缺口：“由于缺乏腐败数据，我无法直接经验性地调查距离与代理成本之间的关系”（第114页）。税收和县数的空间梯度是间接检验——与假说一致，但也与替代解释一致。未来的研究可能利用弹劾历史记录、针对官员的刑事案件或都察院报告来构建腐败代理变量。

5. 模型将政治统一视为给定。 本文明确以统一的、中央集权的中华帝国为出发点（第109页）。但政治统一本身是一个需要解释的结果——中国为何一开始就统一了？为何保持统一？如果规模导致财政虚弱，逻辑预测难道不是帝国应该分裂吗？这正是柯（Ko /koʊ/）与孙端辉（2013）以及柯、小山（Koyama /kɔɪˈjɑːmə/）与孙端辉（2014）的配套论文所探讨的问题，还有我们之前读过的费尔南德斯-比利亚韦尔德等人（2023）的“碎裂土地假说”论文——该论文认为中国平坦、互联的地理创造了统一的激励，尽管有治理成本。但在本文范围内，统一的外生性是一个重要局限。

6. 对大规模的收益考虑不足。 本文专注于治理大领土的成本，但承认政治统一也带来了显著收益——首先是和平红利，正如罗森塔尔（Rosenthal /ˈroʊzənθɔːl/）和王国斌（Wong /wɒŋ/，2011）所强调的（第109页）。一个更完整的分析会权衡成本（代理问题导致的财政虚弱）与收益（减少的战争、更大的市场、防御的规模经济），以评估中国帝国的规模对发展而言究竟是净收益还是净损失。缺少这一点，本文有夸大规模负面效应的风险。

en: Professional scholars and researchers might propose the following extensions:

1. Comparative analysis with other large empires. The principal-agent framework should apply not only to China but to any large, centralized state. A natural extension would compare the Chinese experience with other large premodern empires—the Ottoman /ˈɒtəmən/ Empire, the Mughal /ˈmuːɡəl/ Empire in India, the Russian Empire—to examine whether similar distance gradients in taxation and administration are observed. If the theory is correct, all large centralized empires should exhibit fiscal weakness relative to their size and a negative distance gradient from the capital. Differences in outcomes could be attributed to variation in monitoring technologies, institutional innovations, or decentralization strategies.

2. The role of information technology. The model treats monitoring capacity m as fixed. An extension could endogenize m by modeling how improvements in communication technology—the telegraph, the railroad, modern bureaucratic record-keeping—altered the principal-agent problem. This would connect to the broader literature on how information revolutions transform state capacity. For instance, did the introduction of the telegraph in late nineteenth-century China measurably improve the central government’s monitoring capacity and fiscal performance?

3. Within-dynasty variation in monitoring strategies. Different Qing emperors experimented with different monitoring institutions—Emperor Yongzheng /jʊŋˈdʒʌŋ/’s fiscal reforms in the 1720s, the palace memorial system, and the use of confidential agents. A within-dynasty panel study examining how these institutional innovations affected corruption, fiscal performance, and the distance gradient could provide more compelling evidence for the causal mechanisms.

4. Modern applications: fiscal federalism in contemporary China. The principal-agent problem did not disappear with the Qing dynasty. Modern China’s central-local fiscal relations—the fiscal contracting system of the 1980s, the 1994 tax-sharing reform—can be analyzed using the same framework. An extension could examine whether the distance gradient persists in modern China and how decentralization and modern monitoring technologies have altered the dynamics.

5. Interaction between external threats and internal agency problems. Sng notes that China’s peaceful external environment in the eighteenth century reduced the pressure to build fiscal capacity (p. 119). An extension inspired by the Tilly /ˈtɪli/ tradition could model the interaction between external military threats and internal agency costs. When external threats are high, the ruler faces a cruel dilemma: he needs more revenue to defend the empire, but raising taxes risks triggering the revolt he fears. This might explain why the Qing dynasty’s most dangerous moments came when external and internal threats coincided—as in the mid-nineteenth century when the Opium Wars and the Taiping Rebellion struck simultaneously.

6. Connecting to the decentralization literature. The paper’s conclusion briefly discusses how decentralization after 1850 mitigated the principal-agent problem but created free-rider problems in national-level public goods provision (p. 123). This opens a rich avenue for further research on the optimal degree of centralization in large states—a question that remains highly relevant for contemporary China, India, and other large developing countries. The tradeoff between centralized monitoring and decentralized fiscal autonomy could be modeled more formally and tested against the post-1850 Chinese experience.
zh: 专业学者和研究者可能提出以下扩展方向：

1. 与其他大帝国的比较分析。 委托—代理框架不仅应适用于中国，还应适用于任何大型中央集权国家。一个自然的扩展是将中国经验与其他大型前现代帝国——奥斯曼帝国（Ottoman /ˈɒtəmən/）、印度的莫卧儿帝国（Mughal /ˈmuːɡəl/）、俄罗斯帝国——进行比较，考察是否观察到类似的税收和行政距离梯度。如果理论正确，所有大型中央集权帝国都应表现出相对于其规模的财政虚弱和从首都出发的负向距离梯度。结果差异可归因于监督技术、制度创新或分权策略的变异。
2. 信息技术的角色。 模型将监督能力m视为固定。一个扩展可以通过建模通信技术改进——电报、铁路、现代官僚记录保持——如何改变委托—代理问题来内生化m。这将与关于信息革命如何转变国家能力的更广泛文献相联系。例如，十九世纪晚期中国引入电报是否可测量地改善了中央政府的监督能力和财政表现？
3. 朝代内部监督策略的变异。 不同的清朝皇帝尝试了不同的监督制度——雍正帝（Yongzheng /jʊŋˈdʒʌŋ/）1720年代的财政改革、奏折制度和密探的使用。一项朝代内部的面板研究，考察这些制度创新如何影响了腐败、财政表现和距离梯度，可以为因果机制提供更有说服力的证据。
4. 现代应用：当代中国的财政联邦主义。 委托—代理问题并未随清朝消失。现代中国的中央—地方财政关系——1980年代的财政包干制、1994年的分税制改革——可以使用同一框架分析。一个扩展可以考察距离梯度是否在现代中国持续存在，以及分权和现代监督技术如何改变了动态。

5. 外部威胁与内部代理问题的交互。 孙端辉指出十八世纪中国和平的外部环境降低了建设财政能力的压力（第119页）。一个受蒂利（Tilly /ˈtɪli/）传统启发的扩展可以建模外部军事威胁与内部代理成本之间的交互。当外部威胁高时，统治者面临残酷的两难：他需要更多收入来保卫帝国，但加税有引发他所恐惧的叛乱的风险。这可能解释了为何清朝最危险的时刻出现在外部和内部威胁同时出现之时——如十九世纪中期鸦片战争与太平天国运动同时爆发。
6. 与分权文献的联系。 本文结论部分简要讨论了1850年后的分权如何缓解了委托—代理问题但在全国性公共物品供给中制造了搭便车问题（第123页）。这为进一步研究大国中最优集权程度开辟了丰富的途径——这一问题对当代中国、印度和其他大型发展中国家仍然高度相关。集中监督与分权财政自主之间的权衡可以更正式地建模，并对照1850年后的中国经验进行检验。

en: The Decline and Rise of Democracy: A Global History from Antiquity to Today, Chapters 3 (“Weak States Inherited Democracy”) and 4 (“When Technology Undermined Democracy”).
zh: 《民主的衰落与崛起：从古代到今天的全球史》，第三章（“弱国家继承了民主”）和第四章（“当技术削弱了民主”）。

en: David Stasavage /ˈstæsəvɪdʒ/.
zh: 大卫·斯塔萨维奇 /ˈstæsəvɪdʒ/。

en: 2020, Princeton University Press.
zh: 2020年，普林斯顿大学出版社。

en: The main dependent variable across both chapters is the form of governance a society adopts, specifically whether it practices “early democracy” (council or assembly governance) or “autocracy” (rule without a council). Stasavage operationalizes this primarily as a binary distinction: whether a society has council governance or not. In the Standard Cross Cultural Sample (SCCS) data, this is measured by whether rulers govern with the assistance of a council or assembly versus governing alone (p. 66). In the Western North American Indian (WNAI) data compiled by Jorgensen /ˈjɔːrɡənsən/, this is similarly measured as the presence or absence of council governance (p. 68). The dependent variable is not modern liberal democracy with elections and constitutions, but rather the much older and more widespread practice of collective governance through councils—what the author terms “early democracy.” A secondary dependent variable in Chapter 3 is the presence of bureaucracy (defined as subordinates chosen by the ruler who are not from the ruler’s own family), which is treated as a potential substitute for council governance (p. 74).
zh: 两章的主要因变量是一个社会采用的治理形式，具体而言是该社会实行“早期民主”（议会或大会治理）还是“专制”（没有议会的统治）。斯塔萨维奇将其主要操作化为一个二元区分：一个社会是否存在议会治理。在标准跨文化样本（SCCS）数据中，这通过统治者是在议会或大会的协助下治理还是独自治理来衡量（第66页）。在约根森 /ˈjɔːrɡənsən/ 编纂的西部北美印第安人（WNAI）数据中，同样以议会治理的有无来衡量（第68页）。因变量不是带有选举和宪法的现代自由民主，而是更古老、更广泛存在的通过议会进行集体治理的实践——作者称之为“早期民主”。第三章的一个次要因变量是官僚制的存在（定义为由统治者选择的、不属于统治者自己家族的下属），它被视为议会治理的潜在替代品（第74页）。

en: The main independent variable is state weakness, understood as the ruler’s inability to monitor, tax, and control the population. This concept of state weakness is decomposed into three specific dimensions across the two chapters. First, in Chapter 3, agricultural variability (caloric variability)—the degree to which agricultural potential varies from location to location within a society’s territory. This is measured as the standard deviation of caloric potential across localized grid cells using data from Galor /ɡəˈlɔːr/ and Özak /ˈøzɑːk/ (p. 67). Second, also in Chapter 3, exit options for the population—how easy it is for people to leave if dissatisfied. This is proxied by population density (lower density means easier exit) and by qualitative geographic features such as whether a society occupies a narrow river valley versus open land (pp. 69–72). Third, the ruler’s dependence on the population for military service, proxied by the prestige accorded to warriors in the SCCS (p. 73). In Chapter 4, the independent variable shifts to technology—specifically, technologies of soil mapping, geometry and surveying, agricultural intensification, and writing. These technologies are argued to strengthen the bureaucratic alternative and thereby undermine early democracy (pp. 79–97). Europe’s technological backwardness relative to China and the Middle East is presented as a key factor explaining why early democracy persisted longer in Europe.
zh: 主要自变量是国家的弱势，理解为统治者无法监控、征税和控制人口的能力不足。这一国家弱势概念在两章中被分解为三个具体维度。第一，在第三章中，农业变异性（热量变异性）——一个社会领土内农业潜力从一个地点到另一个地点的变化程度。这通过使用加洛尔 /ɡəˈlɔːr/ 和厄扎克 /ˈøzɑːk/ 的数据，计算局部网格单元间热量潜力的标准差来衡量（第67页）。第二，同在第三章中，人口的退出选项——人们在不满时离开的难易程度。这以人口密度作为代理变量（密度越低意味着退出越容易），以及定性地理特征，如一个社会是占据狭窄河谷还是开阔土地（第69-72页）。第三，统治者对人口军事服务的依赖程度，以SCCS中给予战士的声望来代理衡量（第73页）。在第四章中，自变量转向技术——具体而言，是土壤测绘、几何与测量、农业集约化以及文字等技术。这些技术被认为强化了官僚制替代方案，从而削弱了早期民主（第79-97页）。欧洲相对于中国和中东的技术落后被提出作为解释早期民主为何在欧洲持续更久的关键因素。

en: Stasavage follows Douglass North /nɔːrθ/ in defining the state as an entity that extracts revenue in exchange for protection (p. 62). Protection is broadly construed to include defense against external invasion, insurance against famine, and social protection. Revenue encompasses taxation in currency, taxation in kind, or any redistribution of economic surplus. Crucially, this definition does not require formal institutions: among the Kwakiutl /kwɑːˈkjuːtəl/ of northwest North America, chiefs appropriated up to half the catch from hunting and fishing, which counts as revenue extraction (p. 63). A “weak state” in Stasavage’s framework is one where the ruler faces two fundamental problems: uncertainty about production (not knowing how much people produce, hence not knowing how much to tax) and exit risk (the population’s ability to leave, protest, or refuse to pay) (pp. 63–64). State strength is measured in the SCCS by whether governance exists above the level of the local community and by the presence of bureaucrats—subordinates chosen by the ruler who are not family members (pp. 65–66, 74). The concept of a “strong state” is thus one that has resolved these information and enforcement problems through bureaucratic capacity, intensive agriculture that makes production legible, and technologies like writing and soil mapping that allow rulers to monitor subjects without consulting councils (Chapter 4). This conceptualization stands in direct dialogue with James Scott’s notion of “legibility” from Seeing Like a State and Against the Grain—the idea that states need to render society “readable” in order to tax and control it. Stasavage effectively argues that where legibility was low, democracy emerged as a functional substitute.
zh: 斯塔萨维奇遵循道格拉斯·诺斯 /nɔːrθ/ 的定义，将国家界定为以保护换取收入的实体（第62页）。保护被广义地理解为包括抵御外部入侵、防范饥荒的保险以及社会保护。收入涵盖货币征税、实物征税或任何经济剩余的再分配。关键的是，这个定义不要求正式制度：在北美西北海岸的夸夸嘉夸族 /kwɑːˈkjuːtəl/ 中，酋长从狩猎和捕鱼活动中侵占多达一半的收获，这也算收入汲取（第63页）。斯塔萨维奇框架中的“弱国家”是统治者面临两个根本问题的国家：生产的不确定性（不知道人们生产了多少，因此不知道该征多少税）和退出风险（人口离开、抗议或拒绝缴纳的能力）（第63-64页）。国家力量在SCCS中通过治理是否存在于地方社区层面之上，以及是否存在官僚——由统治者选择的非家族成员下属——来衡量（第65-66、74页）。因此，“强国家”的概念是通过官僚能力、使生产变得可读的集约化农业，以及文字和土壤测绘等技术使统治者无需咨询议会即可监控臣民来解决这些信息和执法问题的国家（第四章）。这一概念化与詹姆斯·斯科特 /skɒt/ 在《国家的视角》和《反谷》中的“可读性”概念直接对话——国家需要使社会变得“可读”以便征税和控制。斯塔萨维奇有效地论证了，在可读性低的地方，民主作为一种功能性替代品出现了。
