en: The core argument spanning both chapters can be stated plainly: early democracy was not a sign of political progress but a consequence of state weakness. When rulers could not observe what people were producing, when people could easily leave, and when rulers needed their population for military service, rulers had to share power through councils or assemblies. Conversely, when rulers had the bureaucratic and technological means to monitor production, fix populations in place, and enforce tax collection without consulting anyone, they governed autocratically. The specific logic is as follows.

Chapter 3 establishes three conditions favoring early democracy. (1) Uncertainty about agricultural production. Where caloric variability was high—meaning agricultural output varied unpredictably across locations—rulers could not know how much to tax without local information. A council provided a forum where local knowledge could be revealed, allowing for more effective taxation by consent rather than by guesswork (pp. 66–69). (2) Exit options for the population. Where people could easily move elsewhere—due to low population density or extensive (non-intensive) agriculture—rulers were in a precarious position. To avoid losing their tax base entirely, they shared power. Evidence from the Jorgensen data on western North American Indians shows that council governance peaked at moderate population densities and disappeared entirely at densities above 25 people per square mile (pp. 71–72). This inverted-U relationship reflects the logic that at very low densities councils are logistically hard to organize, while at high densities people cannot exit and rulers can govern without consent. (3) Military dependence. When prevailing military technology required mass mobilization, rulers granted political voice as compensation for military service—the concept of “military democracy” first articulated by Lewis Henry Morgan /ˈmɔːrɡən/ (p. 73). In the SCCS, societies that accorded great prestige to warriors were roughly twice as likely to have council governance (p. 73).

Chapter 3 also introduces the bureaucratic alternative. Rulers who had a bureaucracy—defined as non-family subordinates—could solve both the information and enforcement problems without a council. Figure 3.4 shows this substitution effect clearly: among societies without bureaucrats, higher caloric variability is strongly associated with council presence; among societies with bureaucrats, caloric variability has no relationship with council presence (pp. 74–75). Bureaucracy and councils can also be complements: if democracy develops first, people develop habits of collective action that allow them to constrain a ruler even after a bureaucracy is established (p. 76). Tax farming is introduced as a third alternative that also economizes on the ruler’s need for information (p. 64).

Chapter 4 extends the argument by examining how specific technologies strengthened the bureaucratic alternative and thereby undermined early democracy. Soil mapping in China (the “fish scale maps” or yulin tu dating from the Ming /mɪŋ/ dynasty, based on a tradition going back to Yu /juː/ the Great) and among the Aztecs /ˈæztɛks/ (soil glyphs recorded in the Códice de Santa María Asunción /koˈðiθe ðe ˈsanta maˈɾia asunˈθjon/) allowed rulers to set differential tax rates based on soil quality without consulting local populations (pp. 80–84). Geometry and the ability to survey land, as practiced in ancient Egypt /ˈiːdʒɪpt/ and Sumeria /suːˈmɪəriə/, gave rulers tools to measure land for taxation that medieval Europeans lacked (pp. 86–87). Agricultural intensification—such as irrigation, the heavy plow, and crop rotation—made production more predictable and populations more fixed, reducing both the information and exit problems. European agriculture was markedly less intensive than Chinese or Middle Eastern agriculture: crop yields per hectare in ancient Egypt, Sumeria, and Tang /tɑːŋ/ dynasty China were three to four times those of medieval England /ˈɪŋɡlənd/ (p. 76). Writing was perhaps the most fundamental technology: it underpinned bureaucratic record-keeping and communication. Like bureaucracy itself, writing shows an indirect effect on democracy: among societies without writing, higher caloric variability predicts council presence; among societies with writing, this relationship disappears (Figure 4.3, pp. 93–94). The chapter also discusses the complexity of writing systems: simpler alphabetic systems (derived from the Old Canaanite /ˈkeɪnənaɪt/ and Phoenician /fɪˈnɪʃən/ alphabets) may have had democratizing effects by lowering access costs, whereas complex systems like Chinese logograms, cuneiform, and the Inka /ˈɪŋkə/ khipu /ˈkiːpuː/ concentrated literacy in an administrative elite (pp. 94–96).

The overarching thesis is therefore paradoxical: technological and civilizational “progress” often undermined democracy. Europe’s relative technological backwardness—its failure to develop soil mapping, its primitive surveying, its less intensive agriculture, and its late adoption of many technologies—left European rulers in a weaker position relative to their societies, and this weakness gave democracy a chance (pp. 79, 97).
zh: 贯穿两章的核心论点可以直白地表述为：早期民主不是政治进步的标志，而是国家弱势的结果。当统治者无法观察人们的生产状况、人们可以轻易离开、且统治者需要人口提供军事服务时，统治者不得不通过议会或大会分享权力。相反，当统治者拥有官僚和技术手段来监控生产、固定人口并在不咨询任何人的情况下强制征税时，他们便实行专制统治。具体逻辑如下。

第三章确立了有利于早期民主的三个条件。（1）农业生产的不确定性。在热量变异性高的地方——意味着农业产出在不同地点之间不可预测地变化——统治者在没有地方信息的情况下无法知道该征多少税。议会提供了一个可以揭示地方知识的论坛，使得通过同意而非猜测来进行更有效的征税成为可能（第66-69页）。（2）人口的退出选项。在人们可以轻易迁移到其他地方的地方——由于人口密度低或粗放型（非集约型）农业——统治者处于不稳定的地位。为了避免完全失去税基，他们分享权力。约根森关于西部北美印第安人的数据表明，议会治理在中等人口密度时达到峰值，而在密度超过每平方英里25人时完全消失（第71-72页）。这种倒U型关系反映了这样的逻辑：在非常低的密度下，议会在后勤上难以组织，而在高密度下，人们无法退出，统治者可以不经同意即进行治理。（3）军事依赖。当流行的军事技术需要大规模动员时，统治者给予政治发言权作为军事服务的补偿——这就是刘易斯·亨利·摩尔根 /ˈmɔːrɡən/ 首先提出的“军事民主”概念（第73页）。在SCCS中，给予战士极高声望的社会拥有议会治理的可能性大约是其他社会的两倍（第73页）。

第三章还引入了官僚制替代方案。拥有官僚制的统治者——定义为非家族下属——可以在不需要议会的情况下解决信息和执法两个问题。图3.4清楚地展示了这种替代效应：在没有官僚的社会中，较高的热量变异性与议会的存在强相关；在有官僚的社会中，热量变异性与议会的存在没有关系（第74-75页）。官僚制和议会也可以是互补的：如果民主首先发展起来，人们会养成集体行动的习惯，使他们即使在官僚制建立后也能约束统治者（第76页）。税收包办（tax farming）被引入作为第三种同样节约统治者信息需求的替代方案（第64页）。

第四章通过考察特定技术如何强化官僚制替代方案从而削弱早期民主来扩展这一论证。中国的土壤测绘（“鱼鳞图”即yulin tu，始于明 /mɪŋ/ 朝，基于可追溯到大禹 /juː/ 的传统）和阿兹特克人 /ˈæztɛks/ 的土壤测绘（记录在《圣玛丽亚阿松森法典》/koˈðiθe ðe ˈsanta maˈɾia asunˈθjon/ 中的土壤字形符号）使统治者能够根据土壤质量设定差别税率，而无需咨询当地民众（第80-84页）。古代埃及 /ˈiːdʒɪpt/ 和苏美尔 /suːˈmɪəriə/ 所实践的几何学和土地测量能力，为统治者提供了中世纪欧洲人所缺乏的土地征税测量工具（第86-87页）。农业集约化——如灌溉、重犁和轮作——使生产更可预测，人口更固定，减少了信息和退出两个问题。欧洲农业的集约化程度明显低于中国或中东农业：古埃及、苏美尔和唐 /tɑːŋ/ 朝中国每公顷的作物产量是中世纪英格兰 /ˈɪŋɡlənd/ 的三到四倍（第76页）。文字可能是最基本的技术：它支撑了官僚制的记录保存和通信。与官僚制本身一样，文字对民主显示出间接效应：在没有文字的社会中，较高的热量变异性预测议会的存在；在有文字的社会中，这种关系消失了（图4.3，第93-94页）。本章还讨论了文字系统的复杂性：较简单的字母系统（源自古迦南 /ˈkeɪnənaɪt/ 和腓尼基 /fɪˈnɪʃən/ 字母表）可能通过降低准入成本产生了民主化效应，而复杂系统如中国的表意文字、楔形文字和印加 /ˈɪŋkə/ 的奇普 /ˈkiːpuː/（结绳记事）则将识字能力集中在行政精英手中（第94-96页）。
因此，总体论题具有悖论性：技术和文明的“进步”往往削弱了民主。欧洲相对的技术落后——未能发展土壤测绘、原始的测量技术、不太集约化的农业以及许多技术的晚期采用——使欧洲统治者相对于其社会处于更弱的地位，而这种弱势给了民主一个机会（第79、97页）。

en: Stasavage proposes four interlinked mechanisms connecting state weakness to early democracy.

Mechanism 1: The information mechanism. When agricultural production is highly variable across locations, a ruler cannot devise an optimal tax policy because he does not know how much each locality produces. Setting taxes too high risks provoking protest or flight; setting them too low leaves revenue on the table. A council solves this by creating a forum for truthful communication about local production conditions. This does not require altruistic motives—truthful communication is sustained by a commonality of interest (e.g., the shared need for defense) and by the mutual preference for peaceful resolution over conflict (p. 64). Council members possess “real authority” through superior information, even without formal veto power over the ruler’s proposals (p. 64). The empirical prediction is straightforward: higher caloric variability should correlate with council presence.

Mechanism 2: The exit mechanism. Drawing on Robert Carneiro’s /kɑːrˈneɪroʊ/ circumscription theory (1970) and Sandra Vehrencamp’s /ˈveɪrənkæmp/ ornithological model of hierarchy, Stasavage argues that when people can easily leave, rulers must govern by consent to retain their tax base (pp. 63, 69). This mechanism is illustrated by the contrast between Mississippian /ˌmɪsɪˈsɪpiən/ societies in southeastern North America—which occupied narrow, fertile river valleys surrounded by less suitable land (high circumscription, autocratic governance)—and Iroquois /ˈɪrəkwɔɪ/ and Huron /ˈhjʊərɒn/ societies in the northeast, which practiced mobile slash-and-burn agriculture and moved villages every ten to forty years (low circumscription, democratic governance) (pp. 70–71). The Jorgensen data show the inverted-U pattern: council governance peaks at moderate population density and vanishes at high density (pp. 71–72).

Mechanism 3: The military bargaining mechanism. When military technology requires mass participation, rulers must compensate soldiers with political voice. This is “military democracy” in Morgan’s sense. The empirical pattern in the SCCS—a strong correlation between warrior prestige and council governance—is best interpreted not as warrior prestige causing democracy, but as warrior prestige and council governance being complementary parts of a single bargain between rulers and fighters (p. 73).

Mechanism 4: The bureaucratic substitution mechanism (Chapters 3–4). Bureaucracy, intensive agriculture, soil mapping, surveying, and writing all reduce the ruler’s dependence on local knowledge. When bureaucrats can assess production and collect taxes, the ruler no longer needs a council for information. When intensive agriculture fixes populations in observable, measurable plots, exit becomes harder and production becomes legible. Figure 3.4 and Figure 4.3 provide the key evidence: the positive relationship between caloric variability and council governance exists only in the absence of bureaucracy (or writing); in the presence of bureaucracy (or writing), the relationship vanishes (pp. 75, 93–94). This is an interaction effect, and it is the most analytically rigorous claim in the two chapters.
zh: 斯塔萨维奇提出了四个相互关联的机制，将国家弱势与早期民主联系起来。

机制一：信息机制。当农业生产在不同地点之间高度变异时，统治者无法制定最优税收政策，因为他不知道每个地方的产出是多少。税收定得太高有可能引发抗议或逃离；定得太低则白白损失收入。议会通过创造一个关于当地生产条件进行真实沟通的论坛来解决这个问题。这不需要利他动机——真实沟通由利益的共同性（例如对防御的共同需求）和对和平解决而非冲突的共同偏好来维持（第64页）。议会成员通过其信息优势拥有“真实权威”，即使没有对统治者提案的正式否决权（第64页）。经验预测是直截了当的：较高的热量变异性应与议会的存在相关。

机制二：退出机制。借鉴罗伯特·卡内罗 /kɑːrˈneɪroʊ/ 的限制理论（1970年）和桑德拉·维伦坎普 /ˈveɪrənkæmp/ 的鸟类学等级模型，斯塔萨维奇认为，当人们可以轻易离开时，统治者必须通过同意来治理以保留其税基（第63、69页）。这一机制通过以下对比得到说明：北美东南部的密西西比 /ˌmɪsɪˈsɪpiən/ 社会——占据被不太适合其集约农业的土地包围的狭窄肥沃河谷（高限制性，专制治理）——与东北部的易洛魁 /ˈɪrəkwɔɪ/ 和休伦 /ˈhjʊərɒn/ 社会，后者实行流动的刀耕火种农业，每十到四十年迁移一次村庄（低限制性，民主治理）（第70-71页）。约根森数据显示了倒U型模式：议会治理在中等人口密度时达到峰值，在高密度时消失（第71-72页）。
机制三：军事讨价还价机制。当军事技术需要大规模参与时，统治者必须以政治发言权补偿士兵。这就是摩尔根意义上的“军事民主”。SCCS中的经验模式——战士声望与议会治理之间的强相关——最好的解释不是战士声望导致民主，而是战士声望和议会治理是统治者与战士之间单一讨价还价的互补部分（第73页）。

机制四：官僚制替代机制（第三、四章）。官僚制、集约化农业、土壤测绘、测量和文字都减少了统治者对地方知识的依赖。当官僚能够评估生产并征税时，统治者不再需要议会来获取信息。当集约化农业将人口固定在可观察、可测量的地块上时，退出变得更难，生产变得可读。图3.4和图4.3提供了关键证据：热量变异性与议会治理之间的正相关关系仅存在于没有官僚制（或文字）的情况下；在有官僚制（或文字）的情况下，这种关系消失了（第75、93-94页）。这是一个交互效应，是两章中分析上最严谨的论断。

en: The two chapters draw on several distinct data sources, spanning different geographic regions, time periods, and units of analysis.

(1) The Standard Cross Cultural Sample (SCCS). This is a set of 186 well-documented societies from around the world, selected by George Peter Murdock /ˈmɜːrdɒk/ and Douglas White to be independent of each other. Each society is coded at a specific “ethnographic present,” typically the point of earliest reliable description. The SCCS provides data on: presence or absence of council governance (DV), whether governance extends beyond the local community, presence of bureaucrats (non-family subordinates chosen by the ruler), prestige accorded to warriors, and whether a system of writing exists (pp. 65–66, 73–74, 92–94). Geographic coverage is global; temporal coverage varies by society (ranging from several centuries BCE to the twentieth century). The unit of analysis is the individual society.

(2) Caloric potential and caloric variability data from Galor and Özak. These are constructed from the FAO’s Global Agro-Ecological Zones database, which records the maximum potential crop production for grid cells covering the globe at a resolution of approximately 10 km × 10 km, assuming no irrigation and no inputs added to the soil. Caloric potential measures the maximum calories producible from a given plot. Caloric variability is the standard deviation of caloric potential across a localized area of nine grid cells (pp. 65, 67). The Columbian Exchange is used to construct a temporal variability measure: the change in how many calories could be produced before versus after the exchange of crops between the New World and Old World (pp. 68–69).

(3) The Western North American Indian (WNAI) data from Joseph Jorgensen /ˈjɔːrɡənsən/. This covers 172 Native American groups located west of the Great Plains, essentially all groups in that region. It records council governance, population density, and various social and political features (pp. 68, 71–72). The unit of analysis is the individual Native American group. This data set is used as an independent check on the SCCS findings.

(4) Qualitative historical and archaeological evidence. For the Mississippian, Iroquois, and Huron societies, Stasavage draws on archaeological records and early European contact accounts (Chapter 3, pp. 70–71). For Chinese soil mapping, he uses the Yu Gong /juː ɡʊŋ/ (Tribute of Yu) text and Ming dynasty fish scale maps (pp. 80–83). For Aztec soil glyphs, he uses the Códice de Santa María Asunción (pp. 83–84). For European agricultural techniques, the Domesday Book, and European reconquest of Arab-controlled lands, he draws on a wide range of secondary historical sources (pp. 85–90). Crop yield data for ancient Egypt, Sumeria, Tang dynasty China, and medieval England come from agricultural history (pp. 76–77).

(5) Data on the origins and spread of writing. Stasavage uses the SCCS to code whether societies had writing, and uses distance from the three independently invented writing systems (Sumerian cuneiform, Chinese, and Olmec /ˈɒlmɛk/) as a predictor of whether a society adopted writing (pp. 91–93). The association between tuber-based versus cereal-based economies and the presence of writing is also tested using FAO suitability data (pp. 91–92).
zh: 两章利用了几个不同的数据来源，涵盖不同的地理区域、时间段和分析单位。

（1）标准跨文化样本（SCCS）。这是由乔治·彼得·默多克 /ˈmɜːrdɒk/ 和道格拉斯·怀特选择的来自世界各地的186个有良好文献记录的社会，被选择为相互独立。每个社会在特定的“民族志现在”（通常是最早可靠描述的时间点）进行编码。SCCS提供以下数据：议会治理的有无（因变量）、治理是否延伸到地方社区之外、官僚的存在（统治者选择的非家族下属）、给予战士的声望，以及是否存在文字系统（第65-66、73-74、92-94页）。地理覆盖是全球性的；时间覆盖因社会而异（从公元前几个世纪到二十世纪不等）。分析单位是单个社会。

（2）来自加洛尔和厄扎克的热量潜力和热量变异性数据。这些数据由FAO的全球农业生态区数据库构建，记录了覆盖全球的、分辨率约为10公里×10公里的网格单元的最大潜在作物产量，假设无灌溉、无土壤投入。热量潜力衡量给定地块可产生的最大卡路里。热量变异性是九个网格单元组成的局部区域内热量潜力的标准差（第65、67页）。哥伦布大交换被用于构建时间变异性指标：新旧世界之间作物交换前后可产出热量的变化（第68-69页）。
（3）来自约瑟夫·约根森 /ˈjɔːrɡənsən/ 的西部北美印第安人（WNAI）数据。这涵盖了位于大平原以西的172个美洲原住民群体，基本上是该地区的所有群体。它记录了议会治理、人口密度和各种社会与政治特征（第68、71-72页）。分析单位是单个美洲原住民群体。该数据集被用作对SCCS发现的独立检验。

（4）定性的历史和考古证据。对于密西西比、易洛魁和休伦社会，斯塔萨维奇利用考古记录和早期欧洲接触记述（第三章，第70-71页）。对于中国的土壤测绘，他使用《禹贡》/juː ɡʊŋ/（禹的贡赋）文本和明朝鱼鳞图（第80-83页）。对于阿兹特克土壤字形，他使用《圣玛丽亚阿松森法典》（第83-84页）。对于欧洲农业技术、末日审判书和欧洲对阿拉伯控制土地的再征服，他利用了广泛的二手历史资料（第85-90页）。古埃及、苏美尔、唐朝中国和中世纪英格兰的作物产量数据来自农业史研究（第76-77页）。
（5）关于文字起源和传播的数据。斯塔萨维奇使用SCCS来编码社会是否拥有文字，并使用与三个独立发明的文字系统（苏美尔楔形文字、中文和奥尔梅克 /ˈɒlmɛk/ 文字）的距离作为社会是否采用文字的预测因子（第91-93页）。基于块茎经济与谷物经济之间的关联以及文字的存在，也使用FAO适宜性数据进行了检验（第91-92页）。

en: The methodology is primarily comparative-descriptive and correlational, supplemented with historical case studies and limited attempts at causal identification. The specific methods include:

(1) Cross-tabulations and box-and-whisker plots. The main empirical results are presented as bivariate or conditional comparisons. For example, Figure 3.1 compares the distribution of caloric variability in societies with and without councils. Figure 3.2 plots the share of societies with councils against population density categories. Figure 3.3 shows the fraction of societies with council governance by the level of warrior prestige. Figures 3.4 and 4.3 present the key interaction: the caloric variability–council relationship is shown separately for societies with and without bureaucrats (or writing). These are descriptive, not based on regression analysis with controls.

(2) Qualitative historical comparisons. The contrast between Mississippian and Northeastern Woodland societies, the comparison of Chinese versus European soil knowledge, the discussion of the European reconquest of Arab lands in Sicily /ˈsɪsɪli/ and Iberia /aɪˈbɪəriə/—all of these are comparative case analyses designed to illustrate the mechanisms. They follow a “most similar systems” logic in some cases (e.g., Huron and Mississippians both grew maize but differed in agricultural intensity and political form).

(3) Robustness checks using independent samples. The WNAI data on Native Americans is used as an out-of-sample check on the SCCS finding that caloric variability predicts council governance (p. 68). The Columbian Exchange is used to test whether temporal (not just spatial) caloric variability also predicts councils (pp. 68–69).

(4) Interaction analysis. The comparison of the caloric variability–council relationship in the presence versus absence of bureaucracy (Figure 3.4) or writing (Figure 4.3) is an informal interaction test. This is the closest the chapters come to a “mechanism test”—if bureaucracy substitutes for councils in solving information problems, then the caloric variability–council relationship should disappear when bureaucracy is present, which it does.

Key assumptions for these methods to be valid include: (a) The SCCS and WNAI data accurately represent the political and economic characteristics of the societies they code—this requires trusting ethnographic observation. (b) Caloric potential and caloric variability, based on modern FAO data with no irrigation and no inputs, reasonably proxy the actual agricultural conditions these societies faced. This is plausible for societies observed before major human transformation of the landscape but less so for societies observed after centuries of agricultural modification. (c) The relationships are not driven by confounding variables. Since the analysis is primarily bivariate, unobserved factors (e.g., cultural diffusion, proximity to trade routes, kinship structures, religious institutions) could plausibly explain the patterns. Stasavage addresses some confounders (e.g., trade and risk-sharing as alternative explanations for the caloric variability–council relationship, p. 68) but does not use multivariate regression to control for all plausible confounds simultaneously. (d) For the interaction analysis, one must assume that the presence of bureaucracy is not itself endogenous to the same factors that drive council presence.
zh: 方法论主要是比较描述性和相关性的，辅以历史案例研究和有限的因果识别尝试。具体方法包括：
（1）交叉表和箱线图。主要经验结果以双变量或条件比较的形式呈现。例如，图3.1比较了有议会和无议会社会中热量变异性的分布。图3.2绘制了不同人口密度类别中拥有议会的社会比例。图3.3显示了按战士声望水平划分的拥有议会治理的社会比例。图3.4和图4.3呈现了关键的交互效应：热量变异性与议会的关系分别在有无官僚（或文字）的社会中展示。这些都是描述性的，不是基于带控制变量的回归分析。
（2）定性历史比较。密西西比社会与东北林地社会的对比、中国与欧洲土壤知识的比较、欧洲再征服西西里 /ˈsɪsɪli/ 和伊比利亚 /aɪˈbɪəriə/ 阿拉伯控制土地的讨论——所有这些都是旨在说明机制的比较案例分析。在某些情况下遵循“最相似系统”逻辑（例如，休伦人和密西西比人都种植玉米，但在农业集约化程度和政治形式上不同）。
（3）使用独立样本的稳健性检查。关于美洲原住民的WNAI数据被用作对SCCS发现（热量变异性预测议会治理）的样本外检验（第68页）。哥伦布大交换被用于检验时间上的（不仅是空间上的）热量变异性是否也预测议会（第68-69页）。
（4）交互分析。在有无官僚制（图3.4）或文字（图4.3）的情况下比较热量变异性与议会的关系，是一种非正式的交互检验。这是两章最接近“机制检验”的部分——如果官僚制在解决信息问题方面替代了议会，那么当官僚制存在时，热量变异性与议会的关系应该消失，事实确实如此。

这些方法有效的关键假设包括：（a）SCCS和WNAI数据准确地代表了它们所编码的社会的政治和经济特征——这要求信任民族志观察。（b）基于现代FAO数据（无灌溉、无投入）的热量潜力和热量变异性合理地代理了这些社会面临的实际农业条件。这对于在人类大规模改造景观之前被观察到的社会是合理的，但对于在几个世纪的农业改造之后被观察到的社会则不太合理。（c）这些关系不是由混杂变量驱动的。由于分析主要是双变量的，未观察到的因素（例如文化扩散、贸易路线的邻近性、亲属结构、宗教制度）可以合理地解释这些模式。斯塔萨维奇处理了一些混杂因素（例如贸易和风险分担作为热量变异性-议会关系的替代解释，第68页），但没有使用多元回归来同时控制所有合理的混杂因素。（d）对于交互分析，必须假设官僚制的存在本身不是由驱动议会存在的相同因素内生决定的。

en: The contribution of these two chapters is multi-dimensional.

First, they offer a new theory of early democracy’s origins that reverses the conventional narrative. Rather than seeing democracy as a product of economic development, urbanization, or cultural enlightenment, Stasavage argues that democracy arose from state weakness—from rulers’ inability to monitor and control. This directly challenges modernization theory (Lipset /ˈlɪpsɛt/) and connects to but substantially reframes the insights of Carneiro (circumscription), Olson (stationary bandit), and Scott (legibility). Where Carneiro explains state formation and Olson explains why autocrats provide public goods, Stasavage explains why some rulers were forced to share power with councils. His framework fills the gap we identified in our earlier discussions: Olson could not explain where “balanced power” comes from; Stasavage’s answer is that it comes from information asymmetry and exit options rooted in geography and technology.

Second, the chapters provide a genuinely global comparative framework. By using the SCCS (186 societies worldwide) and the WNAI (172 Native American groups), Stasavage avoids the Eurocentric bias that plagues much of the democracy literature. The argument applies to the Huron, the Aztecs, the Chinese, and medieval Europeans alike, with the same mechanisms operating across all cases. This global scope is unusual in a field dominated by European or Western-centric narratives.

Third, the interaction analysis between caloric variability, council presence, and bureaucracy/writing represents a novel empirical design for testing substitution mechanisms. The finding that the caloric variability–council relationship vanishes in the presence of bureaucracy or writing is a clean test of the idea that these institutions solve the same underlying problem (information asymmetry).

Fourth, Chapter 4’s argument that technological progress undermined democracy is a provocative contribution that challenges progressive teleologies. The specific comparative evidence—Europe’s technological backwardness in soil mapping, surveying, and agriculture relative to China and the Middle East—offers a compelling explanation for the “European divergence” in political institutions that complements existing explanations focused on geography (Jared Diamond /ˈdaɪmənd/), interstate competition (Philip Hoffman /ˈhɒfmən/), or commercial capitalism. This argument resonates with our earlier reading of Mayshar /ˈmeɪʃɑːr/, Moav /moˈɑːv/, Neeman, and Pascali /pɑːsˈkɑːli/ on land appropriability: where land is more “legible” and appropriable, states can extract without consent; where it is not, rulers must bargain.
zh: 这两章的贡献是多维度的。

第一，它们提出了一种关于早期民主起源的新理论，颠覆了传统叙事。斯塔萨维奇不是将民主视为经济发展、城市化或文化启蒙的产物，而是认为民主源于国家弱势——统治者无法监控和控制的无能。这直接挑战了现代化理论（李普塞特 /ˈlɪpsɛt/），并与卡内罗（限制理论）、奥尔森（坐寇）和斯科特（可读性）的洞见联系起来，但对其进行了实质性的重新框架。卡内罗解释国家形成，奥尔森解释为什么专制者提供公共物品，而斯塔萨维奇解释为什么一些统治者被迫与议会分享权力。他的框架填补了我们在早期讨论中发现的空白：奥尔森无法解释“平衡的权力”从何而来；斯塔萨维奇的回答是，它来自植根于地理和技术的信息不对称和退出选项。
第二，这些章节提供了一个真正的全球比较框架。通过使用SCCS（全球186个社会）和WNAI（172个美洲原住民群体），斯塔萨维奇避免了困扰民主文献的欧洲中心偏见。论证同样适用于休伦人、阿兹特克人、中国人和中世纪欧洲人，相同的机制在所有案例中运作。这种全球范围在一个被欧洲或西方中心叙事主导的领域中是不寻常的。
第三，热量变异性、议会存在和官僚制/文字之间的交互分析，代表了一种检验替代机制的新颖实证设计。热量变异性与议会的关系在官僚制或文字存在时消失的发现，是对这些制度解决同一基本问题（信息不对称）这一观点的清晰检验。

第四，第四章关于技术进步削弱民主的论点是一个具有挑衅性的贡献，挑战了进步目的论。具体的比较证据——欧洲在土壤测绘、测量和农业方面相对于中国和中东的技术落后——为政治制度中的“欧洲分岔”提供了令人信服的解释，补充了聚焦于地理（贾雷德·戴蒙德 /ˈdaɪmənd/）、国家间竞争（菲利普·霍夫曼 /ˈhɒfmən/）或商业资本主义的既有解释。这一论点与我们早前阅读的梅沙尔 /ˈmeɪʃɑːr/、莫阿夫 /moˈɑːv/、尼曼和帕斯卡利 /pɑːsˈkɑːli/ 关于土地可占用性的研究产生共鸣：在土地更“可读”和可占用的地方，国家可以不经同意就提取；在不可读的地方，统治者必须讨价还价。

en: Professional scholars would identify several significant limitations.

(1) The causal identification is weak. The core empirical results are bivariate correlations (caloric variability vs. council presence) presented in box-and-whisker plots without regression controls. This leaves the findings vulnerable to omitted variable bias. For instance, caloric variability may correlate with terrain ruggedness, which independently affects state capacity and political centralization (as in Nunn /nʌn/ and Puga /ˈpuːɡɑː/’s work on rugged terrain in Africa). Cultural diffusion could also confound the results: societies in the same geographic area may share both high caloric variability and a cultural tradition of council governance. To address this, scholars would recommend multivariate regression controlling for geographic region, terrain, distance from early civilizations, kinship structure, and other plausible confounders.

(2) Endogeneity of bureaucracy. The interaction analysis (Figures 3.4 and 4.3) treats the presence of bureaucracy and writing as if they were exogenous moderators. But bureaucracy may itself be endogenous: societies with less caloric variability (more predictable agriculture) may be precisely those that develop bureaucracies, making the interaction potentially tautological. Similarly, the adoption of writing may respond to the same state-building pressures that produce autocracy. Instrumental variables for bureaucracy or writing would strengthen the analysis.

(3) Measurement of “council governance.” The binary coding of council presence/absence in the SCCS is very coarse. It does not distinguish between councils that have real decision-making power and those that are merely advisory, between councils that represent broad segments of society and those dominated by elites, or between councils that meet regularly and those that are convened only in emergencies. The literature on early democracy (including the author’s own Chapter 2) makes clear that “councils” varied enormously in their composition and authority. A more granular measure—perhaps distinguishing between elite councils, open assemblies, and consensus-based decision-making—would add analytical power.

(4) The ethnographic present problem. The SCCS codes each society at a single point in time, typically the earliest reliable description. For many societies, this is the point of European contact, which may have already disrupted indigenous political institutions. The “ethnographic present” may therefore capture a post-contact equilibrium rather than the longstanding pre-contact political form. This is particularly problematic for Native American societies that may have been reorganized by the demographic collapse following European diseases.

(5) Selection into the SCCS. The 186 societies in the SCCS were selected to be independent of each other (i.e., to avoid spatial autocorrelation), but this selection may introduce bias if the criteria for inclusion are correlated with the outcomes of interest. Murdock and White’s selection criteria are not random, and the resulting sample may over- or under-represent certain types of societies.

(6) The European backwardness argument in Chapter 4 is suggestive but not systematically tested. The claim that Europe’s technological backwardness preserved democracy is supported by qualitative comparisons (China vs. Europe, the reconquest of Arab lands) but not by formal empirical analysis. A more rigorous test would measure technological sophistication across many societies and test whether it predicts the bureaucratic alternative, controlling for other factors.

(7) Reverse causality between technology and political form. Chapter 4 argues that technology undermined democracy, but the causal arrow could run the other direction: perhaps democratic societies resisted the adoption of technologies that would undermine their political form. The example of Europeans failing to adopt Arab agricultural innovations after the reconquest (pp. 89–90) could be read as precisely this kind of political resistance to technological change rather than mere backwardness. Scott’s “weapons of the weak” and “the art of not being governed” frameworks suggest that people deliberately avoid legibility-enhancing technologies.

(8) Limited attention to inequality and class structure. The framework focuses on the ruler–society relationship but says little about internal stratification within society. Societies with high inequality might develop councils that represent only elites, while more egalitarian societies might develop genuinely participatory assemblies. The SCCS coding does not capture this distinction, and the theory does not address it.
zh: 专业学者会指出几个重要局限。

（1）因果识别薄弱。核心经验结果是以箱线图呈现的双变量相关（热量变异性与议会存在），没有回归控制。这使发现容易受到遗漏变量偏差的影响。例如，热量变异性可能与地形崎岖度相关，后者独立影响国家能力和政治集中化（如努恩 /nʌn/ 和普加 /ˈpuːɡɑː/ 关于非洲崎岖地形的研究）。文化扩散也可能混淆结果：同一地理区域的社会可能同时具有高热量变异性和议会治理的文化传统。为解决这个问题，学者会建议进行多元回归，控制地理区域、地形、与早期文明的距离、亲属结构和其他合理的混杂因素。
（2）官僚制的内生性。交互分析（图3.4和4.3）将官僚制和文字的存在视为外生调节变量。但官僚制本身可能是内生的：热量变异性较低（农业更可预测）的社会可能恰好是发展出官僚制的社会，使得交互效应可能是同义反复。类似地，文字的采用可能回应与产生专制相同的国家建设压力。官僚制或文字的工具变量将加强分析。
（3）“议会治理”的测量。SCCS中议会存在/不存在的二元编码非常粗糙。它不区分有真正决策权的议会和仅具咨询性质的议会、代表社会广泛阶层的议会和精英主导的议会、定期召开的议会和仅在紧急情况下召集的议会。关于早期民主的文献（包括作者自己的第二章）明确表明“议会”在其组成和权威方面存在巨大差异。更精细的衡量——也许区分精英议会、开放大会和基于共识的决策——将增加分析力度。
（4）民族志现在问题。SCCS在单一时间点（通常是最早可靠描述）对每个社会进行编码。对于许多社会，这是欧洲接触的时间点，可能已经扰乱了本土政治制度。因此，“民族志现在”可能捕捉的是接触后的均衡，而不是长期存在的接触前政治形式。这对可能因欧洲疾病带来的人口崩溃而重组的美洲原住民社会尤其成问题。
（5）SCCS的样本选择。SCCS中的186个社会被选择为相互独立（即避免空间自相关），但如果纳入标准与感兴趣的结果相关，这种选择可能引入偏差。默多克和怀特的选择标准不是随机的，由此产生的样本可能过度或不足地代表某些类型的社会。
（6）第四章中欧洲落后论的论证是启发性的，但未经系统检验。欧洲技术落后保护了民主的主张由定性比较（中国与欧洲、阿拉伯土地的再征服）支持，但没有正式的经验分析。更严格的检验将跨许多社会测量技术复杂程度，并在控制其他因素后检验其是否预测官僚制替代方案。
（7）技术与政治形式之间的反向因果。第四章认为技术削弱了民主，但因果箭头可能指向另一个方向：也许民主社会抵制了会削弱其政治形式的技术的采用。欧洲人在再征服后未能采用阿拉伯农业创新的例子（第89-90页）可以被解读为恰恰是这种对技术变革的政治抵制，而不仅仅是落后。斯科特的“弱者的武器”和“不被统治的艺术”框架表明，人们刻意避免增强可读性的技术。
（8）对不平等和阶级结构关注有限。该框架聚焦于统治者-社会关系，但对社会内部分层着墨甚少。高度不平等的社会可能发展出仅代表精英的议会，而更平等的社会可能发展出真正参与式的大会。SCCS编码没有捕捉这一区分，理论也未涉及。

en: Several promising research extensions emerge from these chapters.

(1) Formal modeling of the information–council bargain. Stasavage sketches the logic of why rulers with imperfect information about production would benefit from councils, but does not present a formal model. A game-theoretic model—building on the cheap talk or mechanism design literature—could specify the conditions under which council governance is a stable equilibrium, when it breaks down, and how it interacts with bureaucratic capacity. This would connect to the Dal Bó /dæl ˈboʊ/, Hernández-Lagos /ɛrˈnɑːndɛz ˈlɑːɡoʊs/, and Mazzuca /mɑːˈzuːkɑː/ approach of using formal theory to derive comparative statics about state formation.

(2) Testing the framework with more rigorous causal identification. One could exploit natural experiments—such as volcanic eruptions or climate shocks that exogenously increase agricultural variability—to test whether increased uncertainty leads to the adoption of council governance. Similarly, the introduction of new crops through the Columbian Exchange (which Stasavage uses qualitatively) could be formalized as a quasi-experiment using difference-in-differences methods.

(3) The long-run consequences of early democracy. These chapters explain where early democracy came from but do not trace its long-run effects. Did societies with early democratic traditions develop different economic institutions, different levels of trust, or different norms of governance? This connects to Hariri /hæˈriːri/’s finding that early indigenous statehood is associated with modern autocracy—Stasavage’s framework implies a more nuanced prediction: early states with councils might have different long-run trajectories than early states without them.

(4) Sequencing of bureaucracy and democracy. Stasavage briefly suggests that the sequence matters: if democracy develops first, the population develops collective action habits that allow it to constrain even a bureaucratized ruler; if bureaucracy comes first, democracy may never emerge (p. 76). This sequencing hypothesis is testable and connects to a large literature on whether state capacity and democratization are complements or substitutes (Besley /ˈbɛzli/ and Persson /ˈpɜːrsən/, Tilly). A systematic test across many societies would be valuable.

(5) The role of trade and commerce. Stasavage briefly mentions that caloric variability might encourage trade (p. 68) but dismisses this as an alternative explanation. Yet in our earlier readings on Abramson and Boix /bɔɪks/’s work on European political fragmentation, commercial classes played a central role in sustaining independent political units. Integrating the commercial mechanism with the information mechanism could yield a richer theory: perhaps trade both increases the information problem (more complex economies are harder to tax) and creates a new constituency (merchants) with both the desire for political voice and the capacity for exit.

(6) Technology adoption as a political choice. Chapter 4 treats European technological backwardness as a given, but scholars like Daron Acemoglu /ˈæsɪmoʊɡluː/ and James Robinson have argued that technology adoption is itself shaped by political institutions. Perhaps European rulers with democratic constraints deliberately avoided adopting technologies that would have strengthened their hand—or perhaps elites in non-European societies actively promoted technologies that undermined councils. Disentangling this co-evolution would be a significant contribution.

(7) Application to modern contexts. Does the logic of “weak states inherit democracy” apply in modern contexts? Many developing countries have states that are “weak” in Stasavage’s sense—they cannot effectively monitor production or prevent exit. Do these countries tend to have more democratic institutions at the local level, even if national institutions are autocratic? This connects to work on decentralization, local governance, and “everyday democracy” in Africa and Asia.
zh: 这些章节产生了几个有前景的研究延伸。

（1）信息-议会讨价还价的正式建模。斯塔萨维奇勾勒了为什么拥有不完善生产信息的统治者会从议会中受益的逻辑，但没有提出正式模型。一个博弈论模型——建立在廉价交谈或机制设计文献之上——可以明确议会治理是稳定均衡的条件、何时崩溃，以及如何与官僚能力互动。这将连接到达尔·博 /dæl ˈboʊ/、埃尔南德斯-拉戈斯 /ɛrˈnɑːndɛz ˈlɑːɡoʊs/ 和马祖卡 /mɑːˈzuːkɑː/ 使用正式理论推导国家形成比较静态的方法。
（2）用更严格的因果识别检验框架。可以利用自然实验——如火山爆发或气候冲击导致农业变异性外生增加——来检验不确定性增加是否导致议会治理的采纳。类似地，哥伦布大交换引入新作物（斯塔萨维奇定性使用了这一点）可以用双重差分方法形式化为准实验。
（3）早期民主的长期后果。这些章节解释了早期民主从何而来，但没有追踪其长期效应。拥有早期民主传统的社会是否发展了不同的经济制度、不同水平的信任或不同的治理规范？这与哈里里 /hæˈriːri/ 的发现相连——早期本土国家与现代专制相关——斯塔萨维奇的框架暗示了更细致的预测：拥有议会的早期国家可能与没有议会的早期国家有不同的长期轨迹。
（4）官僚制与民主的顺序。斯塔萨维奇简要建议顺序很重要：如果民主先发展，人口养成集体行动习惯，使其即使在官僚化的统治者面前也能进行约束；如果官僚制先出现，民主可能永远不会产生（第76页）。这一顺序假设是可检验的，并连接到关于国家能力与民主化是互补还是替代的大量文献（贝斯利 /ˈbɛzli/ 和佩尔松 /ˈpɜːrsən/、蒂利）。跨多个社会的系统检验将是有价值的。

（5）贸易和商业的作用。斯塔萨维奇简要提到热量变异性可能鼓励贸易（第68页），但将其作为替代解释予以排除。然而，在我们早前阅读的阿布拉姆森和博伊斯 /bɔɪks/ 关于欧洲政治碎片化的研究中，商业阶层在维持独立政治单元方面发挥了核心作用。将商业机制与信息机制整合可以产生更丰富的理论：也许贸易既增加了信息问题（更复杂的经济更难征税），又创造了一个新的利益群体（商人），他们既渴望政治发言权，又有退出的能力。
（6）技术采用作为政治选择。第四章将欧洲技术落后视为既定事实，但达龙·阿西莫格鲁 /ˈæsɪmoʊɡluː/ 和詹姆斯·罗宾逊等学者认为技术采用本身受政治制度塑造。也许受民主约束的欧洲统治者刻意避免采用会加强其权力的技术——或者非欧洲社会的精英积极推动了削弱议会的技术。理清这种共同演化将是一个重要贡献。
（7）对现代背景的应用。“弱国家继承民主”的逻辑是否适用于现代背景？许多发展中国家拥有斯塔萨维奇意义上“弱”的国家——无法有效监控生产或防止退出。这些国家是否倾向于在地方层面拥有更多民主制度，即使国家制度是专制的？这与关于非洲和亚洲的分权化、地方治理和“日常民主”的研究相连。

en: “On the Origins of the State: Stationary Bandits and Taxation in Eastern Congo.” Published in the Journal of Political Economy, 2020, Volume 128, Number 1 (pp. 32–74). (p. 32)
Raúl Sánchez de la Sierra /raˈul ˈsantʃeθ de la siˈera/, affiliated with the University of Chicago /juːnɪˈvɜːrsɪti əv ʃɪˈkɑːɡoʊ/ at the time of publication. (p. 32)
Electronically published December 20, 2019; journal issue dated 2020. (p. 32)

The paper has multiple dependent variables organized along three dimensions. The first dimension is the “extensive margin” of the essential functions of a state, which captures whether armed actors establish state-like institutions at a given location in a given year. The specific extensive-margin DVs include: (a) whether the municipality was attacked by any armed actor; (b) whether an armed actor imposed customs taxation on mining output at the exit of a mine; (c) whether an armed actor imposed entry fees (working permits/visas) to work at the mine; (d) whether an armed actor established a monopoly of violence (i.e., became a “stationary bandit”) at the mine or support village; (e) whether an armed actor provided a security service at the mine or support village; and (f) a normalized mean index combining these outcomes. All of these are coded as binary dummies (0 or 1) for each municipality–year observation. (pp. 48–52, Table 1)

The second dimension is household welfare, measured at support villages. Welfare proxies include: (a) a savings/investment index constructed via principal component analysis of yearly cattle acquisitions; (b) the log of yearly weddings celebrated in the village (a costly, prestigious investment in a bride-price society); (c) the log of yearly immigration into the village (reflecting the relative desirability of living there); and (d) a composite welfare index combining the above via principal component analysis. (pp. 57–58)

The third dimension is the “intensive margin” of the essential functions of a state, which captures the sophistication of taxation and administrative capacity. Specific intensive-margin DVs include: (a) whether a poll tax exists; (b) whether a market (food market) tax exists; (c) whether a toll booth exists at the village; (d) whether a mill tax exists; (e) a tax sophistication index (a weighted average of taxes, where the weight reflects how strongly each tax is associated with fiscal administration); (f) whether a fiscal administration exists at the village; (g) whether a judicial administration exists at the village; and (h) an overall intensive-margin index combining the above.

本文有多个因变量，按三个维度组织。第一个维度是国家基本功能的"广延边际"(extensive margin)，即武装行为者是否在特定地点和特定年份建立了类国家制度。具体的广延边际因变量包括：(a) 该市镇是否遭到武装行为者攻击；(b) 武装行为者是否在矿区出口对矿产产出征收海关税（通过设立路障实施）；(c) 武装行为者是否在矿区征收入场费（工作许可/签证）；(d) 武装行为者是否在矿区或支持村庄建立了暴力垄断（即成为"固定匪帮"）；(e) 武装行为者是否在矿区或支持村庄提供安全服务；(f) 以上各项的标准化均值指数。所有变量均为二元虚拟变量（0或1），以市镇–年为观测单位。（第48–52页，表1）
第二个维度是家庭福利，在支持村庄层面衡量。福利代理变量包括：(a) 基于年度购牛情况通过主成分分析构建的储蓄/投资指数；(b) 村庄年度婚礼数量的对数（在聘礼社会中，婚礼是一项昂贵且有声望的投资）；(c) 年度迁入人口数量的对数（反映居住在该村庄的相对吸引力）；(d) 综合以上各项通过主成分分析构建的福利综合指数。（第57–58页）

第三个维度是国家基本功能的"集约边际"(intensive margin)，即税收和行政能力的复杂化程度。具体的集约边际因变量包括：(a) 是否存在人头税；(b) 是否存在市场（食品市场）税；(c) 是否存在村庄收费站；(d) 是否存在磨坊税；(e) 税收复杂度指数（各种税的加权平均值，权重反映该种税与财政管理机构关联的强度）；(f) 村庄是否存在财政管理机构；(g) 村庄是否存在司法管理机构；(h) 综合以上各项的集约边际总指数。（第64–65页，表5）

en: The main independent variables are exogenous shocks to the world prices of two minerals extracted in eastern Congo /ˈkɒŋɡoʊ/: coltan (columbite-tantalite) and gold. Specifically, the paper uses the interaction between the logarithm of the US price of coltan (or gold) in year t and a dummy indicating whether municipality j is endowed with coltan (or gold). The key variation is thus a difference-in-differences design: mineral-endowed locations are “treated” by the price shocks, while locations without those minerals serve as comparisons. (pp. 48–49)

The coltan price shock was triggered in early 2000 when Sony /ˈsoʊni/ announced the Playstation II /ˈpleɪsteɪʃən/, which used coltan as a key input. The US price surged from $90 to $590 per kilogram before collapsing during Christmas 2000 when the console underperformed. The gold price shock came later, driven by investors rushing to gold as a safe haven after the global recession, with the price rising sharply especially after 2006, reaching levels up to 6 times pre-shock levels by 2012. (pp. 34, 44, Figure 1)

For the welfare analysis, additional key IVs include the type of stationary bandit present (militia, external armed actor, or FARDC /ɛf eɪ ɑːr diː siː/ — the Congolese national army), and an instrumental variable based on the 2003 Sun City /sʌn ˈsɪti/ peace agreement. (pp. 58, 61–66)
zh: 主要自变量是刚果（金）/ˈkɒŋɡoʊ/东部开采的两种矿物——钶钽铁矿（coltan）和黄金——的世界价格的外生冲击。具体而言，论文使用的是t年美国钶钽铁矿（或黄金）价格的对数与市镇j是否蕴含钶钽铁矿（或黄金）的虚拟变量的交互项。关键的变异来自双重差分设计：拥有矿产的地点被价格冲击"处理"，而没有这些矿产的地点作为对照组。（第48–49页）
钶钽铁矿价格冲击发生在2000年初，当时
游戏机），该产品以钶钽铁矿为关键输入品。美国价格从每公斤90美元飙升至590美元，随后在2000年圣诞节该游戏机表现不佳时崩溃。黄金价格冲击发生在较晚时期，由全球衰退后投资者涌向黄金作为避险资产驱动，价格在2006年后大幅上涨，到2012年达到冲击前水平的6倍。（第34、44页，图1）
在福利分析中，额外的关键自变量包括固定匪帮的类型（民兵、外部武装行为者、或FARDC /ɛf eɪ ɑːr diː siː/——刚果国民军），以及基于2003年Sun City /sʌn ˈsɪti/（太阳城）和平协议的工具变量。（第58、61–66页）

en: The paper conceptualizes the state following the Weberian and Tillyan tradition. Following Weber /ˈveɪbər/ (1946), a state is defined by its “monopoly of violence.” Following Tilly /ˈtɪli/ (1985, 1990), the “essential functions of a state” are: (1) war making — eliminating or neutralizing rivals outside the claimed territory; (2) state making — eliminating or neutralizing rivals inside the territory; (3) protection — eliminating or neutralizing enemies of the ruler’s clients (i.e., protecting the property of those taxed); and (4) extraction — acquiring the means to carry out the first three activities, i.e., taxation. (pp. 33, 38–39)

Crucially, the paper does not treat the state as a binary concept or as a product of some grand social contract. Instead, it views state formation as a continuous, never-complete process that emerges from the activities of armed actors seeking to extract revenue. The paper draws on Olson’s /ˈɒlsən/ (1993) concept of the “stationary bandit”: a roving bandit who, rather than looting and moving on, decides to settle in a territory, establish a monopoly of violence, tax the population in a predictable way, and — because the bandit is now a residual claimant on the local economy — provide protection. Thus, the emergence of the state is not separate from organized crime; rather, states are the most successful organized crime operations that acquired legitimacy over time. Tilly (1985) famously put it: “War makes states.” (pp. 33–34, 38–42)

The paper further distinguishes between the “extensive margin” and the “intensive margin” of state formation. The extensive margin refers to whether state-like functions exist at all (i.e., whether a monopoly of violence, taxation, and protection emerge). The intensive margin refers to how sophisticated these functions become — whether the ruler develops more complex taxes (poll taxes, consumption taxes), creates fiscal and judicial administrations, and generally builds “fiscal capacity” in the sense of Besley and Persson /ˈbɛzli ænd ˈpɛrsən/ (2009). The historical parallel is drawn to the “maturing” of early states described by Ardant /arˈdɑ̃/ (1975) and Claessen and Skalnik /ˈklɑːsən ænd ˈskɑːlnɪk/ (1978). (pp. 40–42)

Measurement is done through a yearly panel dataset covering 650 locations (239 support villages and 411 mining sites) in eastern DRC from 1995 to 2013, reconstructed via recall-based surveys. The “state” is measured by the binary indicators listed above in the DV section: whether a stationary bandit exists, whether customs or other taxes are collected, whether security is provided, and whether fiscal and judicial administrations operate. (pp. 34, 44–48)
zh: 本文遵循韦伯和蒂利传统来概念化国家。遵循Weber /ˈveɪbər/（韦伯，1946），国家由其"暴力垄断"来定义。遵循Tilly /ˈtɪli/（蒂利，1985, 1990），"国家的基本功能"包括：(1) 战争制造——消除或中和领土外的竞争对手；(2) 国家制造——消除或中和领土内的竞争对手；(3) 保护——消除或中和统治者客户的敌人（即保护被征税者的财产）；(4) 攫取——获得执行前三项活动的手段，即征税。（第33、38–39页）

关键的是，本文不将国家视为二元概念或某种伟大社会契约的产物。相反，它将国家形成视为一个持续的、永远不会完成的过程，源于武装行为者寻求攫取收入的活动。论文借鉴了Olson /ˈɒlsən/（奥尔森，1993）的"固定匪帮"概念：一个流动匪帮决定不再掠夺后转移，而是定居在一个领地内，建立暴力垄断，以可预测的方式向人口征税，并且——因为匪帮现在是当地经济的剩余索取者——提供保护。因此，国家的出现并非独立于有组织犯罪；相反，国家就是那些最成功的、随时间获得了合法性的有组织犯罪行动。蒂利（1985）的名言是："战争制造国家。"（第33–34、38–42页）
论文进一步区分了国家形成的"广延边际"和"集约边际"。广延边际指的是类国家功能是否存在（即暴力垄断、税收和保护是否出现）。集约边际指的是这些功能变得多么复杂——统治者是否发展出更复杂的税收（人头税、消费税），是否创建财政和司法管理机构，以及是否总体上建立了
Besley and Persson /ˈbɛzli ænd ˈpɛrsən/（贝斯利和佩尔松，2009）意义上的"财政能力"。历史类比来自Ardant /arˈdɑ̃/（阿尔当，1975）和Claessen and Skalnik /ˈklɑːsən ænd ˈskɑːlnɪk/（克莱森和斯卡尔尼克，1978）所描述的早期国家的"成熟化"。（第40–42页）
衡量方式是通过一个年度面板数据集，覆盖刚果（金）东部650个地点（239个支持村庄和411个矿场），时间跨度为1995年至2013年，基于回忆式调查重建。"国家"通过上述因变量部分列出的二元指标来衡量：是否存在固定匪帮，是否征收海关税或其他税，是否提供安全服务，以及是否运作财政和司法管理机构。（第34、44–48页）

en: The core argument is that the essential functions of a state — monopoly of violence, taxation, and protection — can emerge endogenously from the revenue-maximizing behavior of armed actors, rather than from any social contract or deliberate state-building project. When the value of extractable output rises and that output can be observed, armed actors have stronger incentives to settle as stationary bandits, establish customs taxation, and provide protection at production sites. When the output is hard to observe (concealable), armed actors still have incentives to capture the surplus, but they must do so indirectly — by settling in the consumption economies (support villages) where miners spend their income, introducing more sophisticated taxes (working permits, poll taxes, market taxes), and building fiscal and judicial administrations to support collection. (pp. 34–35, 42–43)

The paper further argues that the welfare effect of having a stationary bandit depends on the armed actor’s objectives. When the stationary bandit’s mission encompasses the population’s well-being — as with popular militias or the FARDC (despite its corruption) — the net effect on household welfare is positive. This is because the protection these actors provide, and the order they establish, outweighs the costs of their taxation. In contrast, when the stationary bandit is an external armed actor with no ties to the local population, the welfare effect is negligible or negative. (pp. 35, 57–66)

Finally, the paper identifies a novel “administration resource curse”: when output can be directly taxed (as with coltan), a resource boom leads stationary bandits to focus on easy-to-collect customs taxes and to dismantle their more sophisticated fiscal and judicial administrations in support villages. The opportunity cost of maintaining an administration rises when customs revenue is abundant. In contrast, when output is concealable (as with gold), a resource boom forces stationary bandits to invest in more sophisticated taxation and fiscal capacity. (pp. 35–36, 64–68)
zh: 核心论点是，国家的基本功能——暴力垄断、税收和保护——可以从武装行为者追求收入最大化的行为中内生地产生，而非来自任何社会契约或刻意的国家建设项目。当可提取产出的价值上升且该产出可以被观察到时，武装行为者有更强的激励定居为固定匪帮，建立海关税收，并在生产地点提供保护。当产出难以观察（可隐匿）时，武装行为者仍有激励攫取剩余，但必须间接地——通过定居在矿工花费收入的消费经济体（支持村庄），引入更复杂的税收（工作许可、人头税、市场税），并建设财政和司法管理机构来支持征收。（第34–35、42–43页）
论文进一步论证，固定匪帮对福利的影响取决于武装行为者的目标。当固定匪帮的使命涵盖人口福祉——如民众民兵或FARDC（尽管存在腐败）——对家庭福利的净效应是正面的。这是因为这些行为者提供的保护和建立的秩序超过了其税收的成本。相反，当固定匪帮是与当地人口无联系的外部武装行为者时，福利效应可以忽略或为负。（第35、57–66页）
最后，论文识别了一种新型的"行政资源诅咒"：当产出可以直接征税时（如钶钽铁矿），资源繁荣导致固定匪帮专注于易征收的海关税，并拆除其在支持村庄的更复杂的财政和司法管理机构。当海关收入充裕时，维持行政机构的机会成本上升。相反，当产出可隐匿时（如黄金），资源繁荣迫使固定匪帮投资于更复杂的税收和财政能力。（第35–36、64–68页）

en: 8.1 Observable vs. Concealable Output

The central mechanism hinges on the observability of mining output. Coltan is bulky — a miner can produce up to 50 kg per day, and it must be transported in 75 kg bags by foot from the mine to the support village. This makes coltan output highly observable and easy to tax through customs (roadblocks at the mine exit). Gold, by contrast, has an extremely high value-to-weight ratio — daily production per worker is between 0.1 and 10 grams. Miners routinely conceal gold by swallowing it. Gold is thus called the “immaterial mineral.” A customs tax on gold would face systematic evasion and is thus rarely observed. (pp. 44, 50)

When the coltan price rises, the returns to establishing a roadblock and customs taxation at coltan mines increase. Armed actors thus create monopolies of violence and provide protection at coltan mines — the “essential functions of a state” emerge at the production site. When the gold price rises, armed actors cannot tax gold output directly. Instead, they settle in the support villages where miners’ families live and where income from gold is spent. There, they introduce working permits (a tax on mining labor rather than output), poll taxes, and market taxes. Because these taxes are harder to collect, armed actors also invest in fiscal and judicial administrations. (pp. 34–35, 50–52)
8.2 Trade Infrastructure

The coltan effect is concentrated near airstrips, the infrastructure through which coltan is exported, given the DRC’s poor road network. This confirms that the mechanism operates through the ability to tax and ship observable output. The gold effect, by contrast, is equally strong whether or not the village is near an airstrip, consistent with gold’s concealability making physical trade infrastructure irrelevant for the armed actors’ taxation strategy. (p. 54)
8.3 Type of Armed Actor and Welfare

The paper identifies four types of armed actors: (1) village self-defense militias, formed by villagers themselves; (2) regional militias, which originated as village militias but expanded; (3) external armed actors, representing foreign ethnic groups; and (4) the FARDC, the Congolese national army, which is infiltrated by organized crime. Village militias and the FARDC are seen as more legitimate (29% and 70% of episodes, respectively) and are more coethnic with the local population. External armed actors are seen as least legitimate (17%) and commit the most violent attacks (72% of their attacks include pillage, 59% sexual violence, 61% death). (pp. 54–57, Table 2)

The welfare mechanism works as follows: when the stationary bandit’s objective function places weight on the population’s welfare (as with militias and the FARDC), the protection they provide and the order they establish create a net positive effect. The value added through protection of property rights exceeds the value destroyed through tax distortions. When the stationary bandit cares only about extraction (external actors), the surplus is expropriated away and household welfare does not improve. (pp. 35, 42, 57–66)
8.4 Administration Resource Curse

When coltan prices rise, stationary bandits at coltan support villages dismantle their fiscal and judicial administrations. The logic is one of opportunity costs: customs taxation of coltan output is easy and profitable, so armed actors reallocate their limited organizational resources toward customs at mines and away from more sophisticated taxation in villages. Conversely, when gold prices rise, stationary bandits cannot rely on customs taxation and must invest in fiscal capacity to capture the surplus — introducing poll taxes, market taxes, and building administrative capacity. This mechanism is confirmed even after controlling for changes in the composition of stationary bandits (organization fixed effects). (pp. 35–36, 64–68, Table 5)
zh: 可观察产出 vs. 可隐匿产出
核心机制取决于矿产产出的可观察性。钶钽铁矿体积大——一个矿工每天可生产多达50公斤，必须以75公斤的袋装从矿区步行运到支持村庄。这使得钶钽铁矿产出高度可观察，容易通过海关（在矿区出口设路障）征税。相比之下，黄金的价值重量比极高——每个工人的日产量在0.1到10克之间。矿工常通过吞咽来隐藏黄金。因此黄金被称为"非物质矿物"。对黄金的海关税将面临系统性逃税，因此很少被观察到。（第44、50页）

当钶钽铁矿价格上升时，在钶钽铁矿矿区建立路障和海关税收的回报增加。武装行为者因此在钶钽铁矿矿区建立暴力垄断并提供保护——国家的"基本功能"在生产地点出现。当黄金价格上升时，武装行为者无法直接对黄金产出征税。相反，他们定居在矿工家庭居住和矿工花费收入的支持村庄。在那里，他们引入工作许可（对采矿劳动而非产出征税）、人头税和市场税。由于这些税更难征收，武装行为者还投资于财政和司法管理机构。（第34–35、50–52页）
贸易基础设施
钶钽铁矿效应集中在靠近简易机场的地区，鉴于刚果（金）糟糕的公路网络，这是钶钽铁矿出口的基础设施。这证实了该机制通过对可观察产出征税和运输的能力来运作。相比之下，黄金效应在靠近和远离机场的村庄同样强烈，这与黄金的可隐匿性使实体贸易基础设施对武装行为者的征税策略无关紧要的判断一致。（第54页）
武装行为者类型与福利

论文识别了四类武装行为者：(1) 村庄自卫民兵，由村民自己组建；(2) 地区民兵，源于村庄民兵但规模扩大；(3) 外部武装行为者，代表外国族群利益；(4) FARDC，即刚果国民军，被有组织犯罪渗透。村庄民兵和FARDC被认为更合法（分别在29%和70%的事件中），且与当地人口更多同族。外部武装行为者被认为最不合法（17%），并犯下最暴力的攻击（72%的攻击包括抢劫，59%包括性暴力，61%包括死亡）。（第54–57页，表2）
福利机制如下运作：当固定匪帮的目标函数赋予人口福祉以权重时（如民兵和FARDC），他们提供的保护和建立的秩序产生净正效应。通过保护产权创造的附加值超过了税收扭曲破坏的价值。当固定匪帮只关心攫取时（外部行为者），剩余被没收，家庭福利不会改善。（第35、42、57–66页）
行政资源诅咒

当钶钽铁矿价格上升时，钶钽铁矿支持村庄的固定匪帮拆除其财政和司法管理机构。其逻辑在于机会成本：钶钽铁矿产出的海关税收既容易又有利可图，因此武装行为者将其有限的组织资源从村庄的复杂税收重新分配到矿区的海关。相反，当黄金价格上升时，固定匪帮不能依赖海关税收，必须投资于财政能力以攫取剩余——引入人头税、市场税，并建设行政能力。即使在控制了固定匪帮组成变化（组织固定效应）之后，这一机制仍得到证实。（第35–36、64–68页，表5）

en: 9.1 Geographic Coverage and Unit of Analysis

The study covers the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo /ˌdɛməˈkrætɪk rɪˈpʌblɪk əv ðə ˈkɒŋɡoʊ/ (DRC), specifically the provinces of Sud Kivu /suːd ˈkiːvuː/ and Nord Kivu /nɔːr ˈkiːvuː/. The sample consists of 239 support villages and their corresponding 411 mining sites, which together form 239 municipalities. The unit of analysis is the municipality–year (for mine-level and village-level analyses) and the household–year (for welfare analyses). (pp. 44–47, Figure 2)
9.2 Time Period
The panel covers 1995 to 2013. Data collection took place from July 2012 through June 2013 in Sud Kivu and from February through October 2015 in Nord Kivu. (p. 46)
9.3 Data Sources and Collection

The dataset was reconstructed through recall-based surveys. In each support village, a team of 10 surveyors trained local “history specialists” — individuals with long involvement in mining or village elders — to consolidate a municipality history over one week. Simultaneously, surveyors administered private interviews in 6–8 randomly selected households per village. At the end of the week, surveyors held a day-long meeting with the history specialists to cross-check data. The data from this meeting constitute the main source. The author acknowledges three challenges of recall data: measurement error from imperfect recall of magnitudes, measurement error from imperfect recall of dates, and reporting bias for sensitive information. Three months of piloting were used to develop protocols tailored to the cultural context. (pp. 44–46)
9.4 Measurement of Key Variables

Mineral prices: US prices of coltan and gold are taken from the US Geological Survey (2016). Local DRC prices are from the recall survey but are acknowledged to be “extremely unreliable” (which motivates the use of US prices in the reduced-form specification). Mineral endowment dummies are constructed from existing datasets (International Peace Information Service 2009; Référentiel Géographique Commun 2010) supplemented by the author’s own prospections. The armed actor classification (village militia, regional militia, external, FARDC) is based on fieldwork conversations with active officers and combatants of numerous armed groups between 2010 and 2018. (pp. 44–49, Table 2)

For the welfare analysis, only households settled in the support village before 2000 are included to avoid compositional effects from migration. Household-year data are aggregated to support village–year means. An additional implicit association test (IAT) was administered to approximately 10 households in 106 Nord Kivu support villages to measure biases toward different armed groups. (pp. 57–58, 67)
zh: 地理覆盖范围和分析单位

研究覆盖刚果民主共和国 /ˌdɛməˈkrætɪk rɪˈpʌblɪk əv ðə ˈkɒŋɡoʊ/（DRC）东部，具体为Sud Kivu /suːd ˈkiːvuː/（南基伍省）和Nord Kivu /nɔːr ˈkiːvuː/（北基伍省）。样本包含239个支持村庄及其对应的411个矿场，共同组成239个市镇。分析单位为市镇–年（矿区级和村庄级分析）和家庭–年（福利分析）。（第44–47页，图2）
时间跨度
面板覆盖1995年至2013年。数据收集在南基伍省从2012年7月至2013年6月进行，在北基伍省从2015年2月至10月进行。（第46页）
数据来源和收集

数据集通过回忆式调查重建。在每个支持村庄，10名调查员的团队培训当地"历史专家"——在采矿业有长期参与的个人或村庄长者——在一周内整合市镇历史。同时，调查员在每个村庄随机抽取的6–8个家庭中进行私人访谈。在一周结束时，调查员与历史专家举行为期一天的会议以交叉核实数据。该会议的数据构成主要数据来源。作者承认回忆数据的三个挑战：对幅度的不完美回忆造成的测量误差、对日期的不完美回忆造成的测量误差、以及敏感信息的报告偏差。三个月的试点用于开发适合文化背景的协议。（第44–46页）
关键变量的衡量

矿物价格：钶钽铁矿和黄金的美国价格取自美国地质调查局（US Geological Survey, 2016）。刚果（金）当地价格来自回忆调查，但被认为"极不可靠"（这促使在简约形式规范中使用美国价格）。矿物禀赋虚拟变量根据现有数据集（International Peace Information Service 2009; Référentiel Géographique Commun 2010）构建，并辅以作者自己的勘探。武装行为者分类（村庄民兵、地区民兵、外部、FARDC）基于2010年至2018年间与众多武装团体的现役军官和战斗人员的田野调查对话。（第44–49页，表2）
对于福利分析，仅包括2000年之前定居在支持村庄的家庭，以避免因移民造成的组成效应。家庭–年数据聚合为支持村庄–年均值。此外，在106个北基伍支持村庄中约10户家庭还接受了内隐联想测试（IAT），以衡量对不同武装团体的偏见。（第57–58、67页）

en: 10.1 Main Specification: Reduced-Form Difference-in-Differences

The primary empirical strategy is a reduced-form linear probability model estimated via OLS: Y_jt = β_t + α_j + γ_c · C_j · p^US_c,t + γ_g · G_j · p^US_g,t + ε_jt, where Y_jt is a binary outcome in municipality j in year t, α_j and β_t are municipality and year fixed effects, C_j (G_j) is a dummy for coltan (gold) endowment, and p^US_c,t (p^US_g,t) is the log of the US price of coltan (gold). The coefficients γ_c and γ_g indicate how a 1 percentage point increase in the US mineral price differentially affects outcomes in mineral-endowed locations relative to non-endowed locations. Standard errors are clustered at the municipality level. (pp. 48–49)
10.2 Key Identifying Assumptions

For this strategy to identify causal effects, several assumptions must hold. First, parallel trends: absent the price shocks, outcomes in mineral-endowed and non-endowed locations would have evolved similarly. The author provides evidence that pre-shock trends are parallel and that including leads does not affect the contemporaneous coefficient. Second, exogeneity of US prices: the US price of coltan and gold must be unaffected by the behavior of armed actors in the DRC. For gold, the DRC’s share of global output is negligible (0.08%), making this plausible. For coltan, the DRC’s share is larger (up to 20% by 2010), but the year-2000 price spike was driven by speculative demand for Playstation II and the failure of Australian supply, not by DRC conditions. The author also shows that restricting the coltan analysis to 1999–2001 (the only credibly exogenous interval) leaves results unchanged. Third, the price shocks must not be correlated with other unobserved shocks differentially affecting mineral vs. non-mineral locations. (pp. 48–49, 54)
10.3 Instrumental Variables for Welfare Analysis

To estimate the causal effect of different types of stationary bandits on household welfare, the author uses the 2003 Sun City peace agreement as an instrument. The agreement led the RCD /ɑːr siː diː/ (the main external armed actor) and some regional militias to voluntarily withdraw from their territories. The IV strategy is a 2SLS using POST_t × SB^2002_j as an instrument for whether a stationary bandit is present, where POST_t indicates years after 2003 and SB^2002_j indicates whether a location had a stationary bandit in 2002. The exclusion restriction requires that the peace agreement affected household welfare only through the withdrawal (or retention) of stationary bandits, not through other channels. The first-stage F-statistic is above 20. (pp. 61–66, Table 4)
10.4 Additional Methods

For the intensive-margin analysis, the author includes stationary-bandit organization fixed effects to separate changes in armed actors’ behavior from changes in the composition of stationary bandits. The author also reports 13 robustness checks including 2SLS using local prices, Arellano-Bond estimation, alternative year intervals, leads and lags, controls for time-varying village characteristics, and exclusion/inclusion of the FARDC. (pp. 49, 64–68)
FARDC。
zh: 主要规范：简约形式双重差分

主要的经验策略是通过OLS估计的简约形式线性概率模型：Y_jt = β_t + α_j + γ_c · C_j · p^US_c,t + γ_g · G_j · p^US_g,t + ε_jt，其中Y_jt是市镇j在t年的二元结果变量，α_j和β_t分别是市镇和年份固定效应，C_j (G_j)是钶钽铁矿（黄金）禀赋虚拟变量，p^US_c,t (p^US_g,t)是钶钽铁矿（黄金）美国价格的对数。系数γ_c和γ_g表示美国矿物价格每增加1个百分点，对矿产蕴藏地相对于非蕴藏地的差异影响。标准误在市镇层面聚类。（第48–49页）
关键识别假设

要使该策略识别因果效应，必须满足几个假设。第一，平行趋势：在没有价格冲击的情况下，矿产蕴藏地和非蕴藏地的结果变量将以类似方式演变。作者提供了冲击前趋势平行的证据，并表明包含前导项不影响当期系数。第二，美国价格的外生性：钶钽铁矿和黄金的美国价格不受刚果（金）武装行为者行为的影响。对于黄金，刚果（金）的全球产出份额微不足道（0.08%），使这一假设合理。对于钶钽铁矿，刚果（金）的份额较大（到2010年高达20%），但2000年的价格飙升由PlayStation II的投机需求和澳大利亚供应失败驱动，而非刚果（金）条件。作者还表明，将钶钽铁矿分析限制在1999–2001年（唯一可信外生的区间）不改变结果。第三，价格冲击不得与差异性影响矿产与非矿产地区的其他不可观察冲击相关。（第48–49、54页）
福利分析的工具变量

为估计不同类型固定匪帮对家庭福利的因果效应，作者使用2003年Sun City /sʌn ˈsɪti/（太阳城）和平协议作为工具变量。该协议导致RCD /ɑːr siː diː/（当时主要的外部武装行为者联盟）和一些地区民兵自愿撤出其领地。工具变量策略是2SLS，使用POST_t × SB^2002_j作为固定匪帮是否存在的工具，其中POST_t表示2003年之后的年份，SB^2002_j表示2002年某地点是否有固定匪帮。排除性限制要求和平协议仅通过固定匪帮的撤出（或保留）影响家庭福利，而非通过其他渠道。第一阶段F统计量高于20。（第61–66页，表4）
其他方法
对于集约边际分析，作者包含固定匪帮组织固定效应，以将武装行为者行为的变化与固定匪帮组成的变化分离。作者还报告了13项稳健性检验，包括使用当地价格的2SLS、Arellano-Bond估计、替代年份区间、前导项和滞后项、时变村庄特征控制、以及排除/包含

en: First, the paper provides the first systematic, quantitative, causal evidence for a process that has been extensively theorized in historical sociology (Tilly 1985, 1990), anthropology (Claessen and Skalnik 1978), and political philosophy (Hobbes 1651), but never tested with disaggregated panel data. It shows that the essential functions of a state — monopoly of violence, taxation, and protection — emerge endogenously from armed actors’ revenue-maximizing behavior in response to economic incentives. This bridges the gap between the “contractarian” view (states arise from popular will) and the “conflict” view (states are successful organized crime) by showing that both can coexist: popular militias that provide welfare-enhancing governance still originate from extractive motives. (pp. 35–36)

Second, the paper assembles a remarkable original dataset — a yearly panel on 650 locations over nearly two decades in a conflict zone — through an innovative recall-based methodology. This addresses the fundamental data constraint that has hampered the study of early state formation: statistics were first created by states, so data on their origins did not previously exist. (pp. 33–34, 44–46)

Third, the paper introduces the concept of an “administration resource curse”: resource booms can lead rulers to dismantle fiscal capacity when they can rely on easy-to-collect customs revenue. This complements the traditional resource curse literature (which focuses on conflict and the “rentier state”) by showing that resource booms can endogenously affect administrative capacity, with potential long-run implications for state development. (pp. 36–37)

Fourth, the paper provides a nuanced complement to the civil war literature. Rather than simply showing that commodity price shocks increase violence (the “rapacity effect” of Dube and Vargas 2013), it shows that the same shocks can lead to subtler institutional changes — the emergence of taxation, protection, and governance structures — depending on the observability of output. (p. 36)

Fifth, the paper provides credible evidence that the welfare effect of stationary bandits depends on whose interests the armed actor represents. This finding has important policy implications for international peacebuilding and for understanding when armed governance can be welfare-improving in failed states. (pp. 57–66)
zh: 第一，本文首次提供了系统的、定量的、因果性的证据，验证了在历史社会学（Tilly 1985, 1990）、人类学（Claessen and Skalnik 1978）和政治哲学（Hobbes 1651）中被广泛理论化、但从未用分解面板数据检验的过程。它表明国家的基本功能——暴力垄断、税收和保护——从武装行为者对经济激励作出反应的收入最大化行为中内生地产生。这弥合了"契约论"观点（国家源于民众意志）与"冲突"观点（国家是成功的有组织犯罪）之间的鸿沟，表明两者可以共存：提供福利增进治理的民众民兵仍然源于攫取动机。（第35–36页）
第二，论文组装了一个卓越的原创数据集——在冲突区覆盖650个地点近二十年的年度面板——通过创新的回忆式调查方法。这解决了阻碍早期国家形成研究的根本数据约束：统计数据最先由国家创造，因此关于其起源的数据此前并不存在。（第33–34、44–46页）
第三，论文引入了"行政资源诅咒"概念：当统治者可以依赖易征收的海关收入时，资源繁荣可能导致其拆除财政能力。这补充了传统的资源诅咒文献（侧重于冲突和"食利国家"），表明资源繁荣可以内生地影响行政能力，对国家发展具有潜在的长期影响。（第36–37页）
第四，论文为内战文献提供了细致的补充。它不仅简单地表明大宗商品价格冲击增加暴力（Dube and Vargas 2013的"掠夺效应"），还表明同样的冲击可以导致更微妙的制度变化——税收、保护和治理结构的出现——这取决于产出的可观察性。（第36页）
第五，论文提供了可信的证据表明固定匪帮对福利的效应取决于武装行为者代表谁的利益。这一发现对国际和平建设以及理解武装治理何时能在失败国家中改善福利具有重要的政策含义。（第57–66页）

en: First, the recall-based data methodology, while innovative, introduces measurement error in three forms: imprecise recall of magnitudes, imprecise recall of dates, and reporting bias for sensitive information. The local mineral price data are acknowledged to be “extremely unreliable” (p. 49). While the author uses US prices to avoid this problem, the recall-based outcomes themselves may be subject to similar errors. Future work could triangulate with administrative data, satellite imagery (nightlights, deforestation), or real-time event-tracking data where available.

Second, the coltan price shock is a single, sharp, short-lived event (1999–2001), while the gold price shock is a slow, prolonged trend (2006–2012). The asymmetry between these two shocks makes it difficult to cleanly attribute differences in armed actor behavior solely to the observability of output versus other differences between the coltan and gold economies (e.g., different time periods, different geopolitical contexts, different compositions of armed actors). The author partially addresses this through robustness checks restricting the coltan analysis to 1999–2001, but a cleaner design might exploit variation within a single mineral type.

Third, the exclusion restriction for the IV strategy (the Sun City peace agreement) is debatable. The peace agreement may have affected household welfare through channels other than the withdrawal of stationary bandits — for example, through reduced conflict intensity, changes in trade patterns, or influxes of international aid. The author controls for some of these channels, but residual concerns remain.

Fourth, the paper treats “stationary bandit” as a binary variable, but in reality there is enormous variation in the capacity, ideology, and behavior of different armed actors even within the same category. The four-type classification (village militia, regional militia, external, FARDC) is coarse. A more granular analysis of specific armed organizations might reveal important heterogeneity.

Fifth, the paper does not directly observe tax rates, tax revenue, or the quality of public goods provided by stationary bandits. The outcomes are all binary indicators of institutional existence (does a tax exist? does an administration exist?). A richer dataset on the intensity and quality of governance would strengthen the claims about welfare effects.

Sixth, external validity is a concern. The eastern DRC is an extreme case of state failure, with near-total absence of central government authority. Whether the same mechanisms operate in contexts with stronger (but still weak) state presence, or in earlier historical periods, remains an open question.
zh: 第一，回忆式数据方法虽然创新，但引入了三种形式的测量误差：对幅度的不精确回忆、对日期的不精确回忆、以及敏感信息的报告偏差。当地矿物价格数据被承认"极不可靠"（第49页）。虽然作者使用美国价格来避免这一问题，但基于回忆的结果变量本身可能存在类似误差。未来的工作可以与行政数据、卫星图像（夜间灯光、森林砍伐）或可用的实时事件追踪数据进行三角验证。

第二，钶钽铁矿价格冲击是一次单一的、剧烈的、短暂的事件（1999–2001），而黄金价格冲击是一个缓慢的、持续的趋势（2006–2012）。这两个冲击之间的不对称性使得难以将武装行为者行为的差异完全归因于产出的可观察性，而非钶钽铁矿和黄金经济之间的其他差异（例如不同的时间段、不同的地缘政治背景、不同的武装行为者组成）。作者通过将钶钽铁矿分析限制在1999–2001年的稳健性检验部分地解决了这一问题，但更干净的设计可能利用单一矿物类型内的变异。
第三，工具变量策略（Sun City和平协议）的排除性限制是有争议的。和平协议可能通过固定匪帮撤出之外的渠道影响家庭福利——例如通过冲突强度降低、贸易模式变化、或国际援助涌入。作者控制了其中一些渠道，但仍存在残余担忧。
第四，论文将"固定匪帮"视为二元变量，但实际上即使在同一类别内，不同武装行为者的能力、意识形态和行为也存在巨大差异。四类分类（村庄民兵、地区民兵、外部、FARDC）较为粗糙。对特定武装组织更细粒度的分析可能揭示重要的异质性。
第五，论文没有直接观察税率、税收收入或固定匪帮提供的公共物品质量。结果变量都是制度存在的二元指标（某种税是否存在？某种管理机构是否存在？）。更丰富的关于治理强度和质量的数据集将加强关于福利效应的主张。
第六，外部有效性是一个问题。刚果（金）东部是国家失败的极端案例，中央政府权力几乎完全缺失。同样的机制是否在国家存在感更强（但仍然薄弱）的背景下或在更早的历史时期运作，仍然是一个开放的问题。

en: First, a natural extension would investigate the dynamics of legitimacy acquisition by armed actors. The paper documents that some stationary bandits conduct propaganda campaigns, initiation rituals, and witch hunts, but does not analyze these systematically. A follow-up study could examine how armed actors transition from pure extraction to legitimate governance — the process by which “bandits become kings.” The author’s own subsequent work (Henn, Marchais, and Sánchez de la Sierra 2018) moves in this direction by studying the interaction between armed groups and traditional chiefs. (pp. 68, 70)

Second, one could study the long-run persistence of these institutions. Do the fiscal administrations and judicial systems created by stationary bandits persist after the armed actors leave? Do they become the foundations of more permanent governance structures? This would connect to the broader literature on institutional persistence and path dependence.

Third, the “administration resource curse” mechanism could be tested in other settings — for example, in countries where oil or gas revenues reduce governments’ incentives to build tax administrations. The DRC setting provides a micro-level analogue of the macro-level “rentier state” hypothesis, and cross-country evidence could validate the generalizability of this mechanism.

Fourth, the paper’s framework could be extended to study competition between armed actors. When multiple armed groups compete for control, does this lead to better or worse governance for civilians? The paper notes that the strongest armed actors sort into the most profitable locations, but does not systematically analyze the effects of competition versus monopoly in the “market for protection.”

Fifth, one could study the demand side of state formation: do civilians actively seek out or invite stationary bandits in response to security threats? The paper finds that people migrate toward locations with militia-affiliated stationary bandits, suggesting revealed preference for such governance. A deeper investigation of civilian agency in state formation — through surveys, behavioral experiments, or historical case studies — would complement the supply-side analysis in this paper.

Sixth, the framework could be applied to study modern state fragility. Many contemporary states contain regions where non-state armed actors provide governance functions (e.g., Taliban in Afghanistan, cartels in Mexico, gangs in Central American cities). The analytical tools developed in this paper — particularly the distinction between observable and concealable economic activity as a determinant of governance structures — could be productively applied to these contexts.
zh: 第一，一个自然的延伸是调查武装行为者的合法性获取动态。论文记录了一些固定匪帮开展宣传运动、举行入会仪式和猎巫行动，但没有对这些进行系统分析。后续研究可以考察武装行为者如何从纯粹的攫取过渡到合法治理——即"匪帮成为国王"的过程。作者自己的后续工作（Henn, Marchais, and Sánchez de la Sierra 2018）通过研究武装团体与传统酋长之间的互动朝这个方向发展。（第68、70页）
第二，可以研究这些制度的长期持续性。固定匪帮创建的财政管理机构和司法系统在武装行为者离开后是否持续存在？它们是否成为更持久治理结构的基础？这将与关于制度持续性和路径依赖的更广泛文献联系起来。
第三，"行政资源诅咒"机制可以在其他环境中检验——例如在石油或天然气收入减少政府建设税务行政管理激励的国家。刚果（金）的设定提供了宏观层面"食利国家"假说的微观类比，跨国证据可以验证这一机制的可推广性。
第四，论文的框架可以扩展到研究武装行为者之间的竞争。当多个武装团体争夺控制权时，这是否导致对平民更好或更差的治理？论文指出最强大的武装行为者进入最有利可图的地点，但没有系统分析"保护市场"中竞争与垄断的效应。
第五，可以研究国家形成的需求侧：平民是否在安全威胁面前积极寻求或邀请固定匪帮？论文发现人们向有民兵附属固定匪帮的地点移民，暗示对这种治理的显示偏好。对国家形成中平民能动性的更深入调查——通过调查、行为实验或历史案例研究——将补充本文的供给侧分析。
第六，该框架可以应用于研究现代国家脆弱性。许多当代国家包含非国家武装行为者提供治理功能的地区（例如阿富汗/æfˈɡænɪstæn/的塔利班/ˈtælɪbæn/、墨西哥/ˈmɛksɪkoʊ/的贩毒集团、中美洲/ˌsɛntrəl əˈmɛrɪkə/城市的帮派）。本文开发的分析工具——特别是可观察和可隐匿经济活动作为治理结构决定因素的区分——可以有效地应用于这些情境。

en: Of Rule and Revenue, Chapters 2 “The Theory of Predatory Rule” and 3 “Creating Compliance”
Margaret Levi /ˈmɑːrɡərɪt ˈlɛvi/

The main dependent variable is the choice and variation of state revenue production policies: why rulers adopt particular systems of taxation and revenue extraction (such as tax farming, bureaucratic collection, or income taxation) and how compliance with those policies varies over time and across polities. Chapter 2 treats the choice of revenue system as the DV (p. 10, 17). Chapter 3 shifts the DV to variation in compliance with an existing tax contract over the life of the contract, especially the conditions under which “quasi-voluntary compliance” rises or falls (p. 48, 67–68)

The core independent variables are three constraints on ruler behavior: (1) relative bargaining power between rulers and their agents/constituents, (2) transaction costs of implementing and enforcing revenue policies, and (3) the rulers’ discount rates, namely how much they value future returns relative to present ones (p. 10, 13). These three constraints are themselves shaped by deeper structural variables: (1) productive forces and economic structure, (2) the international context, and (3) the form of government (p. 13). In Chapter 3, the additional key IV is the degree to which rulers can create and sustain quasi-voluntary compliance, which depends on the ruler’s credibility, the perceived fairness of the tax bargain, and taxpayer confidence that others are also complying (p. 52–53).

Levi conceptualizes the state through the lens of the ruler as the chief executive of the polity (p. 10, 14). The ruler can be a monarch, a military dictator, a president, or a prime minister, which is intentionally broad enough to cover polities across history. The state is understood as a nexus of contracts between rulers and agents, and rulers and constituents, though Levi notes that this formulation is imperfect because it under-theorizes the institutions that structure contracting and exchange (p. 11, footnote 1). State building, in this framework, is the process by which rulers devise and formalize structures that increase their bargaining power, reduce their transaction costs, and lower their discount rates so as to better capture gains from political exchange (p. 16). Levi uses the observable variation in revenue systems (the kinds of taxes, the administrative apparatus, and the degree of compliance) as the empirical proxies for the capacity and character of the state. The evolution of coercive, economic, and political resources under state control serves as the historical indicator of increasing state capacity (p. 17–19). Levi explicitly includes modern democracies: presidents and prime ministers are “rulers” constrained by legal checks, legislatures, organized agents, and pressure groups (p. 14).

Chapter 2: Rulers are predatory in that they always seek to maximize revenue to the state, but they do so subject to constraints: their relative bargaining power vis-à-vis agents and constituents, the transaction costs of implementing and enforcing policies, and their discount rates (p. 10). Rulers do not always plunder. They attempt to act like “discriminating monopolists,” devising different property rights and tax terms for different groups to maximize total state revenue (p. 10, citing North 1981). The choice of revenue system at any given point in time is determined by the configuration of these three constraints. These constraints are themselves the product of three structural factors: (1) the level of productive forces and economic structure, (2) the international context, and (3) the form of government (p. 13). Rulers actively try to alter these constraints in their favor. Significant differences in state policies across polities and historical periods primarily reflect differences in these constraints (p. 14). For instance, feudal monarchs facing subjects with comparable political and economic resources must make concessions (tax exemptions, services) in exchange for loyalty, while absolute monarchs monopolizing coercive capacity but lacking monitoring ability will create large revenue bases with minimal services, keeping only as much revenue as they can prevent agents from pocketing (p. 14).

Chapter 3: Once a revenue policy is established, compliance with it becomes the central problem. Levi argues that rulers cannot rely solely on coercion to enforce tax payment because coercion is expensive and breeds resentment (p. 32, 50). Nor can they rely solely on ideology or norms, which remain under-theorized and difficult to measure as independent causal factors (p. 51). The key mechanism Levi proposes is quasi-voluntary compliance: taxpayers choose to pay not purely out of fear of punishment nor out of normative conviction alone, but because they have confidence that (1) the rulers will keep their side of the bargain and deliver promised goods, and (2) other taxpayers will also pay (p. 52–53). Quasi-voluntary compliance is contingent and strategic: it depends on perceptions of fairness, on the rulers’ credibility (sustained through precommitment strategies, such as submitting budgets and making themselves residual claimants), and on institutions that coordinate taxpayer expectations (such as parliaments and communal tax-collecting units) (p. 60–66). If the terms of the tax contract shift against taxpayers’ interests without their consent, or if there is evidence that others are not paying, quasi-voluntary compliance will decline and noncompliance will cascade (p. 53–54, 67–68).
Chapter 2-Mechanisms determining revenue system choice:

1.Relative bargaining power operates through the distribution of coercive, economic, and political resources. When rulers monopolize these resources, they can set favorable terms of trade; when others control resources the ruler needs, the ruler must concede (p. 12, 17). Coercive resources historically centered on military capacity, and rulers with centralized armies had more bargaining power (p. 18, citing Bean 1973 and North 1981). Economic resources include ownership of means of production, trade routes, labor supply, and money (p. 19). Political resources include the ruler’s ability to prevent exit, block opposition, and promote support through positive incentives (patronage, legislation) and negative sanctions (exclusion, punishment) (p. 19–20). Ideology, while acknowledged as potentially important, is treated as an under-theorized residual (p. 22).
2.Transaction costs operate through several sub-mechanisms:

(1) Bargaining costs: the costs of acquiring information about bargaining partners and negotiating agreements; institutional arrangements like parliaments and collectivities reduce these costs (p. 28).

(2) Measurement costs: the technology available to standardize and assess taxable property determines what kinds of wealth can be taxed; ancient states could only tax produce because they could not measure land value or income (p. 29).

(3) Monitoring costs: rulers need to observe whether constituents comply and whether agents faithfully collect and remit revenue; technologies and institutions of monitoring (audits, feudal hierarchies, secret ballots, roll-call votes) affect what policies are feasible (p. 29–30).

(4) Agency costs: agents may shirk or steal; rulers must design incentive structures (fixed salaries, tax farming, bureaucracies) to minimize the divergence between the agents’ behavior and the rulers’ interests; Weber’s historical forms of agency (in-kind payments, prebendal organization, tax farming, modern bureaucracy) illustrate the trade-offs (p. 30–32).
(5) Enforcement costs: coercion is expensive; rulers therefore seek quasi-voluntary compliance to reduce the need for direct enforcement (p. 32).

3.Discount rates operate through the ruler’s time horizon. High discount rates (caused by military conflict, insecure tenure, or personal weakness of will / akrasia) lead rulers to extract maximum current revenue even at the cost of future productivity. Low discount rates (caused by security of rule) lead rulers to invest in economic growth and sustainable extraction (p. 32–33).
Chapter 3-Mechanisms determining compliance:
1.Coercion: the threat of sanctions for noncompliance, enforced by police, auditors, and agents. Effective but costly, and unable to achieve total compliance alone (p. 50).

2.Ideological compliance: some individuals pay taxes out of normative conviction. Levi treats this as outside the rational choice model but acknowledges its importance. Following North (1981, 1985), ideology is most influential where the costs of acting on it are low (p. 50–52).
3.Quasi-voluntary compliance is the central mechanism. It works through:

(1) Precommitment: Rulers bind themselves to future behavior (e.g., submitting budgets, making themselves legally accountable, becoming residual claimants), which reassures taxpayers that rulers will deliver promised goods (p. 61–62).

(2) Conditional cooperation between rulers and constituents: With iterated interactions and sufficiently low discount rates, a tit-for-tat strategy emerges. Institutions like parliaments enable repeat transactions that reveal preferences and build trust (p. 62–63).

(3) Manipulation of collective goods: Rulers can structure collective goods to have S-shaped or step production functions rather than linear ones, making individual contributions contingent on others’ contributions and transforming the game from a prisoner’s dilemma into something closer to an assurance game (p. 57–59).

(4) Conditional cooperation among constituents: Rulers promote communal institutions (Roman gens, Cuban /ˈkjuːbən/ CDRs, feudal manors, French village tax-collecting bodies) where members monitor each other, reducing the costs of state monitoring (p. 65–66).

(5) Coordination and loyalty: Rulers provide information that others are cooperating, publicize compliance, punish noncompliance visibly, and thereby coordinate expectations. This makes sanctions appear directed at others and reassures the potentially compliant (p. 66–67).

Chs 2 & 3 are primarily theoretical, laying out the analytical framework. They do not present systematic empirical data themselves, but Levi draws examples from world history: Philip /ˈfɪlɪp/ of Macedon /ˈmæsɪdɒn/ (p. 22, 35–36), Elizabeth /ɪˈlɪzəbəθ/ I and Philip /ˈfɪlɪp/ II of Spain (p. 22), ancient Egypt and China (p. 31), Cuba /ˈkjuːbə/ (p. 65), the United States (p. 23, 30, 51), the French Revolution (p. 22), etc.
Deductive theorizing plus historical comparison.
