zh: 分析数据集是截至1789年法国王国约35,000个教区（不含科西嘉 /ˈkɔːrsɪkə/）的面板，跨越从1710年代到1780年代的八个十年——共282,288个教区-十年观测值。教区是旧制度法国最小的行政单位，此前没有任何研究在如此细粒度的层面分析过这一时期（第7页）。
驿站网络数据是根据《法国驿站总表》构建的原始历史GIS。作者将1714年至1790年每十年的一个版本进行了数字化，提取了所有驿站之间的路段，并将每个地点与相应的教区匹配（第7–8页）。数据来源是行政性的，专为国家信使设计，因此具有高度可靠性。
叛乱数据来自让·尼古拉 /ʒɑ̃ nikɔˈla/ 的调查（Nicolas 1985; 2002），该调查由1980–1990年代的广泛档案研究编纂而成。作者将调查的全部40,000份原始编码表进行了数字化，构建了1714年至1789年间3,172个教区中发生的6,000次叛乱的综合数据库。对于每个事件，他们记录了日期、地点、动机、规模和参与者特征（第9–10页）。

额外数据包括：(i) 来自Ridolfi /rɪˈdɒlfi/（2019）的教区级十年平均小麦价格，用于控制地方经济条件；(ii) 来自1793年人口普查（法国首次人口普查）的人口数据；(iii) 来自卡西尼 /kæˈsiːni/ 1756–90年出版的地图的道路连通性；(iv) 教区是否为行政中心；(v) 盐税制度类型；(vi) 作者为替代机制分析构建的信件邮政系统和宪兵旅的历史GIS（第12页）。
分析单位是教区-十年。实证策略中使用的关键行政区划是
个，中位半径15公里，每个包含39个教区）（第11–12页）。

en: The paper employs a staggered difference-in-differences (DiD) design. The treatment is binary and non-absorbing: parishes can gain and lose horse-post relays over time. To account for heterogeneous and dynamic treatment effects — a well-known problem in staggered DiD settings — the authors use the estimator developed by de Chaisemartin /də ʃɛzmarˈtɛ̃/ and D'Haultfœuille /dotfœˈij/ (2024), which compares switchers (parishes that change treatment status) to not-yet-switchers with the same initial treatment status. This approach avoids the “contamination” of standard two-way fixed effects estimators that can arise when treatment effects are heterogeneous across time and units (pp. 12–13).
The regression equation (Equation 1, p. 12) is:
rebellions_{pda} = α_p + λ_d + Σ δ_ℓ × 1[horse_post_{pd}=1] × 1[d=ℓ] + prices_{pd} + γ_{da} + ε_{pda}

where p indexes parishes, d indexes decades, and a indexes administrative divisions. α_p denotes parish fixed effects; λ_d, decade fixed effects; prices_{pd}, average wheat prices; and γ_{da}, decade-by-administrative division fixed effects (alternatively at the subdélégation or canton level). Standard errors are clustered at the parish level.

The preferred specification uses two leads and three lags in the event-study framework, based on de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfœuille’s rule of thumb that at least half of ever-treated parishes should contribute to the estimation of each coefficient (p. 13).
Key identifying assumptions:

(1) Parallel trends: Absent the introduction of a relay, treated and untreated parishes within the same administrative division would have followed parallel trends in rebellion. The event-study plot (Figure 7, p. 16) shows no evidence of differential pre-trends, supporting this assumption.
(2) No anticipation of treatment: Parishes did not increase rebellion in anticipation of receiving a relay.

(3) Initial conditions: Past treatments before the starting period do not affect outcomes during the analysis period. The authors test this by excluding the 1710 decade, with results essentially unchanged (p. 16, Table 3, column 2).

(4) Exogeneity of relay placement conditional on fixed effects: The crucial identifying strategy exploits the fact that while the placement of major relay nodes in administrative centers may be endogenous to population dynamics, the specific location of relays between these nodes was primarily determined by the physiological need to space relays 10–15 km apart, reflecting horse physiology (Minetti 2003). The preferred specification uses within-canton variation (decade-by-canton fixed effects) to compare parishes within the same small administrative unit over time. This is a demanding specification: it reduces the effective sample from 35,287 parishes to 6,834 and from 282,296 to 54,672 parish-decades (p. 15, Table 2, columns 5–6). The authors demonstrate with a detailed case study of the Auxerre /oˈsɛʁ/ – Dijon /diˈʒɔ̃/ postal route in Burgundy /ˈbɜːrɡəndi/ that relay placement between regional centers was driven by distances along pre-existing roads rather than by population or economic characteristics (pp. 13–14, Figure 6).

Robustness checks include: varying periodization, holding the sample of switchers constant, using only switchers-in, population weights, decade-by-rural-status fixed effects, restricting to moderate/strong rebellions, distinguishing new relays from reconfigurations, using an indicator outcome, testing for archival reporting biases, and alternative clustering and grid-based fixed effects (pp. 16–18, Table 3, and Appendices B–C).
rebellions_{pda} = α_p + λ_d + Σ δ_ℓ × 1[horse_post_{pd}=1] × 1[d=ℓ] + prices_{pd} + γ_{da} + ε_{pda}
zh: 本文采用**交错双重差分（DiD）**设计。处理变量是二元且非吸收性的：教区可以随时间获得和失去驿站。为了处理交错DiD设计中众所周知的异质性和动态处理效应问题，作者使用了de Chaisemartin /də ʃɛzmarˈtɛ̃/ 和 D'Haultfœuille /dotfœˈij/（2024）开发的估计量，该估计量将切换者（改变处理状态的教区）与具有相同初始处理状态的尚未切换者进行比较。这种方法避免了当处理效应在时间和单位间存在异质性时标准双向固定效应估计量可能出现的“污染”（第12–13页）。
回归方程（方程1，第12页）为：
其中p索引教区，d索引十年，a索引行政区划。α_p表示教区固定效应；λ_d，十年固定效应；prices_{pd}，平均小麦价格；γ_{da}，十年-行政区划交互固定效应（在subdélégation或canton层面）。标准误在教区层面聚类。
首选规格在事件研究框架中使用两个前导期和三个滞后期，基于de Chaisemartin和D'Haultfœuille的经验法则，即至少一半曾被处理的教区应贡献于每个系数的估计（第13页）。
关键识别假设：
(1) 平行趋势： 在没有引入驿站的情况下，同一行政区划内处理组和未处理组的教区在叛乱方面将遵循平行趋势。事件研究图（图7，第16页）未显示差异性前趋势的证据，支持这一假设。
(2) 无处理预期： 教区不会因预期到将获得驿站而增加叛乱。
(3) 初始条件： 分析期开始前的过去处理不影响分析期间的结果。作者通过排除1710年代来检验这一点，结果基本不变（第16页，表3，第2列）。

(4) 条件于固定效应的驿站设置外生性： 关键的识别策略利用了以下事实：虽然行政中心主要驿站节点的设置可能对人口动态内生，但节点之间驿站的具体位置主要由每10–15公里设置驿站的生理需要决定，这反映了马匹生理学（Minetti 2003）。首选规格使用canton内变异（十年-canton交互固定效应）来比较同一小型行政单位内的教区随时间的变化。这是一个要求严格的规格：它将有效样本从35,287个教区减少到6,834个，从282,296个教区-十年减少到54,672个（第15页，表2，第5–6列）。作者通过勃艮第 /ˈbɜːrɡəndi/ 的Auxerre /oˈsɛʁ/ – Dijon /diˈʒɔ̃/ 邮路的详细案例研究证明，区域中心之间的驿站设置是由沿已有道路的距离而非人口或经济特征驱动的（第13–14页，图6）。
稳健性检验包括：改变分期、固定切换者样本、仅使用进入处理的切换者、人口权重、十年-农村状态交互固定效应、限于中强叛乱、区分新建驿站与重新配置、使用指示变量结果、检验档案记录偏差，以及替代聚类和网格固定效应（第16–18页，表3，以及附录B–C）。

en: The paper makes several distinct contributions to political science and economic history:

1. A conceptual contribution to state formation theory. The paper’s most important theoretical contribution is the argument that the process of state-building must be analytically distinguished from the outcome of state strength. The paper directly challenges the implicit assumption in much of the state capacity literature (e.g., Fearon and Laitin 2003; Huntington 1968) that greater state capacity straightforwardly reduces disorder. Instead, it demonstrates that in the transitional phase of building capacity, the state can be both powerful enough to intrude into local life and too weak or illegitimate to deter resistance — a paradox that generates contestation (pp. 2–3, 25). This contribution connects the paper to Garfias /ɡɑːrˈfiːəs/ and Sellars’ /ˈsɛlərz/ (2022) finding that state-building can backfire when it threatens local elite authority, and to Pruett’s /pruːˈɛt/ (2024) work on draft evasion in colonial West Africa — but Albertus and Gay extend this insight to a canonical European case and to a much broader range of social groups beyond elites alone (pp. 3, 25). The paper’s finding that parishes near new relays experienced fewer rebellions while the host parishes saw more — the “gathering effect” — also offers a novel micro-level perspective on Tilly’s (1986) theory of evolving repertoires of contention, suggesting that new state infrastructure created new focal points for collective action (p. 21).

2. Original data at unprecedented granularity. The construction of a parish-level panel of 35,000 parishes across eight decades, linking an original historical GIS of the horse-post relay network with the most comprehensive database of eighteenth-century French rebellions, represents a major data contribution. No prior study had analyzed pre-Revolutionary France at the parish level (p. 7).

3. Credible causal identification. The staggered DiD design using the de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfœuille (2024) estimator — combined with the physiological constraint on relay spacing, within-canton variation, and extensive robustness checks — provides a credible claim to causal identification. The design is well-suited to the empirical context because the horse-post relay network was built atop existing transit routes and served as a state-monopolized communications network, which allows the authors to separate the effect of state communication infrastructure from that of transportation infrastructure and from citizen-accessible information networks (pp. 1–2, 23–24).

4. Implications for the French Revolution. The exploratory finding that parishes that received relays and experienced an uptick in rebellion were more likely to later host political societies during the Revolution suggests that grievances generated by state-building did not dissipate but rather accumulated and resurfaced when subsequent shocks — such as the 1788 drought or the return of radicalized soldiers from the American Revolutionary War — created opportunities for mobilization (p. 25).
zh: 本文对政治学和经济史做出了几个独特的贡献：

1. 对国家形成理论的概念贡献。 本文最重要的理论贡献是论证国家建设的过程必须与国家实力的结果在分析上加以区分。本文直接挑战了国家能力文献（如Fearon and Laitin 2003; Huntington 1968）中许多隐含的假设，即更大的国家能力直接减少混乱。相反，它证明在建设能力的过渡阶段，国家可能既有足够的权力侵入地方生活，又太弱或缺乏合法性而无法威慑抵抗——这一悖论产生了争议（第2–3、25页）。这一贡献将本文与Garfias /ɡɑːrˈfiːəs/ 和 Sellars /ˈsɛlərz/（2022）关于国家建设在威胁地方精英权威时可能适得其反的发现，以及Pruett /pruːˈɛt/（2024）关于殖民西非逃避征兵的研究联系起来——但Albertus和Gay将这一洞见扩展到一个经典的欧洲案例，并扩展到远超精英之外的更广泛社会群体（第3、25页）。本文发现新驿站附近的教区叛乱减少而驿站所在教区叛乱增加——“聚集效应”——也为蒂利（1986）关于不断演变的抗争剧目的理论提供了新颖的微观视角，表明新的国家基础设施创造了集体行动的新焦点（第21页）。
2. 前所未有粒度的原始数据。 构建了一个跨越八个十年的35,000个教区的教区级面板，将驿站网络的原始历史GIS与最全面的十八世纪法国叛乱数据库相连接，这代表了重大的数据贡献。此前没有任何研究在教区层面分析过大革命前的法国（第7页）。
3. 可信的因果识别。 使用
设计——结合驿站间距的生理约束、canton内变异和广泛的稳健性检验——提供了可信的因果识别声明。该设计非常适合实证背景，因为驿站网络建在已有交通路线之上并作为国家垄断的通信网络运行，这使作者能够将国家通信基础设施的效果与交通基础设施的效果以及公民可使用的信息网络区分开来（第1–2、23–24页）。
4. 对法国大革命的启示。 探索性发现表明，获得驿站并经历叛乱增加的教区更有可能在大革命期间设有政治社团，这表明国家建设产生的不满并未消散，而是积累起来，当后续冲击——如1788年干旱或从美国独立战争归来的激进化士兵——创造了动员机会时重新浮现（第25页）。
de Chaisemartin和D'Haultfœuille（2024）估计量的交错DiD

en: Professional scholars would likely identify the following limitations and suggest corresponding improvements:

1. The black box of “material consequences.” While the paper argues convincingly that the link between relays and rebellion operates through the material consequences of state penetration rather than through information diffusion, the specific causal chain from relay establishment to individual-level grievance formation to collective action remains somewhat underspecified. The evidence for the mechanisms is primarily correlational — showing that rebellions were directed at state agents — rather than tracing the micro-level process. A scholar might suggest using the detailed motive coding in Nicolas’s survey to construct a more granular typology of grievance sequences and to trace how specific state actions (e.g., a particular tax enforcement campaign documented in archival records) were channeled through the relay network.

2. Measurement of rebellion and selection bias. The authors are commendably attentive to archival reporting biases, but a fundamental concern remains: Nicolas’s survey necessarily records only rebellions that were documented by contemporary authorities. If the introduction of a relay increased the state’s capacity to detect and record rebellions that would have occurred anyway, the estimated effect would be inflated. The authors argue that monitoring rebellions was not part of the horse post’s surveillance function and that the maréchaussée and judiciary — the main recorders of rebellion — followed different spatial logics. However, this argument is indirect. One could imagine that the improved communication enabled by the relay network allowed local subdélégués to report events more quickly to intendants, whose correspondence survives in departmental archives. The authors’ test of restricting to rebellions recorded only by national-level authorities partially addresses this, but a more direct test would examine whether the survival rate of departmental archival series correlates with relay presence.

3. The causal chain from relays to state agents. The paper’s core argument is that relays facilitated the activities of state agents (tax collectors, military recruiters, judicial officials), whose actions then provoked rebellion. But the paper does not directly measure the intensity of state-agent activity. A stronger design would document, for example, the frequency of tax enforcement actions or military recruitment drives at the parish level and show that these increased following the establishment of a relay. The Corvisier /kɔʁviˈzje/ (1964) data on military recruitment rates, which the authors use for heterogeneity analysis, could be developed further for this purpose.

4. External validity. The paper studies one country in one period. While France is a canonical case of state formation, the specific institutional features of the horse-post system — a state monopoly on rapid communication built atop existing roads — may not generalize to other cases where communication and transportation infrastructure were built simultaneously (as in colonial Africa or nineteenth-century America) or where communication networks were not state-monopolized (as with telegraphs in many countries). The authors acknowledge this as a research design advantage (the separation of communication from transportation effects), but it also limits generalizability.

5. The non-absorbing treatment and relay retirement. About 40% of ever-treated parishes lost their relay at some point. The authors treat relay retirement as an exit from treatment, but the social consequences of relay establishment — alliances with postmasters, disrupted local hierarchies, accumulated grievances — may not dissipate immediately upon relay removal. The authors do test for this by comparing results using only switchers-in (column 4 of Table 3), but a more developed theory of treatment persistence and decay would strengthen the analysis.

6. Temporal aggregation at the decade level. The use of ten-year periods as the unit of temporal analysis is dictated by data availability (the Liste des postes was digitized at decadal intervals), but it introduces considerable temporal imprecision. A rebellion in 1731 might be attributed to a relay established in 1721 or to one established in 1731, depending on the coding rules. The authors acknowledge this and test alternative timing specifications, but the fundamental limitation remains.
zh: 1. “物质后果”的黑箱。 虽然本文令人信服地论证了驿站与叛乱之间的联系通过国家渗透的物质后果而非通过信息传播运作，但从驿站建立到个体层面不满形成再到集体行动的具体因果链仍然有些不够明确。机制的证据主要是相关性的——显示叛乱针对的是国家代理人——而非追踪微观层面的过程。学者可能建议使用Nicolas调查中详细的动机编码来构建更细粒度的不满序列类型学，并追踪特定国家行动（如档案记录中记载的特定税收执法运动）如何通过驿站网络被传导。

2. 叛乱的测量与选择偏差。 作者对档案记录偏差的关注值得称赞，但一个根本性的担忧仍然存在：Nicolas的调查必然只记录了被当时权威记录的叛乱。如果驿站的引入增加了国家检测和记录本来就会发生的叛乱的能力，那么估计效果将被放大。作者论证监测叛乱不是驿站的监视功能的一部分，而且宪兵和司法——叛乱的主要记录者——遵循不同的空间逻辑。然而，这一论证是间接的。可以想象，驿站网络提供的改进通信使地方subdélégués能够更快地向省督报告事件，而省督的通信保存在省档案馆中。作者限于仅由国家级权威记录的叛乱的检验部分解决了这一问题，但更直接的检验将考察省档案系列的存留率是否与驿站存在相关。
3. 从驿站到国家代理人的因果链。 本文的核心论点是驿站促进了国家代理人（税收官员、军事征兵官、司法官员）的活动，其行动随后引发了叛乱。但本文并未直接测量国家代理人活动的强度。更强的设计将记录例如教区层面税收执法行动或军事征兵活动的频率，并表明这些在驿站建立后增加了。作者用于异质性分析的Corvisier /kɔʁviˈzje/（1964）军事征兵率数据可以进一步开发用于此目的。
4. 外部有效性。 本文研究的是一个国家的一个时期。虽然法国是国家形成的经典案例，但驿站系统的特定制度特征——建在已有道路之上的国家快速通信垄断——可能不能推广到通信和交通基础设施同时建设的其他案例（如殖民非洲或十九世纪美国），或通信网络不是国家垄断的案例（如许多国家的电报）。作者承认这是研究设计的优势（通信效果与交通效果的分离），但它也限制了可推广性。
5. 非吸收性处理与驿站撤销。 约40%曾被处理的教区在某一时刻失去了驿站。作者将驿站撤销视为退出处理，但驿站建立的社会后果——与驿站长的联盟、被打乱的地方等级、积累的不满——可能不会在驿站撤除后立即消散。作者确实通过仅使用进入处理的切换者来检验这一点（表3第4列），但一个更发展的处理持续性和衰减理论将加强分析。
6. 十年级别的时间聚合。 以十年期作为时间分析单位是由数据可用性决定的（《驿站总表》以十年间隔数字化），但它引入了相当大的时间不精确性。1731年的一次叛乱可能被归因于1721年建立的驿站或1731年建立的驿站，取决于编码规则。作者承认这一点并测试了替代时间规格，但根本性局限仍然存在。

en: Professional scholars would likely propose the following extensions:

Extension 1: From rebellion to revolution. The paper’s exploratory finding that parishes exposed to horse-post relays were more likely to host political societies during the Revolution opens a rich research agenda. A natural extension would systematically investigate whether the spatial pattern of grievance accumulation documented in this paper predicted the geography of revolutionary mobilization in 1789 — including participation in the cahiers de doléances /kaˈjeɪ də dɒleˈɑ̃s/, the storming of chateaux during the Grande Peur /ɡʁɑ̃d pœʁ/, and the formation of National Guard units. The authors’ 2SLS framework (mentioned in Appendix D, p. 25) could be developed into a full paper examining the long-run consequences of state-building for revolutionary outcomes.

Extension 2: Comparative state-building backlash. The paper’s finding could be tested in other contexts of early modern state-building. England’s /ˈɪŋɡlənd/ expansion of county administration under the Tudors /ˈtjuːdərz/, Prussia’s /ˈprʌʃə/ imposition of Kantonsystem /kanˈtoːnzɪsteːm/ military recruitment, the Habsburg /ˈhæpsbɜːrɡ/ expansion of administrative reach into Hungary /ˈhʌŋɡəri/ and Bohemia /boʊˈhiːmiə/, and the Ottoman /ˈɒtəmən/ timar system all provide comparable cases where state infrastructure expansion may have generated popular backlash. The key question is whether the mechanisms identified in France — tax enforcement, conscription, judicial enforcement, and crowding out of private interests — operate similarly in different institutional contexts.

Extension 3: State-building and the development of protest repertoires. The “gathering effect” documented in the paper suggests that new state infrastructure created new focal points for collective action. This connects to Tilly’s (1986) theory of evolving repertoires of contention. A Tilly-inspired extension would trace how the spatial reorganization of protest around horse-post relays contributed to the shift from “parochial, patronized, and particular” forms of contention to “cosmopolitan, autonomous, and modular” ones — a transformation that Tilly argues was central to the emergence of modern social movements.

Extension 4: Winners and losers of state-building. The paper identifies postmasters as winners and horse renters, off-road innkeepers, and commoners facing conscription as losers. A more systematic analysis of the distributional consequences of relay introduction — using the taille and capitation records mentioned by Tonnaire /tɔnˈnɛːʁ/ (2005) — could examine whether state-building generated localized inequality and whether this inequality mediated the rebellion response. This would connect the paper to the literature on fiscal capacity and distributive conflict (Levi 1989; Besley and Persson 2011) that we have discussed in our readings.

Extension 5: The role of legitimacy. The paper’s argument implies that rebellion arose partly because the state lacked sufficient legitimacy to justify its expanding presence. But legitimacy is not directly measured or theorized. An extension could draw on the cahiers de doléances to examine whether parishes exposed to horse-post relays expressed different types of grievances — particularly whether they framed their complaints in terms of justice, fairness, or traditional rights — and whether these framings predicted revolutionary political alignment (royalist vs. republican).

Extension 6: Information diffusion vs. material consequences — a sharper test. While the authors convincingly rule out the letter-post system as a confounding channel for information diffusion, the broader question of whether state communication infrastructure can also facilitate citizen coordination remains open. In contexts where communication networks are not state-monopolized (e.g., the telegraph in the nineteenth century, mobile phones in the twenty-first), the relative importance of material-consequences vs. information-diffusion mechanisms may differ. A comparative study across different types of communication infrastructure — state-monopolized (horse post), quasi-public (telegraph), and open-access (mobile phones) — could shed light on the conditions under which each mechanism dominates.

Extension 7: Connecting to the “paradox of the weak state.” The paper’s finding resonates with what might be called the “Goldilocks problem” of state-building: states that are too weak cannot provoke resistance (because they are not present enough to impose costs), while states that are very strong can suppress it. It is states in the intermediate range — present enough to impose burdens but not strong enough to deter challenges — that face the greatest risk of rebellion. This connects to Pruett (2024) on colonial West Africa and to the broader literature on state-building and civil war (Fearon and Laitin 2003). A formal model of this non-monotonic relationship between state capacity and rebellion could provide a theoretical foundation for the empirical patterns documented in the paper.

This paper does not propose a formal theoretical model. The argument is substantive and historically grounded rather than model-driven. The authors draw on established theories of state formation (Tilly 1990; Mann 1984; Elias [1939] 1994; Migdal 1988) and connect them to the specific institutional context of eighteenth-century France through detailed historical narrative and causal inference. The research design is entirely empirical, centered on a staggered difference-in-differences framework with careful attention to identification assumptions and robustness (pp. 12–18).
zh: 拓展一：从叛乱到革命。 本文关于暴露于驿站的教区更有可能在大革命期间设有政治社团的探索性发现开辟了丰富的研究议程。自然的拓展将系统地调查本文记录的不满积累的空间模式是否预测了1789年革命动员的地理分布——包括参与陈情书 /kaˈjeɪ də dɒleˈɑ̃s/ 的撰写、大恐慌 /ɡʁɑ̃d pœʁ/ 期间的城堡风暴，以及国民卫队单位的组建。作者的2SLS框架（在附录D中提及，第25页）可以发展为一篇完整论文，考察国家建设对革命结果的长期后果。

拓展二：比较性国家建设反弹。 本文的发现可以在其他近代早期国家建设的背景下检验。都铎 /ˈtjuːdərz/ 王朝下英格兰 /ˈɪŋɡlənd/ 郡行政的扩展、普鲁士 /ˈprʌʃə/ 推行的Kantonsystem /kanˈtoːnzɪsteːm/ 军事征兵制、哈布斯堡 /ˈhæpsbɜːrɡ/ 将行政触角延伸到匈牙利 /ˈhʌŋɡəri/ 和波希米亚 /boʊˈhiːmiə/，以及奥斯曼 /ˈɒtəmən/ 蒂马尔制度，都提供了国家基础设施扩张可能引发民众反弹的可比案例。关键问题是法国识别出的机制——税收执法、征兵、司法执法和排挤私人利益——是否在不同的制度背景下类似地运作。
拓展三：国家建设与抗议剧目的发展。 本文记录的“聚集效应”表明新的国家基础设施创造了集体行动的新焦点。这与蒂利（1986）关于不断演变的抗争剧目的理论相联系。一个受蒂利启发的拓展将追踪围绕驿站的抗议空间重组如何促成了从“地方性的、庇护性的和特殊的”抗争形式向“世界性的、自主的和模块化的”抗争形式的转变——蒂利认为这一转变对现代社会运动的出现至关重要。
拓展四：国家建设的赢家和输家。 本文将驿站长识别为赢家，将马匹租赁商、不在邮路上的旅店主和面临征兵的平民识别为输家。利用Tonnaire /tɔnˈnɛːʁ/（2005）提到的taille和人头税记录，对驿站引入的分配后果进行更系统的分析，可以考察国家建设是否产生了地方化的不平等，以及这种不平等是否调节了叛乱反应。这将把本文与我们在阅读中讨论过的财政能力和分配冲突文献（Levi 1989;
Besley and Persson 2011）联系起来。
拓展五：合法性的角色。 本文的论点暗示叛乱部分因国家缺乏足够的合法性来证明其扩展存在的正当性而产生。但合法性并未被直接测量或理论化。一个拓展可以利用陈情书来考察暴露于驿站的教区是否表达了不同类型的不满——特别是它们是否以正义、公平或传统权利的框架来表述投诉——以及这些框架是否预测了革命时期的政治取向（保王派vs.共和派）。

拓展六：信息传播vs.物质后果——更锐利的检验。 虽然作者令人信服地排除了信件邮政系统作为信息传播的混淆渠道，但关于国家通信基础设施是否也能促进公民协调的更广泛问题仍然开放。在通信网络不是国家垄断的背景下（如十九世纪的电报、二十一世纪的手机），物质后果vs.信息传播机制的相对重要性可能不同。跨越不同类型通信基础设施——国家垄断（驿站）、准公共（电报）和开放获取（手机）——的比较研究可以阐明每种机制在何种条件下占主导地位。

拓展七：连接“弱国家悖论”。 本文的发现与可以称为国家建设的“金发姑娘问题”相呼应：太弱的国家无法引发抵抗（因为它们的存在不够强到施加成本），而非常强大的国家可以压制它。处于中间范围的国家——存在感足以施加负担但不够强大到威慑挑战——面临最大的叛乱风险。这与Pruett（2024）关于殖民西非的研究以及更广泛的国家建设与内战文献（Fearon and Laitin 2003）相联系。对国家能力与叛乱之间这种非单调关系的形式模型可以为本文记录的实证模式提供理论基础。
本文未提出形式理论模型。论点是实质性的和以历史为基础的，而非模型驱动的。作者借鉴了既有的国家形成理论（Tilly 1990; Mann 1984; Elias [1939] 1994; Migdal 1988），并通过详细的历史叙述和因果推断将它们与十八世纪法国的特定制度背景联系起来。研究设计完全是实证性的，以交错双重差分框架为中心，对识别假设和稳健性给予了仔细的关注（第12–18页）。
